This is the path the dark-factory AGENT uses. Gap 2 (session.cancel/close) HALT-AND-SURFACE: no durable session-lifecycle cancel/close intent or reconcile seam exists; real impl is new substrate design (durable lifecycle intent + workflow/engine terminal reconcile), not additive — shipping no-op would paper it. title: Firegrid engine-native primitives escape-hatch track (streamWait/streamWaitAny/recycleAwareActivity/reducer/signal) — CONTINGENCY ONLY, opens on trigger
description:
Per docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_ENGINE_NATIVE_PRIMITIVES_ESCAPE_HATCH.md (main commit 76b329314).

STATUS: CONTINGENCY ONLY — NOT yet active. Pre-staged anchor so dispatch is fast if any trigger fires.

DOES NOT REPLACE the current P0 narrow fix (tf-gyxc: engine recycle is suspend/resume, not cancel/fail). Phase 1 continues as planned; this track only opens conditionally.

TRIGGER CONDITIONS (any one fires P1 escalation):
(1) PR #469 recycle fix (tf-gyxc) reveals analogous failures in OTHER DurableDeferred sites
(2) Wave-2B surfaces the same leak in runtime body (now landed; check post-fan-in)
(3) Phase 2 channel work hits another composition failure
(4) simulate:perf shows the composed durable Activity fold is correct but TOO EXPENSIVE

EXTENSION SURFACE (per SDD §Extension Surface Shape):
FiregridWorkflowEngineExtensions service tag with:
- streamWait — highest leverage; replaces the composition PR #469 broke
- streamWaitAny — body-plan wait_for_any first-winner semantics over multiple channels
- recycleAwareActivity — contract extension for in-flight Activities spanning engine generations
- reducer — engine-native merged event body (Phase-1 Lane 1's shape, but engine-owned state)
- signal — (see SDD)

DESIGN PRINCIPLE: Firegrid-specific extension service, NOT upstream WorkflowEngine API changes. Portable workflows stay portable. Firegrid body-plan workflows opt in explicitly.

COORDINATOR WATCH: monitor the 4 trigger conditions during Phase 1 fan-in + Phase 2 dispatch. Open this track as P1 if any fire.

CROSS-REFS:
- tf-gyxc: the current narrow fix this contingency complements
- tf-ovzr PR #469: the RED finding that surfaced the question
- SDD_FIREGRID_ONE_SUBSTRATE_WORKFLOW_ENGINE.md / SDD_FIREGRID_AGENT_BODY_PLAN.md / SDD_FIREGRID_AGGRESSIVE_ONE_SUBSTRATE_SWAPOVER.md

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text:
SDD CORRECTION 2026-05-20 (main commit 9e1e2d1d0): streamWait predicate contract refined.

CORRECTED — streamWait accepts:
- effect/Predicate Predicate<Row> (NOT a required schema-defined matcher DSL)
- Constraint: DETERMINISTIC PURITY — same as workflow bodies/Activity executes:
  * no wall-clock
  * no random
  * no I/O
  * no process-local refs
  * no mutable closure state

OPTIMIZATION (additive, not required):
- Firegrid-owned StreamPredicates factories can return normal Predicate<Row> functions WITH engine-readable symbol metadata for indexed scans
- Bare Effect predicates remain valid; fall back to row-by-row evaluation when no symbol metadata is present

This replaces the previous over-strong schema-only predicate wording. Contingency track still NOT active — bead description carried forward for fast dispatch if triggers fire.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text:
PERF-GATING TRIGGER ADDED 2026-05-20 (main commit 4d7951e42).

NEW TRIGGER (5th condition for opening contingency track):

If Firegrid non-LLM workflow substrate latency becomes a material ratio of LLM network RTT, engine-native primitives move from contingency to active perf work.

Thresholds:
- P1: p95 local substrate latency >= 10% of p95 LLM RTT
- P0: >= 20% or a single local primitive >= 10%

MITIGATION LADDER (in priority order):
1. streamWait / streamWaitAny (engine-native composition)
2. engine-attached observers
3. predicate optimization hints (StreamPredicates factories with symbol metadata for indexed scans)
4. durable lifecycle write coalescing
5. reducer checkpoint policy
6. stable firegrid.engine.* spans

Coordinator watch surface expands to 5 triggers:
(1) tf-gyxc fix reveals analogous failures in other DurableDeferred sites
(2) Wave-2B same-leak class in runtime body post-Phase-1 Lane 1
(3) Phase 2 channel work composition failure
(4) simulate:perf shows composed durable Activity fold too expensive
(5) **NEW: latency ratio crosses 10%/20% thresholds vs LLM RTT**

Trigger #5 fires after Phase 1 fan-in once we can measure end-to-end perf with the merged-body + WaitForWorkflow shapes.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text:
POTENTIAL CONTINGENCY TRIGGER #1 OBSERVED 2026-05-20 — Lane 4 on tf-vnc8 reported: 'literal Stream.merge body pinned workflow suspension in this engine; implemented equivalent durable state-machine driver that folds already-materialized input/output events and parks only on input deferred when neither side has materialized.'

This is a composition leak: Stream.merge inside the workflow body interacts with engine suspension semantics in a way that requires userland workaround. Lane 4's pivot to an explicit state-machine driver SATISFIES SDD §Phase 1 Lane 1's 'alternate shapes must prove equivalent replay behavior' (new scoped-replay test passes), so Phase 1 Lane 1 is NOT blocked.

BUT — this is exactly the class of finding the contingency SDD's 'reducer' primitive would address engine-natively. Catalog as observed-but-not-blocking. If a SECOND analogous composition leak surfaces (e.g., in Phase 2 channel work or simulate:perf), trigger #1 + #3 + the latency-ratio trigger #5 may compound and the engine-native track should open.

Coordinator monitoring; not opening the contingency track yet on this single observation.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text: ADJACENT POST-PHASE-1 FOLLOW-ON FILED 2026-05-20: tf-ho99 (Reconciler-as-workflows). The structural collapse depends on streamWait direction (Path A full collapse vs Path C hybrid). If contingency triggers fire and engine-native primitives go active, tf-ho99 benefits directly. Adds a 6th watch surface to monitor — when Phase 1 fan-in completes, tf-ho99 becomes the next P0 dispatch decision point alongside Phase 2 fan-out.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text:
TRIGGER #6 ADDED 2026-05-20 (main commit 9539b58f3, docs: add polling loop engine trigger).

NEW TRIGGER (6th condition for opening contingency track):

If a host-sdk durable-table polling loop is on a Phase-2/P0 path and can be replaced by event-driven engine dispatch, open the engine-native primitive track. First candidate explicitly named in SDD: packages/host-sdk/src/host/control-request-reconciler.ts.

SDD additions:
- §Why This Exists: pre-engine-era polling loops named as motivating operational case (idle CPU + durable-storage RTTs + multi-second control-plane latency floor)
- Mitigation ladder: replace Clock.sleep(...).pipe(Effect.forever) polling loops with engine-attached streamWait observers that re-attach on recycle
- Decision trigger #6: polling-loop-on-Phase-2-or-P0-path replaceable by event-driven dispatch

Coordinator watch surface now expands to 6 triggers:
(1) tf-gyxc fix reveals analogous failures in other DurableDeferred sites
(2) Wave-2B same-leak class in runtime body
(3) Phase 2 channel work composition failure
(4) simulate:perf composed-fold latency
(5) Latency ratio >= 10%/20% of LLM RTT
(6) **Host-sdk durable-table polling loop on Phase-2/P0 path** (first candidate: control-request-reconciler.ts → tf-ho99)

tf-ho99 is now SDD-elevated as the first-named candidate for trigger #6.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text:
TRIGGER #7 ADDED 2026-05-20 (main commit 6f7e398ee, docs: add runtime context registry collapse target).

NEW TRIGGER #7: if the post-cutover plan chooses to collapse runtime-context-engine-registry from one-scoped-engine-per-context to one-host-scoped-engine-with-many-executions, activate the signal primitive (engine.signal(executionId, channel, payload)) AND the one-engine-many-executions design call.

SDD additions:
- New §Post-Cutover Structural Collapse Targets section names BOTH control-request-reconciler.ts AND runtime-context-engine-registry.ts as standalone-value post-cutover targets.

Coordinator watch surface now 7 triggers:
(1) tf-gyxc analogous failures in other DurableDeferred sites
(2) Wave-2B same-leak class in runtime body
(3) Phase 2 channel composition failure
(4) simulate:perf composed-fold latency
(5) Latency ratio >= 10%/20% of LLM RTT
(6) Host-sdk durable-table polling loop on Phase-2/P0 path (first candidate: control-request-reconciler.ts → tf-ho99)
(7) **runtime-context-engine-registry collapse to one-engine-many-executions** (first candidate: runtime-context-engine-registry.ts → tf-ws2x)

Both tf-ho99 + tf-ws2x are SDD-named candidates under §Post-Cutover Structural Collapse Targets. Trigger #7 explicitly depends on signal primitive activation (so trigger #7 is downstream of contingency track opening for streamWait + signal).

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text:
TRIGGER #1 STATUS UPDATE 2026-05-20: Lane 3's investigation of Lane 4's Stream.merge pinned-suspension finding lands VERDICT (b) — NOT engine-recycle/tf-gyxc class. Per Lane 3's analysis (Gurdas-agreed): 'Stream.merge pinning is not tf-gyxc/recycle related. It's a workflow-body authoring constraint caused by composing multi-fiber stream pulling with an engine model that wants one coherent suspension point per execution step.'

CONSEQUENCES:
- Trigger #1 (analogous DurableDeferred composition leak) does NOT fire on this observation
- streamWaitAny primitive (already in the contingency SDD's candidate list) gets CONCRETE MOTIVATION: exposes one suspension point while coordinating multiple sources internally — exactly the gap Lane 4's pivot worked around
- Lane 3 recommends documenting 'no concurrent stream combinators inside workflow bodies' near runtime-context-workflow-core.ts or in the SDD, citing Lane 4's peek/await state machine as the working pattern

The workflow-body authoring constraint is now an explicit rule (worth elevating into the contingency SDD framing if not already there). streamWaitAny moves from 'candidate primitive' to 'directly-motivated by an observed authoring constraint'.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text: VERDICT-DOC ARCHIVED 2026-05-20: docs/research/workflow-body-single-suspension-rule.md (Gurdas-authored) documents the workflow-body single-suspension-point authoring constraint surfaced by Lane 4's Stream.merge pivot + confirmed by Lane 3's investigation. Reference this doc when assessing trigger #1 status or motivating streamWaitAny.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text:
ENGINE-NATIVE TRACK CLOSURE-PATH ANCHOR 2026-05-20: Gurdas filed tf-wunq (P2) capturing Phase-1 Lane 2's known-issue Option-A bridge exposure: WaitForWorkflow Activity-internal timeout restarts across engine recycle. Classified bounded liveness/SLA degradation, not correctness. Closure path: 'migrate wait_for to engine-native streamWaitAny/streamWait with persisted absolute deadline + restart/bounce sim proving deadline is not re-armed from scratch.'

This is the SECOND concrete motivation for streamWait/streamWaitAny primitives (alongside Lane 3's tf-h17r workflow-body-single-suspension finding). When the engine-native track activates, tf-wunq becomes a closure-path consumer. Strengthens the case for trigger #1 + #4 firing post-Phase-2 perf measurement.

tf-wunq is actionable post-tf-xw0w; coordinator watches alongside the 7 trigger conditions.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text:
CANONICAL CHANNEL PATTERN ANCHORED 2026-05-20 (main commit d00e12cf7).

Channels = generic typed facades over durable operator streams:
- Channel<LinearWebhook> / IngressChannel<LinearWebhook>
- backed by DurableTable CollectionFacade<Row>.rows() → ProjectionStream<Row>
- agent sees wait_for('linear.webhook', { match, timeoutMs })

Webhook-as-channel generalization: ANY inbound source (webhooks, CDC streams, event tables, agent-emitted events, lifecycle signals) gets uniform wait_for(channel, match, timeoutMs) surface. Diversity lives in Layer compositions at host startup.

This is the reference shape for any future engine-native streamWait/streamWaitAny primitive — they should consume / produce channels in this Tag+Layer shape, not Registry+Service shape.

title: investigation: §6 planner stalls at ToolSearch — never issues Firegrid MCP ToolUse (discovery->invocation gap)
close_reason: completed: sidecar/tf-0ro-s6-toolsearch-stall merged as #405. NOTE: its 'terminal ACP-architecture limit' conclusion is DOWNGRADED — see docs/handoffs/COORDINATOR_HANDOFF_s6_dark_factory.md §0a (our own ACP codec double-advertisement is the prime suspect; not ACP). Task done; conclusion superseded.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: HARD-HALT + precise finding (NOT a Firegrid substrate gap; no code shipped — papering would be wrong). Run 2026-05-19T12-51-52 investigated. SOURCE-VERIFIED: (1) Firegrid MCP surface correct — register_toolkit tool_count:8, McpServer.initialize+tools/list present (planner DID enumerate; not the codex non-enum G-MCP-2 case), known-good MCP client lists all 8 per spec; (2) ToolSearch is purely @agentclientprotocol/claude-agent-acp@0.36.1 internal deferred-tool meta-tool — ZERO occurrences in packages/+apps/; Firegrid exposes 8 plain tools, agent runtime interposes ToolSearch; (3) planner wrote full correct §6 plan (22 agent_message_chunk) + called ToolSearch but ZERO McpServer.tools/call, sawTurnComplete:false no agentError/Terminated, prompt() never resolved; substrate ran hard (wait_for.upsert_active ×1330); (4) tools/list_changed ×16 = per-addTool rpc-client span noise (8 tools × 2 layer builds), Effect-AI debounces delivery (McpServer.ts:176-185) — NOT the cause. INFERENCE (labelled): stall = post-ToolSearch model turn not completing; convergent with tf-7dq/#395 directly-reproduced root cause = external Anthropic usage limit (regains 2026-06-01 UTC); claude-agent-acp ToolSearch adds a model round-trip so the quota-exhausted run visibly dies right there; previously invisible because @agentclientprotocol/sdk RequestError dropped error.message (the exact tf-ds2/#403 fix). Cannot isolate whether ToolSearch independently blocks with quota available (all 3 runs quota-exhausted). Owed next: re-run §6 after quota regains WITH #403 merged (agentError will state the reason); ONLY if still stalling with quota, scope a claude-agent-acp deferred-tool/ToolSearch-disable config (agent-runtime argv/env, NOT substrate) — no unverified flag shipped. §6 durability independently PROVEN (#397). Artifact docs/research/tf-0ro-s6-toolsearch-stall.FINDING.md. Coordinator/architect holds gate; NO self-merge.

title: wait_for_any is an in-memory race — not durable across host restart
description: From tf-phk7 audit. runtime-agent-tool-execution.ts:163-177 implements wait_for_any with Effect.raceAll + Effect.timeoutTo, an in-memory race with no durable suspension primitive. A wait_for_any in flight is lost on host restart even today (inside the workflow). Design a durable race over the owning workflow's observation primitives. Latent gap, independent of the de-workflowing cutover.
close_reason: Completed: durable wait_for_any race over owning WaitForWorkflow merged in PR #660.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: Implemented; draft PR #660. wait_for_any was in-memory Effect.raceAll (runtime-agent-tool-execution.ts) -> lost on restart. Fix: generalize the EXISTING WaitForWorkflow (NO new Workflow.make) to race primary source + additionalSources inside its one journaled Activity; route wait_for_any through it; descriptors resolve to serializable CallerFact sources (same as single wait_for). Single wait_for outcome unchanged (winnerIndex only for multi-source) so existing callers/tests untouched. Reuses RuntimeObservationSource + owning workflow primitive — NO source-model or RuntimeContext-ownership change (within STOP bounds). Tests: multi-source winnerIndex + durability proof (completed race replays from durable state across engine reconstruction). typecheck/eslint/dead/semgrep green; affected suites pass. semgrep baseline line for the unchanged WaitForWorkflow.make bumped 46->63 (additive payload edits, no new workflow). Preflight test red = known effect-durable-streams StaleEpoch concurrent-turbo flake (all suites pass per-package/isolated).

title: Implement engine restart recovery sweep for non-clock suspended executions
description: S1 input-suspend-crash-recovery empirically confirmed that table-wait suspensions are not re-armed on DurableStreamsWorkflowEngine reconstruction while clock wakeups are recovered. Implement the smallest engine-runtime recovery sweep symmetric with recoverPendingClockWakeups: on engine construction/reconstruction, discover suspended active executions without finalResult and re-drive them at least once so workflow-owned table input present before/during restart is processed. Acceptance: adapt/re-run the S1 input-suspend-crash-recovery sim so Probe A and Probe B auto-recover after reconstruction without explicit test redrive; Probe C clock auto-recovery remains green; no new deferred mailbox/input-intent bridge. Default to recovery sweep; STOP and report exact boundary only if sweep cannot make both probes green and kernel-owned write+arm is required.
close_reason: STOP/falsified: generic engine suspended-recovery sweep is unsound because suspension rows do not distinguish table-waits from DurableDeferred/interrupt waits; superseded by tf-c9r9 kernel-owned write+arm.

comments.author: s1-sim
comments.text: Originating sim shipped as PR #663 (CLEAN, all CI green): packages/tiny-firegrid/src/simulations/input-suspend-crash-recovery. Probe A/B confirm the table-wait gap; Probe C is the clock-recovery contrast that motivates the kernel-owned write+arm path (tf-c9r9) over this engine-sweep approach.

description: Implement packages/tiny-firegrid/src/simulations/coordination-topology/ as the MVP from docs/research/agentic-patterns-experimental-design.md. comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: REWORK DISPOSITION (Gurdas, 2026-05-21, handoff sent to L4 directly): PR #584 as shaped is NOT acceptable as the experiment — deterministicAgentSource / node -e stdio-jsonl agents do not use an LLM; they only prove channel plumbing. Source of truth: docs/research/agent-orchestration-vs-choreography-experiment.md (landed 9645d18b9). Rework: rebase on main + rebuild around the doc examples — TS client/session API, REAL FRONTIER-MODEL participants, arms A/B/C = single agent / developer-authored orchestration / choreography. Deterministic agents may remain ONLY as isolated CI/regression smoke, never the headline experiment artifact. #584 HELD OUT OF MERGE GATE until the rework lands. (Confirms the no-stub-on-choreography rule — a scripted stub forces the orchestration anti-pattern / false-green.)

comments.author: gary
comments.text: HARD RESET ON ACCEPTANCE (Gurdas, 2026-05-21): The current PR shape is still too contrived. tiny-firegrid already generates OTEL traces and durable-row artifacts; do NOT build a parallel bespoke evidence harness that scrapes participant text/tool-use as the primary experiment product. Required shape: (1) define a non-contrived software-factory/task scenario that real frontier agents can attempt; (2) run it through the public client/session/channel surface; (3) let tiny-firegrid generate trace.jsonl + simulate:show + simulate:perf + durable channel rows; (4) analyze those artifacts after the run into a concise findings doc; (5) fixture mode is only CI smoke, not the experiment. Acceptance is a runnable live simulation plus trace-backed post-run analysis. If PR #588 cannot be simplified into that shape, mark it as a discarded spike and produce a replacement plan; do not merge it as the canonical experiment harness.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: EVIDENCE-SHAPE refinement (Gurdas, #588 review): tiny-firegrid is already an OTEL + durable-row artifact generator; the bespoke "gather evidence" harness in driver.ts (scraping participant text/tool-use into markers) reinvents it. Evidence should PRIMARILY be native artifacts — trace spans/attributes (coordination.* already annotated), durable channel rows, simulate:show/perf. Reduce bespoke evidence collection to a THIN ASSERTION over those; keep the per-arm min-evidence/no-false-GREEN guard but realize it over native artifacts, not a parallel scraper. If the simplification is nontrivial, mark #588 as a useful SPIKE, NOT the canonical experiment-harness shape (canonical harness = follow-on). Reinforces the experiment-doc non-goal "do not hide coordination complexity in harness-only helpers."

comments.author: gary
comments.text: DECISION (Gurdas, 2026-05-21): No more scaffold-only merge for #588. We have already proven Firegrid can host sessions/channels/traces many times. If this rework nets out to another substrate scaffold, BLOCK the experiment until the necessary abstractions land instead of merging placeholder architecture. Acceptable outcomes: (A) a real experiment artifact where frontier agents make meaningful coordination choices and post-run analysis uses tiny-firegrid native artifacts; or (B) close/replace #588 as a learning spike and add explicit blockers on missing abstractions (likely tf-9x11 host-plane router, tf-1r3h sync/async closure, and/or public experiment ergonomics). Do not merge #588 as canonical if the only result is another channel/session scaffold.

comments.author: gary
comments.text: PR #588 review posted: DISCARD AS CANONICAL HARNESS / replacement plan required. Reason: still computes verdict in bespoke driver-side evidence harness over scraped participant text/tool-use instead of tiny-firegrid native artifacts; prompts still prescribe exact JSON, so GREEN measures tool-call echo more than coordination. Required replacement: driver launches/waits only; trace.jsonl + simulate:show/perf + durable rows are source of truth; post-run analysis doc/script derives findings; fixture remains CI smoke; block explicitly on missing abstractions rather than merging another scaffold.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: REPLACEMENT PLAN (post-#588 discard, Gurdas): #588 closed as a learning spike (computed GREEN via bespoke evidence-scraping + exact-JSON echoing — not a real artifact). The CANONICAL coordination experiment is a non-contrived live sim over the public surface whose evidence is purely trace.jsonl + simulate:show/perf + durable rows + a post-run analysis doc (fixture = CI-smoke only). It is BLOCKED ON MISSING ABSTRACTIONS: tf-9x11 (host-plane channel router — clean channel access), tf-1r3h (durable sync/async production semantics — reliable coordination), and tf-r5si (public experiment ergonomics — thin assertion over native artifacts). Implement the canonical harness once those land; do not re-attempt scaffold.

Goal is to lock SDD_FIREGRID_DURABLE_CHANNELS_SYNC_ASYNC.md into production semantics transactionally. Implementation closure means: enumerate public callable/request-row-then-reflect operations; for each, add source evidence + focused tests; implement missing barriers where the result gates the next operation; explicitly classify async send+wait_for paths as already durable mailbox semantics; update SDD wording where it still implies createOrLoad itself must block globally. No half-ships outside tiny-firegrid: any deferred operation must be classified with a blocking follow-up bead before PR merge.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: ESCALATED P1→P0, AUDIT→PRODUCTION SEMANTIC CLOSURE (Gurdas, 2026-05-21; revised acceptance sent to L3 directly). NOT docs-only. LOCKED SEMANTICS: (1) Async mailbox = send durable-append + wait_for/wait_for_any durable-observation — largely implemented, must be backed by tests/evidence. (2) Sync handshake = result/reflection barrier belongs in the callable/DEPENDENT op WHEN result gates the next op; session.prompt/session.start/firegrid.sessions.prompt already wait via awaitSessionDependentContext; createOrLoad does NOT need to globally block if only identity/handle requested. (3) Fix SDD_FIREGRID_DURABLE_CHANNELS_SYNC_ASYNC.md wording if it still implies createOrLoad must be globally blocking. (4) No half-ships outside tiny-firegrid: any deferred op needs a BLOCKING follow-up bead before merge (transactional cutover rule). My earlier docs-only/no-production-changes dispatch framing is WITHDRAWN.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: #587 REVIEW LENS (Gurdas, must pass before undraft; transactional, no half-ship outside tiny-firegrid): (1) UNKNOWN-CONTEXT HANG — firegrid.prompt waits on awaitSessionDependentContext(decoded.contextId) before appendHostPrompt; a typo/unknown context id can wait forever and never hit the HostPrompt context-existence error path. Fix: bounded error path so invalid ids error (not hang), OR name future-context semantics explicitly in finding/SDD + a bounded test. title: INV-2: tiny-firegrid sim — WaitForWorkflow as nested workflow execution (validates SDD Steps 2+3)
description:
Build a sim that defines a custom WaitForWorkflow via Workflow.make whose body is:
  DurableDeferred.raceAll([
    Activity(Stream.runHead(source.filter(trigger))),
    DurableClock.sleep(timeoutMs),
  ])
Wire it so the sim's agent-tool wait_for handler dispatches engine.execute(WaitForWorkflow, ...) instead of WaitFor.match. Drive with claude-agent-acp + a prompt that calls wait_for multiple times against a CallerFact stream seeded with matching + non-matching rows.

Acceptance:
(a) Agent's wait_for tool calls succeed via the nested workflow execution path
(b) trace.jsonl shows DurableDeferred.raceAll firing + Activity result records being written
(c) NO firegrid.durable_tools.wait_router.complete_match spans emitted (the substrate that emits them isn't wired into this sim's host)
(d) FINDING confirms the engine handles this shape natively with no API changes

Estimated work: ~day. NO blockers.

title: Delete public session.whenReady (substrate-materialization leak); push bounded readiness into the ops
description:
tf-1r3h production-closed the durable sync/async axis: dependent writes (firegrid.prompt, firegrid.sessions.prompt, session.prompt, session.start) now own a SHARED BOUNDED reflected-context barrier (awaitSessionDependentContext, bounded by config.contextReflectionTimeoutMs, default 30s; on timeout does authoritative control.contexts.get and fails ContextNotFound if absent). This makes session.whenReady REDUNDANT as a pre-write ceremony — classified Class D in docs/research/tf-1r3h-durable-sync-async-closure-audit.md.

DEFERRED (transactional cutover rule, NOT a half-ship): whenReady is NOT deleted in the tf-1r3h PR (#587) because (a) it remains a valid EXPLICIT, intentionally-unbounded readiness/projection wait that a caller may opt into, and (b) removing public surface is an API-compat decision separate from the semantic closure. This bead is the BLOCKING follow-up that owns that cleanup so the deferral is tracked, not dropped.

SCOPE: decide deprecate-vs-remove for session.whenReady (RuntimeContextHandle.whenReady / FiregridSessionHandle.whenReady), update README/docs that still show whenReady-before-prompt ceremony, and (if removed) migrate/justify any remaining callers. Note: whenReady is currently unbounded for a typo'd id (intentional opt-in wait) — if KEPT, decide whether it should also gain a bounded/error variant.

EVIDENCE: docs/research/tf-1r3h-durable-sync-async-closure-audit.md (Class D row + Closure Plan §4); packages/client-sdk/src/firegrid.ts whenReady at ~:928, shared barrier awaitSessionDependentContext at ~:772.

title: TFIND-002: Session creation still required host identity
description:
Area: client-sdk / host boundary.

Status: resolved (#332, 2d474c70d).

Triage: cat-1 (rubric anchor — client-only frontend hits host-identity wall immediately).

Firegrid.sessions.createOrLoad required CurrentHostSession because it created a host-bound RuntimeContext row through insertLocalRuntimeContext. That made remote-client-shaped tests impossible through the public session creation API: the client had to be composed with host identity, even though a production client should not be in the same Effect environment as a host.

CLUSTER RESOLUTION (Gurdas 2026-05-18, #332 merged — consolidated client/host boundary, Option 1):
- sessions.createOrLoad writes a RuntimeContextRequestRow (full public runtime intent) — no CurrentHostSession on the client path.
- Folded TFIND-001/003/038/039 into the same transaction.
- session.start writes a durable RuntimeStartRequestRow; host-side control-request-reconciler binds contexts + claims/runs starts.
- RuntimeContextRequestRow carries argv/env/agent protocol/MCP — arbitrary public runtime intent expressible client-side.
- CLI/factory migrated to host-owned synchronous startRuntime same-transaction (no compat wait-helper).
- Abandon semantics = terminal/no-revival/10min, idempotent claim via insertOrGet, duplicate-start suppression — guarded by 5 named deterministic failure-mode tests + 6th determinism test; CI-confirmed green; no cast on reconciler/claim/dedup/completion path.
- TFIND-004/008 unblocked: durable-intent/reconciler seam makes separate-process client/host e2e expressible.
close_reason: Resolved by #332 — consolidated client/host boundary, Option 1 (client writes durable intent; host reconciler claims starts).

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: DRAFT PR #625. Fail-loud startup validation for unwritable --otel-file (after #608). observability: checkFiregridOtelFileWritable(filePath) returns typed discriminated result {writable}|{unwritable,reason}, mirroring JsonlFileSpanExporter ctor (mkdir parent recursive + access W_OK on dir + existing file) as an idempotent up-front check. CLI acp: on unwritable, fail with existing typed FiregridCliUsageError 'cannot write OTEL trace file <path>: <reason>' BEFORE any ACP frame (stdout stays pure). Design note: used a typed result, NOT a new Data.TaggedError, because the effect-quality ratchet treats dataTaggedErrorDeclarationCount as lower-is-better + refuses to ratchet up; the typed result keeps it typed/matchable and the CLI surfaces its existing typed error. Evidence: pnpm preflight all gates pass; CLI smoke on unwritable path = exit 1, 0 stdout bytes, stderr ENOTDIR mkdir error; observability tests 6 pass (+2). Scope kept to observability error handling + CLI wiring per dispatch.

comments.text: factory-vision §7.4 delegation sim built (self-contained, auto-discovered by #385 registry — NO registry edit; rebased onto latest main a1988e953). Full CI gate green (typecheck 17/17, lint, lint:deps boundary CLEAN, lint:dead, lint:dup 50/50, test 16/16). Run 2026-05-19T11-54-29-997Z. SOURCE-VERIFIED: handoff-prompt not delivered to delegated child. INFERENCE (owed verification): root is (a) child workflow not forwarding workflow-authored ingress / (b) codec not flushing to child stdin / (c) ingress not appended — needs child-context ingress->codec instrumentation to pin. Not a reach-past (public surfaces only); falsifiable sim (goes green when child receives its prompt). Artifact docs/research/tf-39b-delegation-parent-child.FINDING.md. title: TFIND-036: Firegrid MCP toolkit lacked a read-only runtime-state query tool
description:
Area: MCP / tools.

Status: RE-TRIAGED cat-3 → toy redirect (2026-05-18; was QUEUED-FOR-ARCHITECT/SDD #335).

Triage: cat-3 (rubric CANONICAL — 'agent reads its own runtime exit code' has NO coherent non-Firegrid consumer; test-fixture awkwardness. NO production code shipped — caught at SDD stage).

The triage question: would a real consumer outside Firegrid need an agent to read its own runtime-run exit code? No — the agent is inside the current run (it has not exited), and runtime-run exit/signal is host-plane forensic data with no coherent in-run agent use. The sleep durationMs:0 'workaround' was the toy reaching for a plausible-sounding capability to exercise the MCP bridge — test-fixture awkwardness wearing capability clothes.

Toy fix: exercise the MCP bridge with an existing tool, or use wait_for{RuntimeRun} where the config genuinely needs run presence / terminal state.

PROCESS NOTE: TFIND-036 reached a 4th-revision SDD before this triage was applied — the process gap that motivated the rubric. It was caught at SDD stage; no production code shipped.
close_reason: Closed cat-3 — no coherent non-Firegrid consumer; toy redirect.

title: P0: live ACP edge must answer PermissionRequest instead of deadlocking tool calls
description: Live Zed/claude-acp tool calls can deadlock because the ACP stdio edge drops RuntimeAgentOutput PermissionRequest observations. The codec waits on a PermissionDecision Deferred, so no response means the agent blocks and the host turn times out. Promote the validated stopgap: the edge must answer PermissionRequest through the existing host.permissions.respond route so ACP is always owed a response. Keep scope tight: unblock live testing, add regression coverage, and leave forward-to-Zed/native permission UI + CLI policy as follow-up unless trivially separable. Evidence: docs/investigations/2026-05-21-acp-stdio-edge-permission-deadlock.md.

title: Agent runtime conformance harness: fixture replay + live-agent canaries
description: Build a layered conformance program for the actual agent runtime/sandbox/provider surface. Goal: broaden confidence beyond unit tests without putting live model calls on every PR. Scope: (1) conformance matrix for ACP, stdio-jsonl, MCP/provider attachment, sandbox process lifecycle, permission round trips, restart/replay; (2) tiny-firegrid fixture replay layer for recorded ACP/stdio/MCP transcripts; (3) protocol-conformant fake agents/providers for adversarial behaviors; (4) a small live-agent canary lane using acpx/Codex/Claude/ACP harnesses for drift detection; (5) trace-based acceptance on durable rows, side separation, permission correlation, provider-secret absence, and no polling hot loops. Non-goal: do not make acpx the primary CI harness; use it for capture/live canaries only. First dispatch should be a narrow spike, not a broad framework rewrite.

title: Spike: tiny-firegrid agent runtime fixture replay harness
description: First slice of the agent runtime conformance epic. Add a tiny-firegrid-native fixture replay harness rather than live-model CI. Deliverables: (1) feature/spec or concise research doc defining the conformance matrix and acceptance shape; (2) one ACP transcript replay fixture and one stdio-jsonl transcript replay fixture, recorded or synthetic but protocol-shaped; (3) one fake MCP/provider fixture that exercises permission/tool-call flow; (4) one adversarial restart/replay or disconnect case; (5) simulate:show/perf artifacts demonstrating trace-based assertions: durable rows exist, host/driver/runtime sides stay separated, permission request/response correlates, provider secrets are absent, and no high-volume polling loop appears. Use acpx only as optional capture/live-canary input, not as the CI foundation. Non-goals: no broad eval framework, no live Claude/Codex calls in standard verify, no new orchestration SDK surface.
close_reason: Completed: PR #576 merged (agent runtime fixture replay harness).

comments.author: gary
comments.text:
Refinement from architecture review: this spike must produce reusable artifacts, not just a green sim. Required output should include:

1. A spec-first matrix update, preferably new ACIDs in features/firegrid/firegrid-runtime-agent-event-pipeline.feature.yaml under VALIDATION or a new SOURCE_CONFORMANCE component. The matrix rows should explicitly cover the currently-supported axes found in source: SandboxProvider {local-process, effect-ai}, runtime session mode {raw, codec}, codec/transport {ACP, stdio-jsonl}, and live-agent canary inputs {codex-acp, claude-agent-acp/claude-code-acp where credentials allow}.
2. A checked-in matrix artifact in docs/research or the sim FINDING: rows = provider/runtime/transport/fault class, columns = expected source boundary, codec boundary, durable evidence, trace artifact, and pass/fail/unsupported verdict. Unsupported is acceptable when explicit, e.g. effect-ai has no openBytePipe and therefore cannot run ACP byte-pipe tests.
3. Fixture corpus artifacts: deterministic ACP transcript, deterministic stdio-jsonl transcript, fake MCP/provider permission/tool round trip, and at least one adversarial restart/disconnect transcript. These should live under tiny-firegrid with the sim, not as one-off helper code hidden in the driver.
4. Fuzz/property classes to exercise byte/source behavior: chunk splitting, frame coalescing, stdout/stderr interleaving, malformed/incomplete JSON, early process exit, stdin close/EPIPE, slow/hung response, duplicate tool ids, permission request without response, response after disconnect, provider env/secret leakage, and restart/replay around a committed output row.
5. Generated artifacts from tiny-firegrid: simulate:show markdown tree, simulate:perf summary, trace.jsonl path, and a short FINDING that names which observed failures imply changes to packages/runtime/src/agent-event-pipeline/sources/README.md.

Design intent: use the matrix to decide the sources/README contract. sources/ should own live byte/process/resource acquisition only; if fuzz evidence shows a concern requires protocol correlation, durable row authority, or app-facing config, that concern belongs in codecs/authorities/host-config rather than sources/. This keeps the README evidence-driven and avoids designing the source pipeline by intuition.

title: Use agent source fuzz matrix to redesign runtime sources README

Non-goals: no implementation refactor in this bead, no broad directory restructure, no new public API. This is the documentation/design closeout after the fuzz matrix, not the fuzz harness itself.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: TERMINAL FINDING — HARD-HALT, source-verified, not papered. No available ACP runtime nor the ACP protocol exposes forced tool-choice/must-call for the §6 planner. Source-verified: (a) claude-agent-sdk@0.3.143 sdk.d.ts has NO tool_choice/forcedTool/mustUseTool — only allowed/disallowed/aliases/permissionMode/modes/models/effort; Agent SDK hides the model loop, does NOT forward raw Anthropic tool_choice; claude-agent-acp passes _meta.claudeCode.options straight into these (nothing to set). (a-codex) codex-acp@0.14.0 = 2KB launcher spawning opaque native binary, no JS forced-tool knob; tf-9q4 empirically tools/call=0. (b) Messages-API tool_choice exists but NO ACP runtime forwards/exposes it. (c) claude-code-acp/claude-acp same SDK family + OAuth (tf-ahk); none expose it. ACP @agentclientprotocol/sdk: PromptRequest has NO tool-forcing field; SetSessionConfigOption only exposes modes/models/effort (buildConfigOptions), never tool-choice; no standardized forced-tool primitive. (d) hardcoded ACP shim REJECTED as papering (not agent-driven; substrate-exec already proven #397/#417); a genuine forced-invocation requires a NON-ACP custom Messages-API agent = architecture/PO decision, out of lane. No 540s run spent: constrained behavior decision-grade (tf-9q4/tf-u6l) + lever analysis source-verified from SDK/protocol code; a re-run cannot manufacture an absent capability. CONCLUSION: substrate COMPLETE & proven (#397/#417/#411/#388/#405); live agent-driven §6 blocked SOLELY on agent-side forced tool-invocation no ACP runtime/protocol exposes. Recommend: accept demo story (substrate complete; residual precisely pinned) OR PO-gated non-ACP planner OR upstream feature requests (Gurdas's external-publish call; repro in #415). Artifact docs/research/tf-549-s6-forced-toolchoice.FINDING.md. No Firegrid code changed. Coordinator/architect holds gate; NO self-merge.

description: Production cutover of the host-kernel/controller-owned write+arm primitive validated as reference shape by tf-c9r9 (kernel-owned-write-arm sim) and motivated by the S1 crash-recovery gap (PR #663). Owns both halves as one recoverable command: (1) persist the workflow-owned input/output row, (2) arm/resume the owning RuntimeContext workflow execution, (3) persist command state for restart recovery, (4) serialize with lifecycle ops. Pick the S1 axis-2 variant (atomic-append-and-arm / recovery-sweep / kernel-owned-writer). Per SDD_FIREGRID_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_INPUT_WRITE_ARM_MIGRATION.md (input first); output fact needs an SDD extension. comments.text: CONSTRAINT LENS 2026-05-22: tf-5cn1 is a C5 bridge-exception candidate, not target architecture. Allowed scope: modify the existing parked-body runtime-context path enough to own write+arm during migration, with tf-vrz6/tf-w6qj as deletion targets. It must not introduce a permanent generic kernel primitive, generic engine suspended-workflow sweep, new mailbox/request-claim-completion family, ordering authority, or output-side cutover without an SDD extension. The implementation should be input-first and bounded to existing RuntimeContextWorkflowRuntimeLive authority unless it hits the STOP condition: no durable identity tying command -> context/execution -> row key, or no safe serialization with lifecycle. title: TFIND-030: Snapshot agent output events were typed as records, not protocol unions
description:
Area: client-sdk / projections.

Status: resolved (#329, a7c76c268).

Triage: cat-1 (real client-sdk consumer wants the typed AgentOutputEvent union from snapshot without casts).

The durable-streams-backed test needed a local textDeltas projection that checked agentOutputs[].event as Record<string, unknown> instead of the typed AgentOutputEvent union. The runtime output rows were decoded correctly at runtime, but the client snapshot projection type lost the discriminated event shape.

Resolution: protocol-owned AgentOutputEvent union (byte-mirroring runtime via the same @effect/ai Prompt/Response primitives; @effect/ai added to @firegrid/protocol — Gurdas-blessed, lint:deps clean). Protocol envelope/observation decode parses event against the union; session.snapshot().agentOutputs[].event is the typed union, re-exported from client-sdk. Q2 STRICT: non-conforming event → Option.none.
close_reason: Resolved by #329 — protocol-owned AgentOutputEvent union; SSOT consolidation tracked as TFIND-035.

title: Tiny-firegrid spike: edge transport adapter over channels for ACP/Zed viability
description:
Research/experiment spike: prove whether Firegrid can expose an ACP/Zed-style edge transport as an adapter over channel verbs, without leaking host-sdk/runtime substrate into the adapter and without changing Durable Streams as the first substrate backend.

Context: Zed external agents are ACP-speaking processes launched by Zed over stdio. Firegrid should not become ACP internally; ACP should be one edge projection over the channel surface. The spike should test the viability of a transport adapter shape:

  Zed/test ACP client -> ACP over stdio -> Firegrid edge adapter -> client-sdk/protocol channel verbs -> host/runtime substrate.

Scope / deliverables:
1. Add a tiny-firegrid simulation, tentatively packages/tiny-firegrid/src/simulations/channel-edge-transport-adapter-viability, that drives an ACP-compatible edge adapter process or scoped in-process stdio harness with @agentclientprotocol/sdk. Use a local test ACP client; do not require Zed itself.
2. The adapter must use only protocol/client channel contracts and public client-sdk surface for Firegrid interaction. It must NOT import @firegrid/host-sdk host modules, @firegrid/runtime internals, DurableTable facades, or workflow engine handles. Host composition remains on the simulation host side.
3. Exercise the minimal ACP lifecycle mapping: session/new or equivalent -> channel.call(host.sessions.createOrLoad); prompt -> channel egress/public client prompt; output streaming -> channel.wait_for/session output projected back to ACP sessionUpdate; permission request/response if available; cancel/close if current public surface supports it.
4. Produce trace artifacts with pnpm --filter @firegrid/tiny-firegrid simulate:show and simulate:perf. The FINDING must include the message-flow tree and name any hot polling, missing barrier, duplicate surface, or direct-substrate leak.
5. Produce a short docs/research finding answering: Outcome A viable as generic edge transport adapter; Outcome B viable but missing named channel contract(s); Outcome C not viable because some required ACP behavior cannot be represented without a new channel primitive.
6. Explicitly separate edge transport pluggability from substrate backend pluggability. The spike proves ACP/stdio as an edge projection over channels, not a replacement for Durable Streams HTTP/SSE as the durable substrate backend.

Acceptance:
- A reviewer can see, from imports and trace, that the ACP edge adapter is channel/client-only.
- A reviewer can see which channel verbs cover which ACP behaviors.
- The finding recommends whether to create a first-class firegrid acp-agent / zed-agent adapter package/CLI entrypoint, or what missing channel contracts block it.

Non-goals:
- No direct Zed integration or registry package in this slice.
- No REST/gRPC implementation yet; mention them only as future projections if the ACP adapter validates the pattern.
- No new substrate backend abstraction.
- No broad transport framework; keep this one falsifiable tiny-firegrid spike.
close_reason: DONE: PR #586 merged (LGTM). ACP host-edge transport viability spike — config-shaped (FiregridHostPlaneEdgeTopology + acpStdioEdge + FiregridHostPlaneEdgesLive), two-turn distinct-output assertion, TurnComplete (no process-exit hang), no cancel overclaim, public-verb boundary. Production cutover + README/Zed restore carried by tf-csr0.

Do not schedule ahead of baseline reset / quality-zero wave, factory vision execution, or agentic-patterns showcase experiment. comments.author: gnijor
comments.text:
README QUICKSTART RESTORE — tf-5n1z.1 is now the source-of-truth for restoring the removed ACP/Codex/Claude root-README quickstart (Gurdas, 2026-05-21).

BUG: `pnpm firegrid -- run --agent codex-acp --agent-protocol acp -- npx -y @zed-industries/codex-acp@0.14.0` HANGS after launch + prompt append. Root cause is semantic, not flaky: `run` is sync-run/process-exit semantics (create context -> append prompt -> startRuntime -> wait for runtime PROCESS EXIT). ACP/Zed agents are long-lived stdio/ACP sessions that emit TurnComplete WITHOUT process termination; the runtime workflow exits only on Terminated, not TurnComplete -> hang.

ACTION TAKEN: Gurdas removed the ACP/Codex/Claude root README quickstart commands. Per the transactional cutover rule (docs/cannon/architecture/transactional-cutover-rule.md), public README must NOT advertise a half-shipped edge-transport path.

RESTORE GATE: the quickstart may be restored ONLY once the edge transport adapter is proven AND shipped transactionally (Zed/test ACP client -> ACP over stdio -> Firegrid edge adapter -> client-sdk/protocol channel verbs -> host/runtime substrate). tf-5n1z.1 (the viability spike) owns this; the production cutover that follows it owns the actual README restore.

PRIORITY: bumped P2 -> P1 — public README/canonical demo now depends on it. Related to the conformance gap (ACP-bridged only; live-canary unsupported; native Codex JSONL not wired).

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: ARCH CLARIFICATION (Gurdas, 2026-05-21): direction is Firegrid-AS-ACP-AGENT (NOT Firegrid-as-client driving codex-acp). Firegrid speaks ACP over stdio to Zed/test clients. Mirror claude-agent-acp src/acp-agent.ts:3084-3094 runAcp, injectable: runFiregridAcp({input,output,firegridClient,options}) → ndJsonStream(input,output) → AgentSideConnection(client => new FiregridAcpAgent(client, firegridClient)); input/output default to process stdio, tests use in-memory streams. FiregridAcpAgent maps ACP Agent methods → PUBLIC client/protocol channel verbs ONLY — must NOT import host-sdk host modules, runtime internals, DurableTable facades, workflow handles, or kernel internals (ACP = edge projection). Prompt completion = ACP TURN semantics, not process exit (this is the hang fix). Spike acceptance: in-memory test ACP client drives a turn to TurnComplete with session staying alive + observable output; finding scopes the production cutover.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: REFINEMENT (Gurdas, 2026-05-21): the ACP edge is a CONFIGURABLE HOST-PLANE EDGE ADAPTER in host config/topology — NOT a standalone runFiregridAcp privileged entrypoint as the contract. runAcp (claude-agent-acp:3084-3094) is the IO-seam reference only (stdio→ndJsonStream→AgentSideConnection). Edge transport adapters belong at host-plane config/composition; ACP/Zed stdio is one edge projection over PUBLIC protocol/channel verbs. Adapter decodes inbound ACP Agent methods → channel operations and projects session output channels → ACP sessionUpdate. Composed at host plane (host-sdk topology/projection-edge role) but operates over public verbs only — no reach into host-sdk internal host modules / runtime / DurableTable / workflow / kernel. CLI = thin preset building host config with the ACP stdio edge, not the contract. DISTINCT from agentProtocol:acp child-agent codec (host→child); this is external-client/Zed→Firegrid-host edge.

comments.author: gary
comments.text: SDD CAPTURED (2026-05-21): docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_ONE_SUBSTRATE_PRIMITIVE.md now names the two transport axes explicitly. Host-plane edge transport = external client/tool host into Firegrid (Zed/ACP stdio, HTTP/SSE, REST/gRPC/JSON-RPC/MCP edges) and is composed through host topology/config. Agent runtime transport = Firegrid launching/talking to a child agent (local.jsonl agentProtocol acp/stdio-jsonl/raw). tf-5n1z.1 must implement the ACP/Zed path as a host-plane edge adapter over public protocol/channel verbs, not as a special runFiregridAcp entrypoint and not by confusing it with child-agent agentProtocol:acp.

title: Evaluate workflow-native kernel ownership + channel mailbox/signal primitive options
description:
Evaluate options before building tiny-firegrid prototypes for replacing imperative host workflow control with workflow-native kernel ownership and channel-backed durable mailbox/signal primitives.

Context: commands.ts, RuntimeContextWorkflowRuntime, RuntimeInputIntentDispatcherLive, and control-request-dispatcher currently rebuild two semantics that should belong to workflow/kernel primitives: (1) exclusive claims over context/workflow processing and (2) durable channel-to-workflow signaling/stream delivery. We need a decision matrix before dispatching prototype builds.

Questions to answer:
1. Exclusive claims over context/workflow processing: compare workflow executionId/idempotency, parent HostKernelWorkflow owning child RuntimeContextWorkflow executions, DurableQueue.worker ownership, current request-row claim/completion tables, and raw DurableTable claim rows. For each, explain crash/restart, cross-host ownership, duplicate start, interruption, child workflow lifecycle, and observability semantics.
2. Durable streams over channels to workflows: compare channel -> DurableQueue mailbox, channel -> DurableDeferred signal, channel -> future engine.signal, channel -> streamWait/declarative subscription, and current inputIntent row -> dispatcher -> deferred completion. Explain where the boundary sits, how transport/durable-stream specifics stay hidden, how either channel endpoint can be swapped, and what package owns each part.

Required source reads:
- packages/host-sdk/src/host/commands.ts
- packages/host-sdk/src/host/runtime-context-workflow-runtime.ts
- packages/runtime/src/control-plane/control-request-dispatcher.ts
- packages/runtime/src/workflow-engine/runtime-input-deferred.ts
- repos/effect/packages/workflow/README.md
- repos/effect/packages/workflow/src/DurableQueue.ts
- repos/effect/packages/workflow/src/WorkflowEngine.ts
- docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_ONE_SUBSTRATE_PRIMITIVE.md
- docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_DURABLE_CHANNELS_SYNC_ASYNC.md

Deliverable:
- docs/research/<bead-id>-workflow-kernel-channel-primitives-options.md
- Include at least one mermaid diagram for current vs target.
- Produce a ranked recommendation and 2-3 tiny-firegrid prototype beads with crisp acceptance criteria.
- Do not implement prototypes in this task.

Acceptance:
- Names the actual purpose of each current imperative component.
- Separates kernel ownership, channel contracts, channel Live bindings, durable mailbox/signal implementation, and edge transports.
- Decides which options are worth prototyping and which are dead ends.
- Calls out any Effect workflow limitations that make a pattern unsafe (e.g. naive Stream.runForEach inside workflow body).
close_reason: DONE: PR #579 merged d9b310c8. Workflow-kernel/channel primitive options doc: ranked recs — DurableDeferred (one-shot channel→workflow signals), parent HostKernelWorkflow (context lifecycle ownership), DurableQueue (only for true async mailbox). Gates the prototype builds.

Avoid forkDaemon/host-scope non-durable shortcuts and avoid replay-path duplicate prompts. comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: DRAFT PR #637. Implemented the durable scheduler. schedule_me now starts ScheduledPromptWorkflow fire-and-forget (execute(...,{discard:true}) returns executionId immediately) + returns {scheduled:true} at once; workflow durably sleeps to when then appends the prompt as a runtime input intent exactly once (insertOrGet idempotent on scheduleId). idempotencyKey=scheduled-prompt:<scheduleId>; handler registered on host-engine scope (toolCallWorkflowSupportLayer) so engine resumes after delay; replay/restart re-starts no-op + re-appends idempotently. NO forkDaemon/host-scope sleep. Table write isolated in agent-event-pipeline/authorities/scheduled-prompt-append.ts (avoids no-table-yield-in-workflow-body rule + folder cycle). Scoped to current input-intent seam, not Phase-0C cursor. EVIDENCE: schedule_me returns {scheduled:true} immediately for when:0 + far-future when(10min) [non-blocking regression]; runtime-codec-event-plane (real engine) exercises workflow registration/routing; preflight ALL gates green (new Workflow.make baselined as owned durable-timer). HONEST: immediate-return + registration proven; a clock-advancing fire-exactly-once-after-delay/replay test is the recommended additional gate before live Zed retest (fire-once rests on DurableClock deadline persistence + insertOrGet idempotency + host-scope registration).

title: whenReady absent-id existence floor: fail bounded for a provably-absent context, stay unbounded for existing-but-slow
description:
RESIDUAL CORRECTNESS QUESTION owned here (raised by OCA in the #587 review, re-flagged in the #593 / tf-2osu review). session.whenReady is an EXPLICIT, intentionally-unbounded readiness wait (kept as a public primitive by tf-2osu for read/observe + host-execution + forked-consumer paths the dependent-write barrier does not cover). The footgun: for a context id that is PROVABLY ABSENT (e.g. attach() to a typo'd session id — no context row AND no context-request row was ever written), whenReady waits FOREVER. The desired contract: stay unbounded for an existing-but-slow context (it WILL materialize), but fail bounded for one that can never materialize.

WHY NOT in tf-2osu/#593: that PR was deliberately narrowed (review feedback) to ONLY removing the redundant pre-prompt/start whenReady ceremony. Adding an existence floor + its tests would re-expand it. tf-1r3h/#587 already bounded the prompt/start dependent-write barrier (awaitSessionDependentContext) for absent ids; this bead closes the SAME footgun for the standalone whenReady primitive.

PROPOSED MECHANISM (validate before building): distinguish provably-absent from existing-but-slow via the control-plane request/context rows. If NEITHER control.contexts.get(id) NOR the context-request row (runtimeContextRequestId(id)) exists, the id is provably absent -> fail bounded (PreloadError/ContextNotFound). If a request row exists but the context has not materialized -> existing-but-slow -> keep waiting unbounded. Alternative/lighter: a generous bounded floor (timeout) only when no request row is observed. DECIDE which.

ACCEPTANCE: whenReady on a provably-absent id fails bounded (test, with TestClock or short bound); whenReady on an existing-but-slow context still completes when it materializes (test: subscribe, materialize late, completes); no change to the happy path. Update the whenReady API doc + SDD to state the floor.

EVIDENCE/REFS: packages/client-sdk/src/firegrid.ts whenReady (handle interface + impl) + waitUntilContextReady; awaitSessionDependentContext bounded barrier (tf-1r3h); docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_DURABLE_CHANNELS_SYNC_ASYNC.md; OCA #587 review; #593 review (REQUEST CHANGES, blocking issue 3).

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: SUBSUMED by tf-2osu's full whenReady deletion: striking whenReady from the public surface removes the absent-id hang entirely (no public whenReady = no unbounded absent-id wait); any internal readiness helper is bounded by construction. Captured in tf-2osu, not dropped.

title: acp-trace-health misreports completed codec tool calls as open
description:
Investigation reference: docs/investigations/2026-05-21-acp-parent-child-output-channel-gap.md
Trace artifact: .firegrid/acp-trace.jsonl

The ACP trace-health report showed tool-call balance as tools/call=5 handle=5 resolve=5 execute=10 result=0, open=5. Manual trace inspection showed the five tested tools completed on the ACP/MCP path: McpServer.tools/call spans completed, toolkit handling completed, and ACP tool_call_update status completed rows were present. The report is therefore treating this codec path as open even when the calls are complete.

Required fix: update the trace-health script/metric so completed ACP codec tool calls are counted as completed results. Distinguish real in-flight tool calls from completed responses and fast invalid-input/error completions.

Acceptance criteria:
- Running the report on the captured trace no longer reports the five completed tool calls as open.
- A fixture or targeted regression check covers this ACP codec completion path.
- Genuine open/in-flight tool calls still report as open.

STOP/EXIT instruction: if the fix cannot be contained to trace-health parsing/normalization and instead requires changing span taxonomy or tool-result instrumentation across ACP/MCP/runtime, exit the task and report the exact span contract gap that prevents a narrow tooling fix.
close_reason: Completed: trace-health ACP codec tool-call completion accounting fixed in PR #640.

title: TFIND-046: Client SDK exposes ControlPlane tag but not its stream-URL builder
description:
Area: client-sdk / durable tables.

Status: open (low priority — client-sdk ergonomic completeness; annotated in #341 MIGRATION_NOTES).

Triage: cat-1 (real consumer gap — a client-side live control-plane query, the Flamecast useDurableTable pattern, must reach into protocol for the stream URL; low severity but consumer-facing; fix = client-sdk re-export/fold. NOT toy-test-cleanness: a real non-Firegrid consumer hits the identical import).

Client SDK exposes FiregridRuntimeTables.ControlPlane (the durable table tag), but to instantiate its layer a consumer must supply the stream URL via runtimeControlPlaneStreamUrl, which is only exported from @firegrid/protocol/launch. So consumer-shaped code that builds a client-side live query over the control-plane table (the Flamecast useDurableTable(FiregridRuntimeTables.ControlPlane) + live-query pattern) must reach past the client SDK into a protocol URL helper.

Next action: re-export runtimeControlPlaneStreamUrl from @firegrid/client-sdk so consumer-shaped code does not import protocol URL helpers directly.
notes: Signoff recorded (Gurdas, 2026-05-18): decided narrow-B — expose only the namespace-scoped runtime control-plane URL helper through @firegrid/client-sdk. Do not expose host-owned stream builders from the client SDK. Implementation owed on PR #359; bead remains non-terminal/in_progress.
close_reason: Landed in #359; decision:narrow-client-control-plane-url-helper honored.

title: tf-bffo follow-up: relocate session-self + reach-past durable surfaces below runtime
description:
Transactional remainder of the tf-bffo channel-boundary cutover (PR #589). Two durable surfaces still live in host-sdk/src/host and must move below runtime so host-sdk owns NO durable-state wiring:

1. session-self checkpoint channel (packages/host-sdk/src/host/channels/session-self/index.ts, ~329 lines): exposes workflow-engine durable tables (handle.table.executions/activities/deferreds/clockWakeups query + rows) + control.runs.rows() as channels. Relocate the durable read/stream logic into @firegrid/runtime/channels (config-parameterized); host-sdk composes the Live Layer only. Mirror the pattern used for session-agent-output/host-control in #589.

2. RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents reach-past (the §9.2 tail): packages/host-sdk/src/host/agent-tool-host-live.ts captures `yield* RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents` (line ~559) + tiny-firegrid sims inv1-stream-zip-body/host.ts + phase0-wave-2b-stream-zip-restart-replay/host.ts read it via @firegrid/runtime/runtime-output. Route these through the existing SessionAgentOutputChannel. CARE: RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents.after(contextId, seq) is an after-SEQUENCE stream vs the channel's full ingress stream — the sims' zip/replay semantics need real rework (not a trivial swap). Once migrated, consider whether the @firegrid/runtime/runtime-output subpath can be removed entirely (making RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents fully kernel-internal).

Owner: tf-bffo lane (codex). Acceptance: host-sdk/src/host carries zero durable-state wiring; full preflight green for packages/*. This bead BLOCKS tf-bffo final close.
close_reason: COMPLETE. session-self durable channel logic relocated into @firegrid/runtime/channels (host-sdk SessionSelfChannelsLive composes-only); agent-tool-host-live approval wait routed through SessionAgentOutputChannel.forContext(ctx).binding.stream (validated by APPROVAL_CALL.3). Both landed in #589, full preflight green except the known pre-existing start-runtime flake. The 2 durable-replay sims (inv1-stream-zip-body + phase0-wave-2b) reach-past is parked in tf-tj58 (P2, live-validation gated) — NOT shipped unvalidated. tf-tj58 wired as a blocker of tf-bffo.

comments.author: gary

To avoid conflicts, start tf-77ab from the #589/tf-bffo branch after #589 merges, or keep it with the tf-bffo owner if continuing before merge. Do not start an independent off-main rewrite that duplicates #589's runtime/channel moves.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text:
PROGRESS + a validation blocker on the reach-past.

DONE + GREEN (pushed to #589): session-self durable channel logic relocated into @firegrid/runtime/channels (makeSessionSelfChannels, control table + structural checkpoint source); host-sdk SessionSelfChannelsLive is compose-only. Full preflight green except the known pre-existing start-runtime flake.

REACH-PAST — split by validatability:
- agent-tool-host-live.ts uses agentOutputEvents.forContext(contextId) (a whole-context wait) — TEST-COVERED (APPROVAL_CALL.3). Safely migratable to SessionAgentOutputChannel's per-context stream.
- inv1-stream-zip-body + phase0-wave-2b sims use events.initial(source)+events.after(source) inside Stream.unfoldEffect WITHIN Activity.make (durable per-sequence replay). These sims have ZERO automated test coverage (no .test.ts; env-gated runnable probes needing live durable-streams infra). Routing them through SessionAgentOutputChannel (full live ingress stream) is the durable-replay redesign Gurdas flagged as "real rework, not a swap" — and I CANNOT validate it here (no test; needs live infra). Per the no-CI-determinism-on-probes + don't-ship-unvalidated discipline, I will NOT blind-rewrite validation harnesses.

The reach-past's boundary VALUE (freeing the @firegrid/runtime/runtime-output subpath so RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents becomes fully kernel-internal) requires ALL THREE migrated + sim-run validation.

ASK: (a) want me to migrate the test-covered agent-tool-host-live read now (partial), then do the 2 sims as focused work WITH live sim-run validation; or (b) treat the full reach-past as focused follow-up (since its value needs all three + live validation)? title: Implement the host-plane channel router (typed router value + runtime dispatch interpreter)
description: Implement the router specced in docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_HOST_PLANE_CHANNEL_ROUTER.md (tf-rd3d/#590). Shape (post Effect-native review): router is a typed VALUE of routes with edge-only dispatch; @firegrid/protocol owns router TYPES + route descriptors (callable/egress/ingress); runtime owns the dispatch INTERPRETER + route implementations; route implementations are DESCRIPTORS so the router derives Layer composition; ParseError explicit on schema decode failure; dispatch observability span; host-sdk composes FiregridHostChannelRouterLive + edges; application/agent code never dispatches — only edges do. KEYSTONE: the ACP/MCP/CLI/HTTP edges dispatch through this, and host-control channel lives relocate onto it. GATED ON #590 (the SDD) RATIFYING — do not dispatch until the SDD lands; scope may still refine while #590 is in draft.

title: Live ACP tool-call hangs: per-context agent-output observer re-subscribes from output start ~2000x; tool result never returns; edge turn times out
description: Surfaced on live Zed claude-agent-acp run (trace .firegrid/acp-trace.jsonl, ~25k lines). claude enumerated + CALLED the Firegrid sleep tool (McpServer.tools/list ok), but the turn died with AcpStdioEdgeTurnOutputError reason=timeout 'timed out waiting for Firegrid agent output'. Trace signature: firegrid.runtime_output.per_context.agent_output.initial fired 1987 times (observer re-subscribing from output sequence 0 repeatedly = replay storm), workflow.output.completed/event.handle/activity.execute all ~2000, runtime-context.state...output.N.after replayed for N=0..8; McpServer.tools/call never closed (0 completed), agent_event_pipeline.acp.tool_result=0 -> the sleep tool result never propagated back to the agent, so the agent never completed the turn and the edge timed out. NOT agent-specific (codex is separately non-enumerating; this is the claude path that DOES call tools). NOT caused by tf-r1gz observability changes (exporter-only). Likely the agent-output observer reading 'initial' (from start) instead of incrementally-after-sequence on each workflow resume/event. Related: tf-9sx9 observer-leak removal, A4 output-stream correctness seam. Repro: firegrid acp --agent claude-acp --agent-protocol acp -- npx @agentclientprotocol/claude-agent-acp@0.36.1, prompt 'sleep 0ms using your Firegrid tool'.
close_reason: Completed: #612 merged

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: Second live turn (sleep durationMs=3000) confirms + sharpens: (1) durationMs=0 RULED OUT — 3000ms hangs identically. (2) EXACT 1:1 self-feeding loop: cumulative agent_output.initial=2500 == workflow.output.completed=2500. Each completed output triggers one fresh observer re-subscription from sequence 0, which re-reads history -> another completed output -> another re-subscription. Never converges. (3) Loop is UPSTREAM of tool execution: McpServer.tools/call completed=0, acp.tool_result=0, and NO clock/sleep/timer/ClockWakeup-named span at all (durable sleep never observed to run). Refined hypothesis: per-context agent-output observer is (re)created per output.completed and seeds from initial(seq 0) instead of resuming after last-delivered sequence; delivering an output re-triggers creation -> closed loop. Fix likely: resume observer from last-observed sequence (incremental) and/or create once per turn not per output event. Running triage doc: docs/investigations/2026-05-21-live-acp-tool-call-triage.md

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: after_sequence distribution PROVES re-read amplification (not a producer loop): ~107 real output sequences (after_seq -1..106), each re-queried ~80x (initial(-1) 81x, initial(0) 81x ... decaying to 17x for newest) = ~2642 initial spans. Decay (earliest re-read most) = observer RESTARTS ~80x and replays history from start each time (seeds from initial, not after-last-sequence). SOURCE: per-context-output.ts exposes initial(L147, from-scratch) + after(L156/171, incremental); trace load is all on initial. Consumers of initial: runtime-context.ts:281 (events.initial inside the WORKFLOW BODY) + runtime-observation-streams.ts:103. Each workflow-body resume re-runs initial at the SAME checkpointed afterSequence (not advancing) -> re-reads history. NOTE the ACP edge uses forContext (full stream), not initial, so the storm is in the runtime-context workflow body / observation streams, not the edge. Likely a wait-without-progress loop: body awaits tool result -> wakes -> re-reads outputs via initial -> no terminal output -> re-awaits, ~80x until edge 30s timeout; durable sleep never observed to run (no clock span). Fix direction (UNVERIFIED): hold an incremental after(lastSeq) stream for the turn instead of re-entering initial per resume. Needs source read of runtime-context.ts workflow body to confirm why it resumes without advancing.

comments.author: gary
comments.text: Escalated to P0 based on live Zed ACP evidence and architecture impact. Created stacked P0 reference follow-on tf-ly2g to make the target tiny-firegrid reference prove the missing agent-output/result-return seam: durable output append log, incremental resume-after(lastSequence) observation, tool-call -> tool_result -> TurnComplete round-trip, and an O(outputs) trace invariant against re-read amplification. Provider-specific enumeration remains out of scope for the reference.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: CORRECTION + root cause (source-read). (1) 'execute' also hangs -> tool-INDEPENDENT (sleep 0ms/3000ms + execute all hang). (2) Earlier 'tool ran, result didn't return' was WRONG: McpServer.tools/call=0, Toolkit.handle=0, mcp.runtime_context.resolve=0, agent_tools.tool_use.execute=0 -> no tool reached the handler body (resolve fires-and-closes EARLY in handler; its absence = turn never progressed to issue the call). ROOT CAUSE LOCALIZED: runtime-context.ts:274 completedRuntimeOutput wraps events.initial(afterSequence=lastProcessedOutputSequence) (span=workflow.output.completed) called per body step (L762). It is a LIVE NON-MEMOIZED DurableTable read INSIDE the durable workflow body, so it re-executes on every @effect/workflow replay/resume -> O(resumes x output-history). after_sequence decay (-1 read 81x ... 106 read 17x) is the replay signature. Tool-call flow (verified toolkit-layer.ts:58 + successful wait-pre-attach sim): MCP tools/call -> Toolkit.handle -> handleTool runs ToolCallWorkflow on runtime-context engine, BLOCKS on host-owned deferred; result returns as MCP response (not via output stream). Open Q (needs tf-9ia9 in-flight spans): agent blocked mid-tools/call (open span) vs never issuing it; resolve=0 leans 'never issued'. FIX DIRECTION: don't re-run live output reads on replay - single durable streaming consumer advancing afterSequence once per output (O(outputs)), or memoize as activity, or move output egress off the replay path.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: SOURCE-VERIFIED (pinned to lines). Body driver runMergedEventLoop (runtime-context.ts:815-845): (1) the output-discovery read is the ONLY loop op NOT an Activity — siblings startSessionActivity(L139)/sendSessionActivity(L167)/runToolUseActivity(L395)/transitionRuntimeContextEventActivity(L695) are Activity.make (memoized on replay), but completedRuntimeContextEvent(L749)->completedRuntimeOutput(L762)->events.initial(L281) is a plain LIVE Effect that re-executes every replay; (2) loop state is an in-memory Ref(L820) seeded initial(lastProcessedOutputSequence=-1, L349) that does NOT survive replay, so each resume re-walks from seq -1 re-running the live read per sequence; (3) events.initial is a FULL output-table scan (table.events.rows() reduced to min seq>after, per-context-output.ts:110-140). Resume cadence: body parks in awaitNextRuntimeContextEvent->DurableDeferred.await(L210); every agent output chunk appends a row -> resume -> full re-walk. ~107 chunks -> ~80 resumes -> O(replays x history x rows) => the storm + after_sequence decay. Memoized transitions kept short turns cheap (hid the bug). FIX = make output observation a single replay-safe streaming consumer advancing afterSequence once per output (O(outputs)) [matches SDD output-observation target], OR memoize the read as an activity, OR move output egress off the replay path. Status: root cause source-verified.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: DRAFT PR #612 (hotfix, merge-gated). FIX: memoize immutable completedRuntimeOutput/events.initial Some results in a process-scoped Ref<HashMap> created once at registration (survives in-process replays), threaded down the output path; None frontier re-polls live. Kills the QUADRATIC re-walk (the live read re-executed full-table-scan on every @effect/workflow replay because the merged-loop cursor is a non-durable Ref reset to -1). EVIDENCE (real claude-agent-acp@0.36.1 via FiregridLocalHostLive + production workflow, new sim tf-7kq8-output-replay-storm, apples-to-apples A/B): verbose text-only turn main=129 reads/42 seq=3.1x vs fix=51/42=1.2x (both complete); tool prompt main=77/11=7.0x vs fix=21/11=1.9x. Ratio is size-dependent (quadratic): live Zed turn was 74x (7951 reads)=the hang; fix flattens to ~1.2x regardless of size. HONEST CAVEAT: eliminates the quadratic re-walk; small LINEAR frontier-poll residual remains by design (removed only by lane-2 durable-await streaming consumer tf-ly2g). Sims show ratio drop at small scale, not literal hang->complete (hang = live 74x). Gates green (typecheck runtime+tiny-firegrid; host-sdk workflow tests 14; lint+dead+dup+deps; effect-diag baseline). FINDING: docs/research/tf-7kq8-output-observation-replay-storm.FINDING.md. Scope: runtime-context.ts output path + repro sim only; #607 untouched.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: Review blockers addressed on #612 (force-pushed, rebased): BLOCKER 1 (stale/destructive branch): rebased onto current origin/main (was 5 commits behind #599/#609/#610/#611 -> two-dot view looked like reversions); final diff is now ONLY the 5 hotfix files (runtime-context.ts + sim + FINDING), 309 insertions, no reversions; Semgrep will re-run clean. BLOCKER 2 (tool-call round trip not proven): root was the missing session.permissions.autoApprove (claude-agent-acp gates tool calls behind canUseTool; un-approved -> tools/call never reaches handler -> the tools/call:0 in the prior revision). Added autoApprove + sleep tool prompt. POST-FIX tf-7kq8 sim run (real claude-agent-acp, production path): McpServer.tools/call=1, Toolkit.handle=1, agent_tools.tool_use.execute=1, agent marker FIREGRID_SLEEP_DONE observed, DriverCompleted -- full Firegrid sleep round trip completes. Same scenario A/B reads: main 47/13=3.6x vs fix 21/13=1.6x. Independent wait-pre-attach-roundtrip on fixed branch corroborates (tools/call=1, Toolkit.handle=1, tool_use.execute=1, DriverCompleted, 1.4x). Verbose text-only A/B: main 3.1x vs fix 1.2x. Honest: eliminates quadratic re-walk; small linear frontier-poll residual remains BY DESIGN (explicitly temporary; lane-2 durable cursor tf-ly2g removes it). typecheck+lint+deps green (verified before push this time).
