title: TFIND-048: MCP route + URL lifecycle ownership unresolved in #332 model
description:
Area: client/host boundary / MCP route + URL lifecycle.

Status: open — REFRAMED 2026-05-18 (Reading 2 / architectural; was mis-shaped as 'client-sdk missing helper'); framing-gated, folds into the #332-impl MCP-lifecycle question; blocks Codex ACP.

Triage: cat-1/2 architectural (real production-model gap, not a missing API: re-exporting sessionContextIdForExternalKey would canonize the backwards lifecycle = WRONG fix. The determinism primitive itself is sound; the smell is URL-lifecycle ownership. Migration-as-validation working exactly as designed — proved #332 left an MCP-lifecycle hole. NOT cat-3 toy-fix).

The #332 client/host model does not resolve who builds the concrete contextId-scoped MCP URL and when. Codex ACP bakes it client-side pre-createOrLoad; production (host-sdk/mcp-host.ts, CLI) has the host own the MCP server with a /mcp/runtime-context/:contextId route resolved at tool-call time. Client-baking a concrete URL into the intent is test-fixture-shaped.

Evidence (file:line):
- sessionContextIdForExternalKey (protocol/src/session-facade/schema.ts:482) is sound deterministic primitive for client/host independent reconciliation, NOT for clients to pre-bake route URLs.
- Production host owns MCP server: host-sdk/src/host/mcp-host.ts:1-26 mounts /mcp/runtime-context/:contextId; route param is request authority, resolved at tool-call time (:118-124), no pre-provisioning.
- Codex ACP test/config builds concrete URL pre-createOrLoad and embeds in intent — test fixture starts server before context exists.
- SDD_CONSOLIDATED_CLIENT_HOST_BOUNDARY.md:49-95 is silent on who builds the concrete contextId-scoped MCP URL and when.
- No other config uses MCP or pre-derives contextId-scoped URL. Codex ACP is unique.

Resolution path: architectural framing decision on MCP route/URL lifecycle in #332 model. Codex ACP stays paused/unmigrated; resumed migration is the validation.
close_reason: DECIDED: Reading 2 / Option A. Host provisions the contextId-scoped MCP route URL and delivers it at start; the protocol carries a URL-less runtimeContextMcp marker on schema so clients structurally cannot express the host-owned fact. sessionContextIdForExternalKey remains the determinism primitive for client/host reconciliation, not a client-side route-URL pre-bake. CLI co-located pre-bake is the documented exception, not the model. Five conditions ratified and honored in PR #344: (1) host-scoped FiregridRuntimeContextMcpBaseUrl Option-typed primitive with single construction site, late-bound on bind; (2) URL-less runtimeContextMcp marker on protocol schema; (3) single injection site at codec-adapter resolveEffectiveMcpServers, with CLI pre-createOrLoad deletions confirmed; (4) Codex ACP resume validates as deterministic gating seam with zero real-LLM dependence — no it.skip, no test-side shortcuts, real-LLM e2e demoted to non-gating smoke (not deleted); (5) doc-commented SINGLE-PURPOSE primitive — do not generalize. Deterministic-seam restructure complete via oca2; CI green across all five gates. Dependents tf-7u6 (codex-acp-tool-call-pipeline config) and tf-kdf (TFIND-051: codex-acp re-runnability) unblock on close.

title: Production sync-handshake semantics on createOrLoad/call channels (compat-gated)
description: Per tf-lfxs GREEN-but-narrow verdict (sim finding): the createOrLoad-style CALLABLE binding can absorb the request-row → reflected-context barrier, eliminating a separate client whenReady at call sites for operations whose RESULT gates the next action (sync axis). Async axis needs NO new abstraction — existing send + wait_for over a neutral bidirectional DurableTable-backed channel already works via tool lowering / WaitForWorkflow. ACTIONABLE: implement production sync-handshake semantics on createOrLoad (and other call channels where result-gates-next-action) — GATED by a compatibility/behavior review (do NOT migrate production createOrLoad from the sim alone). HARD NON-GOALS (from the finding): do NOT dispatch a broad new Channel/Queue abstraction; do NOT expose effect/Channel/Queue/Mailbox as Firegrid API. The SDD (SDD_FIREGRID_DURABLE_CHANNELS_SYNC_ASYNC.md) is canonized as useful per the verdict.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by tf-lf9p (gary's precisely-scoped version of the same tf-lfxs GREEN follow-up). tf-lf9p encodes the narrow scope — move request-reflection barrier into callable channel bindings (createOrLoad return: request-row ack → reflected RuntimeContext evidence), keep whenReady redundant — plus the explicit guardrails (no whenReady deletion, no tf-fyyk prompt work, no new public verb, no sync/async API split, no broad Channel/Queue abstraction, no Effect Channel/Queue/Mailbox, no provider channels). title: client-sdk wait.forAgentOutput hot-loops at ~30k iter/sec: missing afterSequence auto-threading in driver while loop (per tf-s8y, ~66k contexts.get in 4 min)
description:
Post-spike (tf-s8y) finding. Pre-existing bug, exposed by spike trace analysis.

## What was observed

In the tf-s8y dark-factory trace (4-min run):
- 66,452 calls to `firegrid.runtime.contexts.get`
- All parented by `firegrid.client.session.wait.for_agent_output`
- All with `row_found: true` (finds the row every time)
- 32% of all sdk-side spans

## Root cause

Driver code in `packages/tiny-firegrid/src/simulations/dark-factory/driver.ts`:

    while (true) {
      yield* session.wait.forAgentOutput({ timeoutMs: 15_000 })
    }

`session.wait.forAgentOutput` internally (packages/client-sdk/src/firegrid.ts:558-585):
1. Calls `resolveContext(contextId)` → `control.contexts.get(contextId)`
2. `projectionWait(output.events.rows().filterMap(...), observation => observation.contextId === contextId && (afterSequence === undefined || observation.sequence > afterSequence))`

Since the driver passes NO `afterSequence`, the predicate matches the FIRST emitted observation every iteration. projectionWait completes immediately with the first match. while(true) iterates at full speed.

## Why this is pre-existing

Same hot loop likely present in the 2026-05-19 dark-factory live run (baseline) and any other while-loop driver. tf-s8y just made it visible by attributing 66k spans to a single span name.

## Fix candidates

(a) `session.wait.forAgentOutput` returns the observation + auto-threads its sequence as the next call's `afterSequence` (state in the SessionHandle).
(b) Driver convention: track sequence manually and pass it explicitly each iteration.
(c) The session handle exposes a Stream<Observation> that consumers fold over with proper back-pressure instead of a while loop.

Option (a) preserves API ergonomics; (c) is the bigger architectural fix.

## Acceptance criteria

- Pick a fix variant
- Confirm by re-running dark-factory and counting `firegrid.durable_table.get` collection=contexts spans — should drop from ~66k/4min to ~tens
- Update dark-factory and any other while-loop-on-wait drivers

## Cross-references

- tf-s8y trace evidence (PR #444)
- packages/client-sdk/src/firegrid.ts:558-585 (waitForAgentOutputObservation)
- packages/client-sdk/src/internal/projection-wait.ts (projectionWait helper)
- tf-la7 (ProjectionStream brand — orthogonal but related wait-bucket discipline)
close_reason: DECIDED: PR #448 merged at bc75af274. Auto-thread afterSequence in FiregridSessionHandle.wait.forAgentOutput via per-handle Ref. Public surface unchanged. Unit test asserts structural invariant directly. Quantitative span-count drop (66k → single-digit-per-event) will manifest empirically in real-stack runs; correctness guaranteed by unit test.

title: Amend input-table cutover SDD: workflow assigns input order on consume
description: Decision bead surfaced by tf-i05u. Re-evaluate the cutover SDD's assumption that producers/server allocate dense input sequence numbers atomically. Compare P1 (DurableTable CAS), P2 (single-writer allocator), and P3 (producers insertOrGet by inputId; RuntimeContextWorkflow assigns processing order on consume and owns the cursor). Recommend P3 if it better matches SDD_TARGET_TINY_FIREGRID_ARCHITECTURE_REFERENCE and eliminates tf-i05u/tf-eta6 substrate coordination debt. Acceptance: SDD amendment clearly states chosen ordering model, impact on RuntimeContextWorkflowTable rows, idempotency, replay, cursor semantics, and which beads are superseded or still required.
close_reason: Merged in PR #653: input-table cutover SDD Amendment 1 adopts workflow-assigned input order on consume (P3).

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text:
DONE (commit d3e414efe, branch codex/tf-87vj-input-table-p3-workflow-order-sdd; no PR yet). Drafted Amendment 1 to SDD_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_WORKFLOW_INPUT_TABLE_CUTOVER.md. check:docs + check:specs green.

DECISION: adopt P3. Producers insertOrGet input rows keyed by inputId (idempotent, contention-safe via the existing insert-if-absent fence); the single per-context RuntimeContextWorkflow assigns the dense processing ordinal ON CONSUME and advances a workflow-owned durable input cursor (sibling of DurableOutputCursor / tf-qk6h). Load-bearing insight: the atomic-reservation requirement that broke tf-eta6/tf-i05u exists ONLY because the original spec assigns the dense sequence at WRITE time, where multi-author producers (client/workflow/tool/system) contend. Moving assignment to CONSUME time = single-writer workflow = no contention = no CAS, no multi-row transaction. Matches SDD_TARGET reference ("do not allocate sequences server-side").

ORDERING MODEL: delivery order = workflow consume order over arrival order; matches today effective semantics (single dispatcher already processes in arrival order). Not a client-controlled global order; cross-author ordering must be tested.

ROW SHAPE: drop contexts.nextInputSequenceToAssign; inputs PK = inputId (drop the separate inputIds index); keep contexts.nextInputSequence as the consume cursor; add workflow-private assignedInputs[ctx/ordinal]->inputId (point-addressable, single-writer). 

REPLAY/CURSOR: durable nextInputSequence reconstructed by one get (no Ref reset); consumed input read by point get on assignedInputs then inputs.get(inputId), never inputs.query; mirrors DurableOutputCursor INV-1..3. One open sub-point (both consume-side, both << P1/P2): P3-A tail inputs.rows() filtered, entered only on point-read miss (NO substrate change, preferred); or P3-B surface the insertOrGet append offset as arrival sequence (tiny additive read-surfacing). Input + output arms should converge on ONE durable-cursor primitive (tf-qk6h generalized).

BEAD IMPACT (acceptance):
- tf-i05u (CAS): SUPERSEDED -> close not-required.
- tf-eta6 (atomic append + point-addressing): SUPERSEDED as written; atomic-append dropped; point-addressing survives as workflow-assigned ordinals (existing composite-PK get, no new primitive); folds into the input-cursor work.
- tf-vrz6 (Stage A): STILL REQUIRED, re-scoped to this row shape; channel write becomes plain inputs.insertOrGet(inputId), no atomic-append precondition.
- F3 wakeup (engine.signal/table-write resume): STILL REQUIRED (suspended body still needs waking on input write).
- NEW: workflow-owned durable INPUT cursor -> fold into tf-qk6h (one merged-loop cursor for both arms).
- tf-9rpy (SCC gone): unchanged goal; P3 is the path (body reads input via cursor, not engine deferred mailbox -> body->engine input edge removed; still gated on F3 + legacy-mailbox deletions).

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: Draft PR opened: https://github.com/gurdasnijor/firegrid/pull/653 (branch codex/tf-87vj-input-table-p3-workflow-order-sdd, commit d3e414efe). PR body states the tf-i05u/tf-eta6/tf-vrz6 supersession decisions as REQUIRED of the coordinator; this PR does not close/re-scope them.

title: Runtime directory structure design: replace ambiguous authorities taxonomy
description: Design-first cleanup for the runtime source tree before moving more files. Current state has two confusing authority folders: packages/runtime/src/authorities/ for runtime control-plane capability providers and packages/runtime/src/agent-event-pipeline/authorities/ for runtime output/ingress capability providers. The term authority currently means unique Effect Layer provider for a durable table/capability family, but the directory taxonomy uses it as both a role and a domain, making the architecture hard to read. Scope: inventory current runtime src folders; define an intelligible domain-first directory target; decide whether authority remains a term, is renamed to capability/provider/commit-point, or is only used in docs; decide target homes for RuntimeControlPlaneRecorderLive, RuntimeControlRequests, RuntimeAgentOutputEvents, RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents, per-context runtime output providers, control-request dispatcher, streams, subscribers, codecs, and verified ingest. Output should be a short architecture note or SDD amendment with a target tree, naming glossary, and migration slices. Non-goals: no production file moves in this bead, no behavior changes, no deletion of load-bearing control-plane dispatcher/request-row bridge. Acceptance: downstream implementation beads can move/rename files without re-litigating terminology; there is no target tree with two unrelated directories named authorities.
close_reason: DONE: PR #575 merged. Runtime kernel directory design FINAL + ratified by Gurdas. Resolutions: (1) box = kernel/ (substrate reserved for the whole-Firegrid thesis; kernel = privileged durable core reached only via drivers exposing channels); (2) channel Live bindings co-locate to runtime/kernel/channels — protocol owns contracts, runtime/kernel owns durable implementations, host-sdk only composes; (3) pipeline adapters stay sibling outside kernel; (4) Slice-3 = tf-bffo sub-bead (above-box doorway already channel-clean; only a bounded 3-file reach-past tail + export-trim remain). 4-slice migration mapped. Unblocks tf-bffo.

comments.author: gary
comments.text: Additional scope from Gurdas: the current packages/runtime/src/control-plane/ directory is also suspect, not just the two authorities dirs. Current contents are only index.ts and control-request-dispatcher.ts, and index.ts re-exports ../authorities, so the directory is acting as an awkward umbrella for request-row daemon + control-plane capability exports. Design must decide whether control-plane remains a bounded context directory, gets split into explicit request-dispatch/authority modules, or is renamed under a clearer runtime-host/control-requests domain. Do not let implementation treat control-plane as already-canonical.

comments.author: gary
comments.text: Architectural correction from Gurdas: do not frame runtime authority services as the approved doorway that application/runtime code should depend on long-term. With the channel architecture, write ownership should be fully isolated inside the substrate box; code above the substrate boundary accesses durable state through channels, not RuntimeContextInsert/RuntimeRuns/RuntimeControlRequests-style service doors. The design should treat current authority tags as migration-era substrate internals or implementation details, not as a public/upper-runtime abstraction to preserve. Target directory structure should make the substrate box explicit and route above-boundary interaction through channel contracts.

comments.author: gary
comments.text: Additional required review scope from Gurdas: include packages/host-sdk in this pass, not only packages/runtime. The current host-sdk tree still contains substrate-shaped modules and imports: host/channels/* live layers touch RuntimeControlPlaneTable/RuntimeOutputTable directly; host/layers.ts composes RuntimeControlPlaneRecorderLive and RuntimeControlRequestControlPlaneLive; host/runtime-substrate.ts and per-context-runtime-output.ts expose runtime substrate wiring; agent-tools/execution/* depends on runtime workflow/tool execution and channel catalog internals; host/runtime-context-session/codec-adapter.ts reaches into workflow/session substrate. The design output must classify which host-sdk files are true binding-edge composition vs substrate internals that should move below the substrate box or become channel implementations. Acceptance should include a host-sdk leak inventory and migration slices, otherwise the runtime tree can be cleaned while host-sdk remains the parallel substrate entrypoint.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text: SCOPE EXPANSION (Gurdas): the design/review pass must cover packages/host-sdk too, not just runtime/src — else we clean runtime while host-sdk remains the parallel substrate entrypoint. Initial scan of host-sdk substrate-shaped surfaces to inventory + classify (binding-edge composition vs substrate-internal that should move below the substrate box OR become channel implementations): (1) host/channels/* live layers touch RuntimeControlPlaneTable / RuntimeOutputTable directly; (2) host/layers.ts composes RuntimeControlPlaneRecorderLive + RuntimeControlRequestControlPlaneLive; (3) host/runtime-substrate.ts + per-context-runtime-output.ts expose runtime substrate wiring; (4) agent-tools/execution/* depends on runtime workflow/tool execution + channel-catalog internals; (5) host/runtime-context-session/codec-adapter.ts reaches into workflow/session substrate. Required output now includes a host-sdk leak inventory + migration slices alongside the runtime-directory target tree. Carries the authorities correction (authority tags = substrate-internal, NOT a public doorway; channels are the single doorway above the substrate box).

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: DECIDED (Gurdas, 2026-05-21): channel Live bindings CO-LOCATE into runtime (runtime/{substrate|kernel}/channels, name TBD), NOT host-sdk. Ratified ownership split: @firegrid/protocol owns channel CONTRACTS/Tags/schemas; runtime owns the durable channel IMPLEMENTATIONS (Live bindings that touch DurableTable/runtime state); host-sdk only COMPOSES those Layers (topology assembly + projection edges), never owns durable-state wiring. Flips tf-8ryo design Bucket A (recommended leave-in-host-sdk). Rationale: bindings in host-sdk make host-sdk read as a second substrate doorway; co-location makes channels-are-the-only-doorway enforceable. tf-8ryo Q1 = NEEDS CHANGES; box-name substrate-vs-kernel still open; tf-bffo green-light pending revised design + naming.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: DECIDED (Gurdas, 2026-05-21): box name = kernel/ (not substrate/). "substrate" is overloaded for the whole Firegrid thesis and too floaty for an internal dir; "kernel" is well-defined and captures intent: you do NOT import the kernel directly into a program — you reach it through DRIVERS that expose CHANNELS. So: runtime/kernel = privileged durable core (write-ownership / the authority Tags live here, NOT a public import surface); drivers expose channels; code above interacts ONLY via channels. Combined with the Bucket-A co-location decision, the design revision set is complete = {rename substrate->kernel + glossary the kernel/drivers/channels framing; co-locate channel Live bindings to runtime/kernel/channels, host-sdk composes only}. Everything else in tf-8ryo stood. tf-bffo green-lights on the revised #575.

title: AgentOutputEvent schema migration path — Schema.decodeUnknown strict-mode means future _tag union expansion fails decode on existing persisted rows
description:
Source-read tf-wdv0 verdict STRICT-DECODE-MIGRATION-RISK landed via PR #473 (now on main).

Root cause: runtimeAgentOutputObservationFromRow uses Schema.decodeUnknown which is STRICT — unknown _tag variants reject. Adding a new variant to the AgentOutputEvent _tag union without migration handling would cause persisted rows from the new variant to FAIL decode on older readers, AND vice versa.

Not blocking Phase 1 (current _tag union is fixed). Becomes load-bearing whenever the union expands.

Mitigation options (for whoever picks this up):
1. Schema migration layer (parseV1/parseV2 fallback chain)
2. Schema.optionalMatch-style permissive decoder that returns Option.none on unknown variants
3. Versioned envelope (currently the row carries the data directly; add a wrapper with schemaVersion field)

Not assigning a closure path — depends on which expansion lands first. Filed as follow-up so the migration concern is tracked.
close_reason: Completed: PR #552 merged (forward-compatible AgentOutputEvent unknown-tag decode path).

title: Scope claude-agent-acp settingSources to project-only for sim runs (user-level Skills leak into agent available_commands per tf-s8y)
description:
Post-spike (tf-s8y) finding. Sim-run hygiene.

## What was observed

tf-s8y's dark-factory run with the spike .mcp.json showed user-level Skills leaking into the agent's `available_commands_update`:
- cmux-browser, cmux-markdown
- effect-ai, effect-check, effect-core
- claude-api, batch, batching-caching, etc.

These are NOT Firegrid MCP tools; they're from `~/.claude/agent-memory/<agentType>/` or `~/.claude/settings.json`.

## Mechanism

claude-agent-acp@0.36.1 acp-agent.js:1414 hard-codes:
  settingSources: ["user", "project", "local"]

Passed to claude-agent-sdk options. SDK loads settings from all three sources. "user" = `~/.claude/settings.json` = whatever the host user has configured locally.

## Options to investigate

(a) Does claude-agent-sdk respect `_meta.claudeCode.options.settingSources` (i.e., does the userProvidedOptions spread at acp-agent.js:1416 honor an override)?

  if yes → codec adds `settingSources: ["project"]` to its `_meta.claudeCode.options`

(b) If no, spawn the agent subprocess with HOME shimmed to a sim-run temp dir so `~/.claude/` is empty/inert:

  child env: { ...process.env, HOME: '/tmp/firegrid-sim-home/<runId>' }

(c) Upstream issue with claude-agent-acp to support `_meta` override of settingSources.

## Acceptance criteria

- Identify whether (a) works via codec test against claude-agent-acp source
- If yes, implement codec change
- If no, implement (b) sandbox-spawn-time HOME shim OR (c) upstream issue + (b) as interim

## Cross-references

- tf-s8y / PR #444 (spike that surfaced this)
- tf-v7t (codec _meta split — depends on this for clean sim runs)
close_reason: DECIDED: shipped via PR #479 (merge f915bb15b). Closure path (a) — codec sends settingSources:['project'] via _meta.claudeCode.options spread; eliminates user-level Skills leak in sim runs.

title: Runtime shrink structural target: make engine/runtime-context SCC gone
description: Structural track target #1 from runtime-shrink-loop. Source-read the central multi-node SCC (engine-runtime.ts <-> runtime-context.ts <-> runtime-control in current measured report; doc may mention a four-way SCC from a larger corpus) and make a bounded implementation attempt only if there is a small direct cutover. Valid outcomes: merge the SCC into one module, or cleanly break the reciprocal cycle with a typed one-directional boundary. Acceptance: in --skeleton over the fixed corpus, the named SCC no longer appears as a multi-node SCC; tests/typecheck/lint pass; N does not rise except by a credited clean SCC break in baseline.broken_sccs. Guardrail: do not add an external await/deferred bridge or harden tf-jpcg-style bridge_debt. STOP if this spans broad architecture; report exact boundary and suggested SDD decision.
notes: STOP/report (see comment 2026-05-22): runtime-flow SCC, no static-import cutover; only legitimate break = workflow-owned-table input reshape (appendRuntimeInputDeferred removal) = full Phase 0 migration. Block behind that reshape; do not attempt standalone.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text:
STOP — spans broad architecture; no small direct cutover. SOURCE-READ VERDICT (engine-runtime.ts 503L, runtime-context.ts 961L, runtime-control-request.ts 83L):

WHY NOT A FILE CUTOVER: the SCC is a RUNTIME-FLOW cycle, not a static-import cycle. grep confirms ZERO import between runtime-context.ts and engine-runtime.ts in either direction (only a string-prefix const 'runtime-context:'). depcruise has no edge to flip.

EXACT BOUNDARY (the reciprocal seam):
- engine -> body: engine-runtime resume()/execute() invokes each registered Workflow body (entry.execute), incl. runtime-context + the 3 runtime-control.* workflows.
- body -> engine: runtime-context awaits its NUMBERED-DEFERRED INPUT MAILBOX -- DurableDeferred.await(runtimeInputDeferredFor(contextId, seq)) + engine.deferredResult(...) (runtime-context.ts:203-229,803,823) -- plus Activity.make execution via engine.activityExecute. runtime-control-request.ts adds more Workflow.make bodies driven by the same engine over request/claim/completion rows.

This is exactly the appendRuntimeInputDeferred mailbox the SDD_TARGET_TINY_FIREGRID_ARCHITECTURE_REFERENCE targets and the map's Reading A (co-locate to workflow-owned DurableTable). Co-locating the files would be a FAKE merge AND architecturally wrong: engine-runtime is the GENERIC WorkflowEngine for all workflows; the SDD forbids a router/specific-body inside the engine.

THE ONLY LEGITIMATE BREAK = replace the deferred-mailbox input with workflow-owned table reads (table.inputs.rows() filtered to sessions.nextInputSequence), making body->engine one-directional. That is the entire Phase 0 migration (tf-jpcg/tf-vfq9 territory), multi-week, and the dispatch guardrail forbids hardening the tf-jpcg external-await/deferred bridge.

REQUIRED SDD DECISION: sequence tf-9rpy BEHIND the workflow-owned-table input reshape (the appendRuntimeInputDeferred removal). The SCC cannot be made-gone before the deferred mailbox is replaced with table-state input. Recommend blocking tf-9rpy on that reshape bead and NOT attempting it as a standalone cutover.

title: Projection surfaces: client SDK and CLI presets over protocol launch/channel contracts
description:
Generalize Firegrid consumer surfaces as projections over protocol-owned substrate/channel contracts, not as separate orchestration APIs. This includes @firegrid/client-sdk, @firegrid/cli, future REST/gRPC/JSON-RPC bindings, and ergonomic agent presets such as `--agent codex` / `--agent claude`.

Ground truth:
1. `packages/client-sdk/README.md` documents @firegrid/client-sdk as writing durable session intent and reading durable projections. Its canonical create path is `sessions.createOrLoad({ runtime: local.jsonl(...) })`.
2. `packages/cli/src/bin/run.ts` currently lowers decoded argv to `local.jsonl({ argv: config.agentArgv, ...present })`.
3. `docs/cannon/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_SCHEMA_PROJECTION_CONTRACT.md` defines protocol -> bindings -> execution, where agent tools, client SDK, and CLI bind the same protocol contracts without importing execution substrate.
4. `docs/cannon/architecture/host-sdk-runtime-boundary.md` names client-sdk, host-sdk, and CLI as peer bindings over protocol schemas.

Target:
1. Treat client-sdk as the TypeScript/app projection of protocol operations and durable channel/session observations.
2. Treat CLI presets as command-line projections of the same launch/session contracts, compiling to `local.jsonl(...)` / protocol `RuntimeConfig` plus host-owned runtime-context MCP/channel attachments.
3. Make room for future REST, gRPC, JSON-RPC, or MCP bindings as additional projections over the same operation catalog.
4. Do not introduce a central graph/orchestration API; projections expose choreography primitives and observations.

Examples:
1. TypeScript: `sessions.createOrLoad({ runtime: local.jsonl({ argv: [...], agentProtocol: ... }) })`.
2. CLI: `pnpm firegrid -- run --agent codex --prompt ...` lowers to the same protocol launch intent.
3. Future REST/gRPC/JSON-RPC endpoints validate against the same protocol operation schemas and delegate to binding/execution services rather than cloning contracts.
acceptance_criteria:
1. Client SDK README and root README explain client/CLI/REST/gRPC/JSON-RPC as projections over protocol contracts, not separate graph APIs.
2. CLI preset design for Codex and Claude Code is specified as lowering to explicit protocol launch intent / `local.jsonl(...)` shape.
3. Tests prove preset lowering produces the same normalized `PublicLaunchRuntimeIntent` as explicit config.
4. Any future REST/gRPC/JSON-RPC binding plan points at protocol operation schemas as source of truth.
5. README quickstart distinguishes tested stable paths from scaffolded/advanced adapter paths honestly.
6. No central orchestration DSL or graph configuration API is added.

Write-path channel collapse (callable methods → channel dispatch, 6 write helpers deleted, operations.ts 64→14, invariant rg @firegrid/host-sdk packages/client-sdk/src=0, jscpd 96→48). Regression caught+fixed in review: context-exists guard restored in shared HostPermissionRespond factory + negative-control test. Deferred follow-ups: tf-fyyk (prompt egress-return), tf-qu7l (read-path). Unblocks tf-05jj Slice B, tf-cyet, tf-qu7l, tf-k138.

title: Collapse durable-tools/ onto the workflow engine — one substrate; see SDD_FIREGRID_ONE_SUBSTRATE_WORKFLOW_ENGINE.md

Per Gurdas's reframe, the correct move is bigger and simpler: COLLAPSE durable-tools/ onto the workflow engine. Agent-tool wait_for becomes engine.execute(WaitForWorkflow, ...) where WaitForWorkflow's body is DurableDeferred.raceAll([Activity(Stream.runHead), DurableClock.sleep]). Runtime-context body becomes stream-zip + handler. NO engine API changes needed; DurableStreamsWorkflowEngine.test.ts proves the engine already has all primitives.

Read SDD_FIREGRID_ONE_SUBSTRATE_WORKFLOW_ENGINE.md (the new SDD) for full framing + acceptance criteria + sequencing. Estimated net -2400 lines (delete durable-tools/ ~2500 + restructure runtime-context-workflow-core ~400; add WaitForWorkflow ~80 + test helper ~30).

  4. Restructure runtime-context-workflow-core.ts to stream-zip + per-event handler.
  5. Delete durable-tools/ in full.
  6. Body-plan Slice A (tf-lawq) becomes implementable on top.

Acceptance: durable-tools/ deleted; 5 tf-qoyg-hanging tests pass via new sync; workflow-core-paths sim shows AgentOutputAfter wait_for.match/wait_router.complete_match go to zero (substrate deleted); CallerFact path runs via WaitForWorkflow; restart-replay sim demonstrates engine durability; body-plan SDD Slice A unblocked.

title: Runtime control-plane cleanup: align dispatcher and recorder with channel/capability architecture
description: Post-baseline cleanup for vestigial control-plane naming/API traces. `packages/runtime/src/control-plane/control-request-dispatcher.ts` and `packages/runtime/src/authorities/runtime-control-plane-recorder.ts` are load-bearing, not dead: the dispatcher bridges protocol-owned durable control request rows into runtime workflow execution, and the recorder/authority layer is the single provider for RuntimeControlPlaneTable contexts/runs/control-request capability tags. Scope: rename/refactor the old reconciler/recorder vocabulary so the code reads as the target architecture (control-plane dispatcher/daemon + runtime control-plane authority capabilities), remove no-op deprecated option fields if callers/tests no longer need them, hide internals from public barrels where possible, and update docs/tests/spec ACIDs that still describe polling/reconciler-era concepts. Non-goals: do not delete request-row substrate, do not remove RuntimeControlRequests/RuntimeContextInsert/RuntimeRunAppendAndGet capabilities, do not change client channel semantics. Acceptance: imports/exports reflect dispatcher/authority names; no host-sdk public composition exposes reconciler/engine internals; tests cover daemon row-subscription behavior and authority-provider uniqueness; docs explain why these files remain below the channel layer.

comments.author: gary
comments.text: Sequencing update: make this implementation cleanup depend on tf-8ryo first. The current two-authorities-folder taxonomy is itself part of the problem, so rename/move work should wait for the directory-structure design note instead of choosing names locally.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text: SCOPE REFINEMENT (Gurdas): source-tree organization should become DOMAIN-FIRST / ROLE-SECOND, not two folders named 'authorities' at different levels. 'authority' may remain a narrow CONCEPT in docs if useful, but the source tree should organize by DOMAIN (e.g. control-plane/, runtime-output/) with role (recorder/dispatcher/authority) as a secondary descriptor — NOT a top-level role-named 'authorities/' folder duplicated across levels. So tf-bffo's cleanup = (1) align dispatcher/recorder vocabulary + hide internals from public barrels (original scope) AND (2) reorganize the source tree domain-first, collapsing the duplicate 'authorities' folders into their domains. Still gated on the tf-dr0k/#570 baseline gate.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text: CORRECTION (Gurdas) — authorities target framing: the authority tags (RuntimeContextInsert / RuntimeRuns / RuntimeControlRequests-style) are MIGRATION-ERA substrate internals / implementation details — NOT a public doorway for application/runtime code, NOT an abstraction to preserve. TARGET ARCHITECTURE: write-ownership lives INSIDE the substrate box; code ABOVE that boundary accesses durable state through CHANNELS (the single doorway). So tf-bffo's directory design must (a) make the substrate box EXPLICIT, and (b) PREVENT the 'authority' vocabulary from becoming a SECOND API layer alongside channels. The domain-first/role-second reorg is therefore not just cosmetics — it draws the substrate-box boundary so authorities read as inside-the-box internals, not a competing public abstraction. Cross-ref tf-8ryo (Gurdas updated it with this correction). Still gated on #570.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: DECIDED (Gurdas, 2026-05-21): channel Live bindings CO-LOCATE into runtime (runtime/{substrate|kernel}/channels, name TBD), NOT host-sdk. Ratified ownership split: @firegrid/protocol owns channel CONTRACTS/Tags/schemas; runtime owns the durable channel IMPLEMENTATIONS (Live bindings that touch DurableTable/runtime state); host-sdk only COMPOSES those Layers (topology assembly + projection edges), never owns durable-state wiring. Flips tf-8ryo design Bucket A (recommended leave-in-host-sdk). Rationale: bindings in host-sdk make host-sdk read as a second substrate doorway; co-location makes channels-are-the-only-doorway enforceable. tf-8ryo Q1 = NEEDS CHANGES; box-name substrate-vs-kernel still open; tf-bffo green-light pending revised design + naming.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: DECIDED (Gurdas, 2026-05-21): box name = kernel/ (not substrate/). "substrate" is overloaded for the whole Firegrid thesis and too floaty for an internal dir; "kernel" is well-defined and captures intent: you do NOT import the kernel directly into a program — you reach it through DRIVERS that expose CHANNELS. So: runtime/kernel = privileged durable core (write-ownership / the authority Tags live here, NOT a public import surface); drivers expose channels; code above interacts ONLY via channels. Combined with the Bucket-A co-location decision, the design revision set is complete = {rename substrate->kernel + glossary the kernel/drivers/channels framing; co-locate channel Live bindings to runtime/kernel/channels, host-sdk composes only}. Everything else in tf-8ryo stood. tf-bffo green-lights on the revised #575.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: Q1 RESOLVED via tf-8ryo Slice-3 consumer audit (#575 §9, CI green, read-only). Slice 3 = SUB-BEAD of tf-bffo, NOT a peer bead. Key finding: the true above-box doorway is ALREADY channel-clean — @firegrid/client-sdk + @firegrid/cli have ZERO authority-Tag usage in src (one client-sdk TEST harness shortcut reads RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents); apps/factory does not exist. The channel-collapse wave (tf-aago/tf-qu7l/tf-jbtu/tf-05jj) already moved the app-facing surface onto channels. So Slice 3 is NOT a large external-consumer migration. Consumer classes: (a) box-interior re-point in Slices 1-2 [authorities, control-plane, agent-event-pipeline/authorities, workflow-engine/workflows, streams]; (b) host-adapter glue KEEP/re-point only [host/layers.ts, config-live.ts, agent-tool-host-live.ts, commands.ts, runtime-context-helpers.ts, mcp-host.ts]; (c) Bucket-B move-below-box Slice 4 [runtime-substrate.ts, per-context-runtime-output.ts]; (d) CHANNEL-MIGRATION TAIL = the only genuine migrations = 3 files reading RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents directly should use existing SessionAgentOutputChannel [sims inv1-stream-zip-body + phase0-wave-2b, plus agent-tool-host-live read]; (e) runtime/src/index.ts = the export surface Slice 3 trims (one file). False positive: host/sync-run.ts (comment only). COORDINATOR PLAN: keep Slice 3 inside tf-bffo; isolate ONLY the runtime/src/index.ts export-trim (the single behavior-surface change) as a THIN sub-bead vs the cosmetic renames, for clean review/revert.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text:
SLICE 1 GREEN → DRAFT PR #580 (base main, codex/tf-bffo-kernel-migration). Full preflight green 2/2 under calm conditions (typecheck/lint/lint:dead/lint:dup/lint:deps/lint:semgrep/effect:diagnostics/test). Zero `authorities/` dirs remain: authorities+control-plane → kernel/control-plane; agent-event-pipeline/authorities → kernel/observation. Git-tracked pure renames; @firegrid/runtime/control-plane + /runtime-output subpath names unchanged (targets re-pointed). Tooling reconciled: .semgrep.yml, .dependency-cruiser.cjs sanctioned subpaths, runtime README + boundary SDD kernel role.

⚠ FORCED DEVIATION from #575 §2 literal layout — needs your ack before merge (merge is gated anyway): the design shows recorder.ts + request-dispatcher.ts as flat siblings in kernel/control-plane/. That CANNOT pass the hard-zero `runtime-src-no-folder-cycles` depcruise rule — recorder is a leaf consumed by workflow-engine, while the dispatcher depends on workflow-engine, so co-locating them recreates a control-plane ↔ workflow-engine folder cycle that the OLD separate authorities/+control-plane/ folders avoided. (It also caused a real module-init crash: Schema.optional ast-undefined in tiny-firegrid.) Minimal fix preserving "control-plane domain owns both": isolate the leaf write-owner into kernel/control-plane/recorder/ (no path to workflow-engine); request-dispatcher.ts + the combined barrel stay one level up. NOTE: this same shape question will recur in Slice 2 when workflow-engine moves under kernel/ (the cycle is at directory granularity regardless of where workflow-engine lives).

PAUSING before Slice 2 per "land Slice 1 standalone to validate before the rest." Will start Slice 2 on your go (or once #580 merges). Flake note: start-runtime.test.ts "duplicate starts" dedup test is timing-flaky under heavy concurrent machine load (failed 2x while I had parallel verify runs going; passes 8/8 isolated incl. turbo test --force 3/3; main flakes identically — pre-existing, not from this rename).

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: REFRAME (Gurdas rejected #580, 2026-05-21): the "pure-rename Slice 1 first" approach is DROPPED — moving folders under kernel/ is layout churn, not progress, and is not how the goal is met. tf-bffo must deliver the SEMANTIC boundary enforcement that makes "channels are the only doorway" REAL + demonstrable: (1) LEAD = narrow the public export surface (remove authority tags + kernel internals — RuntimeControlPlaneRecorder/RuntimeContextInsert/RuntimeRuns/RuntimeControlRequests/dispatcher/output-journal internals — from runtime/src/index.ts + any host-sdk public barrel) so above-box code CANNOT import them; internal composition re-points to non-public paths; (2) co-locate channel Live bindings host/channels/* -> kernel; (3) migrate the 3-file RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents reach-past tail -> SessionAgentOutputChannel. Folder layout is INCIDENTAL — only where it serves the boundary, never standalone. Demonstrate above-box code can only reach durable state via channels.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text:
RE-AIM PROGRESS — branch codex/tf-bffo-channel-boundary (off main; #580 closed, kernel-rename dropped).

✅ LEAD DELIVERABLE DONE + FULL PREFLIGHT GREEN (all gates incl. new demonstration test): narrowed the @firegrid/runtime ROOT public barrel to EXCLUDE the kernel internals — RuntimeControlPlaneRecorderLive, RuntimeContextInsert(Live), RuntimeContexts, RuntimeContextRead, RuntimeLocalContextResolver, RuntimeRuns, RuntimeRunAppendAndGet, RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents, RuntimeAgentOutputEvents(Layer), RuntimeAuthorityAgentOutputObservation. Above-box code (apps/client-sdk/cli) can no longer import these from @firegrid/runtime; internal host composition keeps working via the /control-plane + /runtime-output subpaths (re-pointed 2 host-sdk tests). Added packages/runtime/test/public-surface-boundary.test.ts asserting root excludes them while subpaths still expose them. host-sdk public barrel was already clean of these (verified). Audit confirmed: only those 2 host-sdk TESTS imported the tags from root; client-sdk/cli channel-clean.

FORK on the two PLUS moves — both larger/riskier than the headline; want your steer before I burn effort:
1. Channel Live-binding co-location (host/channels/* → runtime): feasible via the ratified split — move the durable RuntimeOutputTable/RuntimeControlPlaneTable-touching BUILDER into runtime, host-sdk keeps the thin Layer that injects RuntimeHostConfig/CurrentHostSession. But it's 5-6 bindings (~846 LOC) of cross-package surgery + composition rewiring.
2. Reach-past → SessionAgentOutputChannel: the 3 consumers are NOT above-box product code (2 tiny-firegrid replay sims + agent-tool-host-live, a host-sdk composition layer). And it's not a trivial swap — RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents.after(contextId, seq) is an after-SEQUENCE stream; SessionAgentOutputChannel is the full ingress stream, so the sims' zip/replay semantics need real rework.

QUESTION: (a) press on and land all of {lead + co-location + reach-past} in this ONE PR now (will take a while, higher risk), or (b) open the boundary PR with the lead (the real, demonstrable above-box boundary change) now and do co-location + reach-past as the immediate next commits on the same branch? Either way it stays one branch/PR — just asking whether to gate the PR-open on the heavier moves. Lead is committed + green; nothing pushed yet.

title: Expose delegated session output as agent-visible wait_for channel
description:
Investigation reference: docs/investigations/2026-05-21-acp-parent-child-output-channel-gap.md

Live Zed testing confirmed the parent can call session_new and session_prompt, but no host-declared ingress/wait surface lets the parent observe the child session's agent output. The agent had to guess channel names and unknown-channel behavior blocked retrieval. The existing host path has SessionAgentOutputChannel as a separate service while wait_for only exposes registered MCP/lifecycle/checkpoint channels.

Required fix: expose a declared child/delegated session output wait surface, e.g. session.agent_output or equivalent, keyed by sessionId/contextId. session_new/tool metadata should tell the agent the channel name and match shape instead of relying on guesses. The channel must support cursor/afterSequence semantics so parent sessions do not re-read stale child output.

Acceptance criteria:
- A targeted or live ACP run can perform session_new -> session_prompt -> wait_for and observe the child TextChunk/TurnComplete output.
- session_new or tool metadata documents the observable channel and fields needed to wait on it.
- The implementation avoids stale replay/duplicate reads via sequence/cursor handling.

STOP/EXIT instruction: if this requires redesigning the wait_for source model, RuntimeObservationSource, router dynamic route semantics, parent/child authority, or workflow input/result ownership beyond a narrow patch, exit the task and report exactly which architectural boundary prevents completion so we can do a focused architecture analysis.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: STOPPED per STOP/EXIT instruction — NOT a narrow patch. Draft PR #641 + docs/investigations/2026-05-22-delegated-session-output-wait-boundary.md. Source-verified: agent wait_for input is {channel,match} only (no sessionId/afterSequence, schema.ts:103-107); router resolves static targets by exact string (router.ts:131-143); lowering uniformly builds CallerFact{stream:target}, never cursored AgentOutputAfter (tool-use-to-effect.ts:359); child contextId is runtime-created by session_new (==sessionId) with no output-channel binding and no durable parent->child observation authority. Crosses ALL FOUR named boundaries: wait_for source model, RuntimeObservationSource, router dynamic routes, parent/child authority + child-result ownership. Request is inexpressible+unroutable+unsourced+unauthorized. Blocked on tf-8hwy (architecture SDD); tf-1ymw (protocol contract) filed. No production code changed.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: COORDINATOR REFRAME 2026-05-22: the original 'wait_for channel' wording should be read as 'make child output observable through the existing typed channel/router substrate,' not as 'invent a new wait_for/session_read protocol.' Artifact to delete: guessed dynamic channel strings. Load-bearing primitive: SessionAgentOutputChannel scoped to child session/context, routed/authorized through the host-plane channel router with cursor semantics from the underlying output stream.

Verify target-reference simulate run/perf and typecheck.

title: TFIND-005: Workflow layer composition leaks type precision (KEYSTONE)
description:
Area: Effect layer typing.

Status: #326 VERIFIED (Crux-B both arms pass; strict scope; gates green), NOT flip-ready — co-gated on TFIND-044 + TFIND-045 + TFIND-050.

Triage: cat-2 (rubric anchor — any leaks Layer precision through public host factories; type-honesty boundary).

Composing Workflow.toLayer, DurableTable.layer, and DurableStreamsWorkflowEngine.layer leaks any through Layer pipe inference even when every consumed service is named explicitly.

FRAMING SIGNED OFF (Gurdas 2026-05-18): curried (ns,schemas)<Self>() shape accepted; options-object / .tag<Self>() rejected. The SDD proved minimal fix inert and naive precise fix type-unsound (cross-table Identifier unification). Implementation on #326; zero runtime/behavior change.

EXECUTION VERIFIED (Agent 2, 2026-05-18): Strict idiom migration on 15af74c4e. Crux-B binding condition MET (both probe arms pass); strict scope confirmed (7 prod + 14 test idiom appends); gates green (lint + tests). Flip HARD-GATED on flamecast typecheck — co-gated on TFIND-044 (flamecast main.tsx:360) and TFIND-045 (host-sdk reconciler env leak).

Keystone cascade: TFIND-007-step2 + TFIND-029 (#328) unblock on #326 merge.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text: STATUS REFRESH 2026-05-20 — all 4 blockers (tf-uiz, tf-fmh, tf-n3u, tf-qy4) are CLOSED. Bead transitioned blocked → open. Original scope was 'TFIND-005: Workflow layer composition leaks type precision (KEYSTONE)' from the prior arc. The §6 observability batch (#431) + session.whenReady (#435) + MRC (#434) have substantially reshaped the workflow-layer surface area; the type-precision concern may have been incidentally addressed OR may still apply to specific composition sites. Gurdas should reaffirm whether the original scope is still relevant, OR close as historical with rationale, OR re-scope with citation to specific current-main surface. Currently assigned oca1 — assignee may also be stale post-restructure.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text:
STALE-CHECK DISPOSITION (direct-source-verified on origin/main 0888d3a15): AFFIRM — original concern STILL APPLIES, re-enters actionable queue. Evidence:

(1) packages/effect-durable-operators/src/DurableTable.ts:191 still declares the leaky shape: `export type DurableTableTagClass<Schemas extends TableSchemas<Schemas>, Self = any>` with eslint-disable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any directly above. Line 1126 still has the load-bearing factory cast: `return DurableTableTag as unknown as DurableTableTagClass<Schemas>`. These are the exact two architectural defects the SDD called out.

(2) Commit 023dfadcc ("refactor(effect-durable-operators)!: DurableTable <Self>()-curry factory (closes Self=any honestly...)") is NOT in origin/main ancestry. `git merge-base --is-ancestor 023dfadcc origin/main` → false. The keystone migration was verified on the #326 branch but never merged to main; the "Strict idiom migration on 15af74c4e" note in the bead refers to a doc-only commit, not the implementation.

(3) Active workaround comments in 11+ files still reference TFIND-005 as a present-tense / future-tense problem:
  - packages/host-sdk/src/host/control-request-reconciler.ts:55 — "Before TFIND-005s curry, RuntimeOutputTables tag identity was any, so it was mutually assignable with RuntimeControlPlaneTable" (still discharging via explicit enumeration as workaround).
  - packages/effect-durable-operators/src/react.ts:49 — "once TFIND-005s curry makes them precise" (future tense — hasnt happened).
  - 8 to-be-migrated/* sims carrying identical "TFIND-005: production host factories still return a layer whose public..." comments.

(4) Current call sites use the OLD non-curried shape `extends DurableTable("ns", schemas)` everywhere: verified at WorkflowEngineTable, VerifiedWebhookFactTable, SandboxSupervisorCommandTable, TestSourceTable, all 12 test sites in durable-table.test.ts. No curried `extends DurableTable<MyTable>()(...)` form on main.

NET: the 4 blockers (tf-uiz, tf-fmh, tf-n3u, tf-qy4) closed without the keystone landing. The keystone is now unblocked AND unresolved. Recommendation: keep open, re-aim impl from the #326 branch state (commit 023dfadcc is the substantive impl; eefca6bd8/3377f05a1/6d7e03731 are completion/sweep follow-ups also off-main).

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text: AFFIRM 2026-05-20 — Gurdas direct-source-verified against origin/main HEAD 0888d3a15. Two-line verdict: (1) packages/effect-durable-operators/src/DurableTable.ts:191 still has Self = any default with eslint-disable directly above; (2) DurableTable.ts:1126 still has 'return DurableTableTag as unknown as DurableTableTagClass<Schemas>' — the load-bearing cast. The substantive curry-refactor commit 023dfadcc is NOT in main's ancestry (git merge-base --is-ancestor returns false). The 'Strict idiom migration on 15af74c4e' line in the prior status note was the DOC-correction commit, not the impl. 11+ files still carry active TFIND-005 workaround comments in present/future tense; all 12 current call sites use the old non-curried 'extends DurableTable("ns", schemas)' shape. Blockers cleared without keystone landing → unblocked AND unresolved. Implementation can be lifted from PR #326 branch (023dfadcc + 3 follow-up sweeps). READY TO DISPATCH.

title: Validation slice: prove HostKernelWorkflow-mediated host control plane + native evidence + prune plan (router=dispatch-intent only)
description: BLOCKING DEPENDENCY surfaced by tf-8aw5 first-move verification (lane 1, origin/main as of #601). Evidence: durable lifecycle cancel plumbing EXISTS (makeRuntimeLifecycleRequestRow lifecycle=cancel; agent-tool session_cancel), but the host-plane channel router has NO cancellation route. RuntimeHostControlChannelsLive dispatches create/prompt/start/permission/context/create-or-load ONLY, and SessionLifecycleChannel is EXPLICITLY observation-only, not router-dispatched. LOAD-BEARING QUESTION (gary): is observation-only intentional (cancel must flow through a different mechanism, e.g. direct durable lifecycle-row append below the router), or should SessionLifecycleChannel be promoted to a router-dispatched lifecycle route? This decision shapes tf-8aw5 (cancel), tf-rqyh (load/resume/close), and constrains the ACP edge surface. No new substrate invented until decided. Per transactional-cutover-rule, the chosen path is a direct cutover, not a parallel mechanism.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: DECIDED (gary, #602): Option A — add a DISTINCT host-plane router lifecycle-intent/cancel route (NOT overloading observation-only SessionLifecycleChannel). Router = edge dispatch boundary only; runtime/kernel owns route body + durable append. Invariant: exactly one durable lifecycle append impl post-cutover; ACP cancel + agent session_cancel converge on it (migrate/reuse appendCommittedLifecycleRequest below the runtime route body). Implementation proceeds on tf-c8cy (route+convergence); tf-8aw5 = ACP edge consumer next.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: ARCHITECTURE CORRECTION (Gurdas) — gary's Option A SUPERSEDED. The bug: both options assumed the router owns durable control-row appends; the router was growing an internal control plane (host-control-routes.ts/host-control.ts + protocol runtimeControlPlaneSchemas + control-request-dispatcher.ts). TARGET MODEL: router = thin EDGE/system-call boundary; cancel/close/resume/newSession/prompt/start SIGNAL a long-running HostKernelWorkflow (signal/mailbox) which exclusively owns RuntimeContextWorkflow lifecycle. Durable control-row schemas (inputIntents/contextRequests/startRequests/lifecycleRequests/controlRequestClaims/controlRequestCompletions) are KERNEL-PRIVATE; protocol owns public route/channel contracts only; SessionLifecycleChannel stays observation-only. tf-c8cy RE-SCOPED to decision/spike + SDD amendment for the HostKernelWorkflow signal boundary (NOT route implementation). tf-8aw5/tf-rqyh stay blocked until the boundary is defined.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: REFINED (Gurdas): NOT a broad refactor and NOT docs-only — a narrow VALIDATION SLICE. Implement HostKernelWorkflow as long-running exclusive owner of a small set of RuntimeContextWorkflow children; route minimal intents (create/load, start, prompt, cancel-if-feasible) via signal/mailbox; run through tiny-firegrid/narrow runtime slice; collect NATIVE evidence (trace/perf + durable rows); COMPARE vs today's dispatcher/request-row path; deliver a DELETION/PRUNE plan for control-request-dispatcher + lifecycle/claim/completion rows + host-control route bodies IF clean. RULE: anything accumulating claims/polling/request-rows/completions/retries/lifecycle-transitions/exclusive-ownership is a control plane → model as a WORKFLOW, not router bodies. Router stays decode/authorize/dispatch-intent. tf-8aw5/tf-rqyh re-scope around this proof.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: RESIDUAL from #603 (gary): the RuntimeInputIntentDispatcherLive / inputIntent row bridge is a MIGRATION TAIL — currently kernel-private interim, NOT final architecture. Fold it into this slice's evaluation + prune plan alongside control-request-dispatcher, lifecycle/claim/completion rows, and host-control route bodies. #603 merged as the rebase base.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: gary #602 review: slice DIRECTIONALLY SOUND as validation foundation; proves narrow workflow-mailbox path (zero request/claim/completion rows for create/load/start/prompt/cancel). CHANGES-NEEDED: CI red (Lint + Effect diagnostics) → fix before merge. NOT-yet-proven (prune-plan gate before deleting control-request-dispatcher / lifecycle-claim-completion rows): router→workflow dispatch, restart replay, duplicate/concurrent request identity (gary wants an explicit DUP/CONCURRENT SIGNAL GATE), host singleton/failover, close/resume, protocol control-row relocation. These gate the PRUNE, not this PR's merge.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: #602 GREEN (b99aca7d9): Effect diagnostics/Lint/Semgrep/Tests/Typecheck all SUCCESS; pnpm run verify passed. Added router→workflow dispatch proof: host-control-router test shows create/load + start routes dispatch into injected HostKernelControlPlane.signal (partially closes gary's router→workflow gap). SDD prune gate now lists all preconditions (dup/concurrent signal, restart replay, singleton/failover, close/resume, control-row relocation, migration-tail). gary's only hard blocker was the red CI — now cleared. Awaiting Gurdas merge-as-foundation confirm.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: DECISION (Gurdas): NO MERGE. #602 stays open as the unmerged validation proof — slice verdict + SDD prune gate are the deliverable; host-kernel.ts is NOT landing to main yet. ACP follow-ons (tf-8aw5/tf-rqyh) and the prune remain gated; do not build on a merged foundation (there isn't one). Held pending Gurdas's next call on coverage/cutover sequencing.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: SEQUENCING UPDATE (gary tf-3w1e): target-arch SDD narrowed to Phase 0A 'channel→workflow-owned DurableTable write/read→workflow reads table state' seam ONLY — explicitly does NOT validate Host→Child session workflow relation yet. tf-c8cy's #602 slice IS Host→Child orchestration, so it sits AHEAD of the validation sequence (reinforces the no-merge). New narrower start: tf-pzsl (impl tiny-firegrid workflow-owned table seam), blocked on tf-3w1e. Phase 0A forbidden list: authority, Host→Child orchestration, request/claim/completion/deferred bridges, input intents, appendRuntimeInputDeferred, WorkflowEngineTable.deferreds, RuntimeInputIntentDispatcherLive, RuntimeControlPlaneTable.inputIntents, broad registries, adapter subsystems. STOP/re-eval if complexity exceeds the bare channel→table→read seam. #602 stays parked pending Gurdas's re-sequencing call.

title: Implement HostKernelWorkflow-owned write+arm for runtime-context table inputs
description: Follow-on to S1 (#663) and tf-12q9 STOP. Engine-level blanket recovery sweep is unsound because DurableDeferred.await and table-waits are indistinguishable as suspended execution rows; it regresses deferredDone idempotency and interrupt terminality. Implement the sound shape: HostKernelWorkflow/kernel controller owns the serialized command that writes workflow-owned runtime-context input rows and arms/resumes the owning execution as the same durable control step. On restart, the kernel must replay/re-arm only genuine pending write+arm facts it owns, not blanket-resume arbitrary suspended workflows. Acceptance: S1 Probe A/B recover without explicit test redrive through the kernel-owned path; tf-gyxc interrupt terminality and deferred-done-idempotency stay green; no generic resume-all-suspended sweep; no new input deferred mailbox or ordering authority. STOP only if HostKernelWorkflow lacks the authority/identity needed to own context execution write+arm, and report the exact missing primitive/API.
close_reason: Completed: kernel-owned write+arm reference and final cannon/closeout docs merged in PRs #665 and #666.

title: ACP host-edge production cutover: FiregridAcpStdioEdgeLive + CLI preset (restores README/Zed quickstart)
description:
PRODUCTION cutover of the tf-5n1z.1 ACP host-edge spike (#586, viability proven). Per the transactional cutover rule, the spike must NOT close the implementation it motivates — this bead owns the production work + the README/Zed quickstart RESTORE (the restore gate moves here from tf-5n1z.1).

SHAPE (from tf-5n1z.1 FINDING): a configurable host edge adapter/layer (e.g. FiregridAcpStdioEdgeLive) composed into host topology from a host-plane edge config; a thin CLI preset that builds host config with the ACP stdio edge and routes process stdin/stdout into it (CLI = convenience, NOT the semantic API). BOUNDARY: public protocol/channel verbs only — no host-sdk internal modules / runtime / DurableTable / workflow / kernel.

DEFERRED ACP methods to resolve (from finding; some need their own blocking deps): loadSession/resumeSession via firegrid.sessions.attach + transcript replay; closeSession via a public session lifecycle/terminate control channel; cancel needs a public session cancel/control channel before it is more than a no-op; authenticate auth layer. Each unshipped method that the production edge advertises must have a blocking follow-up before merge (cutover rule).

ACCEPTANCE: `firegrid run`-style ACP host edge works end-to-end against a real external ACP client (Zed / codex-acp), turn-completion semantics (no process-exit hang), restores the README quickstart transactionally. Spike (tf-5n1z.1) closes when #586 merges; this bead carries the production line.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: ROUTER ALIGNMENT (tf-rd3d/#590): the production ACP host edge must DISPATCH THROUGH the host-plane channel router (FiregridHostChannelRouter), not wire to channels directly. Composition shape per SDD: Layer.mergeAll(FiregridRuntimeKernelLive, FiregridHostChannelRouterLive, FiregridAcpStdioEdgeLive(...)). The edge maps ACP methods → router routes (string-addressed dispatch) → route implementations in runtime/kernel. Same pattern for the MCP/CLI/HTTP edges.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: PRODUCTION TRANSPORT SHAPE (Gurdas): the #586 spike edge.ts (acp-edge-transport) is PRE-ROUTER — it creates FiregridStandaloneLive + calls client-sdk methods DIRECTLY. The production transport must be HOST-PLANE CONFIG: RuntimeKernelLive + HostChannelRouterLive + AcpStdioEdgeLive, with ACP methods mapping to router.dispatch over protocol channel ROUTES. Do NOT build another special ACP entrypoint around client-sdk internals — that is what unlocks pluggable edge transports cleanly. SEQUENCING: keep pressure on tf-9x11 (router) + tf-1r3h (sync/async) through merge, THEN immediately route tf-csr0.

cutover follow-ups filed for deferred ACP methods: tf-8aw5 cancel primitive, tf-rqyh load/resume/close lifecycle primitives, tf-msqu authenticate primitive. Current edge fails unsupported methods explicitly and does not advertise load/resume/close.

task-exit.sh excludes .beads/ from git add -A (fixes the .beads/ commit conflict class — tf-482w/PR #531 root cause)
description: task-exit.sh:42 runs git add -A which sweeps the lane worktree's local br-update mutations to .beads/issues.jsonl into the lane commit. Those then collide non-patch-equivalently with cron-pushed origin/main .beads/ updates on rebase. The script header at line 5 already documents the INTENT — 'Does NOT push .beads/issues.jsonl — that is the canonical beads-sync's job' — but the implementation did not enforce it. Concrete recent fallout: PR #531 (tf-482w finding) had to be closed-as-superseded because it carried stale .beads/ state; the finding was lifted to main directly. Fix: replace 'git add -A' with 'git add -A -- :!.beads/' (git pathspec exclude). Lane work commits as before; the .beads/ working-tree mutations stay local and never get pushed. The cron remains the canonical pusher of .beads/issues.jsonl per existing architecture.
close_reason: DONE: PR #536 merged at 89617f929 — task-exit.sh now excludes .beads/ from git add -A (pathspec :!.beads/) on both the status-porcelain gate and the git-add line. Eliminates the .beads/ commit conflict class that forced PR #531 (tf-482w) closeout. Pre-task-exit .beads/ hygiene workaround is no longer required for lanes that have rebased onto main >= 89617f929.

title: runtime acp codec drops JSON-RPC error.message (acpPromise->codecError) — halts undiagnosable from trace
description: Proven in tf-7dq/#395: @agentclientprotocol/sdk RequestError consumed by the runtime ACP codec (acpPromise -> codecError(op,message,cause)) drops error.message; only errorKind:unknown survives to the trace artifact, making external/model halts undiagnosable. Scope: runtime agent-event-pipeline acp codec error.message (and/or child stderr) propagation into codecError. Substrate-boundary review-scoped — do NOT bundle into a sim PR.
close_reason: completed: sidecar/tf-ds2-codec-toolarg-error-propagation merged as #403 (verified against gh merged-PR state).

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: ACP codec tool-arg + JSON-RPC error propagation DELIVERED (substrate-boundary, additive, observability-only, no behavior change). Root cause from #398: (1) ACP agents stream real tool args via tool_call_update.rawInput but the codec collapsed every update to opaque status -> observedToolInputs={}; (2) codecError kept only the static op message, JSON-RPC error.message buried in cause -> agentError generic not real reason. Fix: jsonRpcErrorMessage(cause) extracts message+code+data; codecError composes '<op>: <underlying>' only when present (cause unchanged); ACP prompt-failure emits recoverableError(error.message,...) so trace agentError carries the real reason; tool_call_update with rawInput now also emits a ToolUse with the real args (observation-only — runtime-context workflow skips tool executor for acp protocol, ACP tool calls are provider-executed; no-rawInput updates still flow as status). Validated: runtime typecheck green; ACP codec 11/11; runtime 117/117; host-sdk 103/103 (incl ACP tool_call observation + PermissionRequest block/resume) in isolation x2; full gate green incl lint:effect-quality ratchet OK. turbo-run host-sdk#test failure = pre-existing concurrency flake (host-sdk 103/103 exit 0 isolated, same as #397), unrelated — reported not papered. Artifact docs/research/tf-ds2-codec-toolarg-error-propagation.FINDING.md. Coordinator holds gate; NO self-merge.

Also fix task-reap.sh:23 (JSONL="$RR/.beads/issues.jsonl" reads the inert in-repo jsonl — point to canonical). comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: VERDICT: PATCHABLE — no broad engine architecture. F3 wakeup already exists: engine.resume(workflow,executionId) [public @effect/workflow WorkflowEngine.d.ts:41; engine-runtime.ts:175,343] re-runs a suspended body WITHOUT a deferred (deferredDone = record-row + resume, engine-runtime.ts:431-457; interrupt also calls resume), and Workflow.suspend(instance) [public, Workflow.d.ts:353; used by DurableDeferred.await but not tied to deferreds] lets a body suspend on table-row-absent. Draft PR #651 adds a production-engine test proving: workflow point-reads a workflow-owned DurableTable, Workflow.suspend()s when absent; inputs.insert + engine.resume completes it; ZERO deferred rows (no mailbox), no polling. 18/18 file tests pass, typecheck+eslint green. NOT STOP. Production body cutover stays gated on bead A (atomic append) + tf-vrz6 Stage A (input table). Optional thin signalRuntimeContextInput wrapper over engine.resume is unnecessary.

title: main CI: Effect diagnostics check pre-existing red — blocks --error flip on tf-2y01

title: Runtime input: atomic append and point-addressing primitive
description: Prerequisite bead from SDD_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_WORKFLOW_INPUT_TABLE_CUTOVER transactional deletion bead #1. Provide the point-addressed next-input read path or indexed lookup for (contextId, sequence), and provide an atomic appendRuntimeContextWorkflowInput operation or equivalent transaction/conditional update that atomically checks inputId idempotency, reserves the next sequence, writes the input row, writes the idempotency row, and advances nextInputSequenceToAssign. Prove distinct concurrent producers cannot allocate the same sequence. Blocks source-of-truth channel writes to workflow-owned input table. STOP if this requires a new DurableTable transaction/conditional-update substrate SDD before implementation.
notes: STOP/report: atomic append cannot be implemented with current DurableTable per-key insert-if-absent + unconditional upsert surface. Blocked on tf-i05u: DurableTable conditional update/CAS primitive. Point-addressing can remain in Stage A; atomic sequence reservation needs CAS/substrate work.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED as written by PR #653 / tf-87vj: atomic append/write-time sequence reservation removed. Point-addressing survives as workflow-assigned ordinal reads inside tf-vrz6/P3 cursor work.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text:
STOP — atomic append requires a NEW DurableTable conditional-update/transaction substrate primitive (SDD-worthy). Point-addressing is patchable trivially (no new primitive). Source-grounded.

SUBSTRATE READ (packages/effect-durable-operators/src/DurableTable.ts + effect-durable-streams Writer.ts/Producer.ts):
The ONLY atomic conditional write DurableTable offers is per-PRIMARY-KEY insert-if-absent. insertOrGet (DurableTable.ts:761) -> appendInsertWithPrimaryKeyFence (504) -> DurableStream.appendWithProducer with producerId='durable-table:<type>:<hex(key)>', producerEpoch:0, producerSeq:0. That is a Kafka-style idempotent-producer fence keyed per row: first append for a key = Appended(Inserted); replay of same (producerId,epoch,seq) = Duplicate(Found).
- upsert (DurableTable.ts:378) and delete = UNCONDITIONAL stream.append (no fence, overwrite). NO compare-and-set on any field.
- No compareAndSwap / conditionalUpsert / expectedRevision / appendBatch / transaction anywhere in either durable package. Producer.ts:209 comment confirms the producer is single-active-drain-fiber 'so no CAS is needed' — i.e. concurrent multi-writer CAS is explicitly NOT a design goal.

WHY insert-if-absent CANNOT build atomic appendRuntimeContextWorkflowInput:
The SDD primitive must atomically bind TWO invariants — (a) inputId idempotency and (b) dense unique sequence allocation. insert-if-absent gives atomicity for exactly ONE key, so it can arbitrate ONE invariant, not the joint pair.
- Sequence-as-arbiter (probe inputs.insertOrGet(ctx/S) upward): gives collision-free dense sequences, BUT concurrent same-inputId both probe and win DIFFERENT seqs => inputId gets 2 sequences => idempotency VIOLATED.
- inputId-as-arbiter (inputIds.insertOrGet(inputId)): gives idempotency, BUT carries no atomically-known sequence; two distinct inputIds reading the same cursor claim the same seq => sequence collision, or two-phase write leaves orphan/phantom input rows on the crash window (workflow point-reads the phantom).
Either way you get phantom rows / gaps / dup sequences under concurrency or crash. This is the classic 'needs an atomic counter or CAS or transaction' result.

The low-level producer protocol DOES have monotonic-seq-at-expected-position (SequenceGap{expectedSeq}, Writer.ts:100-107) that could in principle back an atomic counter, BUT: (1) it is NOT exposed through DurableTable; (2) repurposing producerSeq as a domain counter conflicts with DurableTable's per-key fence which already pins producerSeq=0; (3) it is single-active-producer/epoch-fenced, not concurrent CAS (Producer.ts:209); (4) even granting a counter, it still does not bind idempotency+sequence jointly => transaction still required.

VERDICT by prerequisite:
- Prereq #1 point-addressed next input: PATCHABLE, no new primitive. inputKey =  string primaryKey + existing get(key) point read. Belongs in the Stage A table schema (tf-vrz6), nothing to build in the substrate. Not shipping it alone here (half-ship; no consumer until the table exists).
- Prereq #2 atomic appendRuntimeContextWorkflowInput: STOP. Needs a new DurableTable substrate primitive.

PROPOSED SDD BOUNDARY (new substrate SDD, smallest viable):
Add ONE atomic conditional-update/CAS primitive to DurableTable — collection.compareAndSet(key, expectedRevision|expectedAbsent, nextRow) — implemented via a revision-fenced conditional append (or by exposing the producer expected-seq protocol per row). This directly enables the SDD_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_WORKFLOW_INPUT_TABLE_CUTOVER 'conditional context-row compare/update with insert rollback semantics' path: CAS-advance contexts.nextInputSequenceToAssign (N->N+1 iff currently N) to reserve a sequence, then point-insert inputs[ctx/seq] (insert-if-absent), then inputIds.insertOrGet for idempotency convergence; loser of the CAS retries. Alternative heavier shape = a multi-row all-or-nothing DurableTable transaction (touches State-Protocol txid coordination broadly) — NOT recommended as the first step. Recommend the single CAS primitive.
Chain: tf-9rpy <- tf-vrz6 <- tf-eta6 <- NEW substrate-CAS bead/SDD.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: Correction (shell ate the interpolation in prior comment): Prereq #1 inputKey is the composite string primary key contextId + "/" + sequence (e.g. ctxAbc/0, ctxAbc/1). Workflow reads next input via existing get(inputKey) point read; no new substrate primitive needed for #1.

title: Phase 0 Wave-2A: permission-stream consumption sim — can permission-response intents fold into the stream-zip body, or does the body need a separate permission stream?
description:
Per SDD_FIREGRID_AGGRESSIVE_ONE_SUBSTRATE_SWAPOVER.md §Phase 0 Wave-2A. Branch with SDD: codex/tf-auuv-aggressive-substrate-swap (commit e6ae05142).

QUESTION: Can permission-response intents be consumed as ordinary runtime input stream events in the stream-zip body, or does the body need a separate permission stream?

WORK:
- Build a tiny-firegrid stream-zip body sim that triggers a permission request mid-flight
- Drive a permission response
- Prove ONE of these shapes:

VERDICT (record explicit one in FINDING doc):
- GREEN-zip-2: widened runtimeInputStream carries permission responses
- GREEN-zip-3: body uses a third permission stream alongside inputs and outputs
- RED-needs-redesign: permission consumption should not be changed in Phase 1

PHASE 1 EFFECT (drives Lane 1 implementation shape):
- GREEN-zip-2 → Lane 1 folds permission responses into runtime input stream
- GREEN-zip-3 → Lane 1 implements three-stream body shape
- RED-needs-redesign → Lane 1 preserves current observable permission behavior through output/event handler; defers approval-channelization to Phase 2 Lane 5

BOUND: 1-hour maximum. Sim-only or source-read; NOT research. The Phase 1 fan-out is gated on your verdict (only Lane 1 affected if RED).

DELIVERABLE: docs/research/tf-<bead>-phase0-wave-2a-permission-stream.FINDING.md with explicit VERDICT line + trace evidence + sim code if built.
close_reason: DECIDED: shipped via PR #465 (merge 82b4167d92). VERDICT: GREEN-zip-2-routing-confirmed; CORRELATION-NEEDS-WAVE-2C-RESOLUTION. PermissionRequest in runtimeOutputStream (output seq 7); permission response in runtimeInputStream (input seq 1). Two-stream INV-1 shape suffices for permission routing. Correlation/pairing nuance deferred to Wave-2C (zipLatest semantics) — NOT a Phase-1 Lane 1 routing gap; correlation is a separate body-shape concern. Phase-1 Lane 1 (tf-vnc8) permission-handling LOCKED to GREEN-zip-2 per OLA's Phase-1-effect mapping.

title: Prompt channel egress-return reconciliation (HostPrompt/SessionPrompt) — DECISION + impl
description: DECISION-GRADE. tf-aago (PR #560) deferred the prompt-method channel collapse. Gap: lane 3's HostPrompt/SessionPrompt are EGRESS channels (append->void), but client firegrid.prompt/session.prompt return the stored RuntimeInputIntentRow and tests assert returned===stored (createdAt + _otel). Egress-void can't preserve that. DECISION needed: (a) add an append-that-returns-the-row egress variant (Live Layer does insert + returns the row; preserves client contract), OR (b) change client prompt return contract to an ack (breaking-ish client API change), OR (c) model prompt as a CALLABLE channel (request->response where response=stored row) instead of egress. Recommend (a) — least disruptive, preserves the client contract, the Live Layer already has the inserted row. Until decided, prompt stays on appendRuntimeInputIntent (the last direct-write residual; relevant to tf-cyet). Owner decision: gary/Gurdas (channel-contract shape).
close_reason: DONE: PR #582 merged fda802c3a. Prompt egress-with-receipt per ratified option (a): EgressChannel<…,RuntimeInputIntentRow> returns the durable acceptance receipt; HostPrompt/SessionPrompt stay direction=egress; no CallableChannel conversion; client returned===stored tests preserved.

comments.author: gary
comments.text: DECISION: choose option (a), but phrase it precisely as egress-with-receipt, not callable and not ack-only. HostPrompt/SessionPrompt remain EgressChannel direction because prompt is a unilateral durable append, not request/response RPC. The client return value is a producer-side durable acceptance receipt: the stored RuntimeInputIntentRow from insertOrGet. Implement the smallest channel-core extension needed to let the egress binding return a receipt while preserving public send semantics for agents. Preferred shape: add a receipt generic/default to AppendTargetBinding/EgressChannel, e.g. append(payload): Effect<Receipt, ...> with Receipt defaulting to void, or a narrowly named Recorded/Accepted egress helper if that is less invasive. Public client prompt/session.prompt should call the channel binding and return the stored RuntimeInputIntentRow exactly as today. Agent-tool send/session_prompt may continue returning its existing tool output/ok shape and may discard the receipt. Do NOT model prompt as CallableChannel; that would incorrectly imply a runtime/application response and would route this through request/completion semantics. Do NOT change client prompt to ack-only in this slice; preserving returned===stored tests is part of the migration constraint. Acceptance: direct appendRuntimeInputIntent residual in client-sdk prompt paths is removed; HostPrompt/SessionPrompt channel bindings perform insertOrGet and return Found row or stamped row; existing client tests asserting stored row/createdAt/_otel continue to pass; egress direction metadata remains egress.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: DECIDED (Gurdas/Gary, 2026-05-21): option (a) as EGRESS-WITH-RECEIPT — NOT callable, NOT ack-only. HostPrompt/SessionPrompt stay EgressChannel direction (prompt = unilateral durable append, not RPC). Client return value = producer-side durable ACCEPTANCE RECEIPT (the stored RuntimeInputIntentRow from insertOrGet), not an application response. Shape: smallest channel-core extension letting egress append return a receipt while preserving public send semantics — add a Receipt generic/default to AppendTargetBinding/EgressChannel (append(payload): Effect<Receipt,...>, Receipt defaults to void), OR a narrowly-named recorded/accepted egress helper if less invasive. HostPrompt/SessionPrompt bindings do insertOrGet + return Found/stamped row. client-sdk prompt/session.prompt call the binding + return RuntimeInputIntentRow exactly as today. agent tool send/session_prompt keeps its ok shape, discards the receipt. ACCEPTANCE: direct appendRuntimeInputIntent residual removed from client-sdk prompt paths; HostPrompt/SessionPrompt metadata still direction=egress; existing returned===stored/createdAt/_otel tests pass; NO CallableChannel conversion. Gurdas relayed directly to L3.
