comments.text: POST-PR-#434 FIX SHAPE: validated against the wait-pre-attach-roundtrip reproducer. Apply Stream.tap + per-chunk Effect.withSpan pattern to row-write sites in packages/host-sdk/src/host/runtime-context-workflow-core.ts — specifically the firegrid.runtime_context.workflow.output.handle scope and similar long-lived producer scopes. #434 validated the pattern at ONE site as a side effect of the wire-attrs work. Estimated ~3 sites in that file. Verification per fix: re-run wait-pre-attach-roundtrip and check orphan-parent count on wait_router.complete_match drops from ~59% toward 0.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: MEASURED: wait-pre-attach-roundtrip sim, my baseline = 123/136 (90.4%) complete_match orphan parents. Applied dispatch fix (Stream.tap+per-chunk Effect.withSpan at 4 host-sdk Stream.withSpan sites: per-context-runtime-output.ts after()+forContext(), runtime-context-engine-registry.ts intent dispatcher, codec-adapter.ts codec.outputs). Result = 123/136 (90.4%) UNCHANGED — fix had zero impact on this metric. ROOT CAUSE actually located: the 11 unique orphan parent IDs are unexported attach_source spans (per-wait, long-lived Stream.withSpan) created in packages/runtime/src/durable-tools/internal/wait-router.ts:231. wait_router subscribes per-wait, attach_source wraps the source stream for the wait lifetime, span never closes before trace export. complete_match (in same file) inherits attach_source as ambient parent when row._otel is absent. Row._otel IS absent because RuntimeEventRow has no _otel field (tf-3tg only added _otel to control-request/intent/wait/execution rows; the output table was not stamped). FIX OPTIONS: (A) wait-router.ts attach_source -> Stream.tap per-row + drop the Stream.withSpan parent (in packages/runtime, explicitly OUT of scope per dispatch), (B) add _otel to RuntimeEventRow in packages/protocol + stampRowOtel inside per-context-runtime-output.ts appendEventRow (short-lived per-write producer span already exists at line 71). Option B is the host-sdk-shaped surface fix and is consistent with tf-3tg architecture. Will proceed with B unless redirected. My current host-sdk Stream.tap changes are structurally fine but immaterial to this metric — happy to keep, drop, or hold pending direction.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: CONFIRMED ROOT CAUSE: wait-pre-attach-roundtrip trace has 14 unique wait.names with complete_match spans but only 3 `firegrid.durable_tools.wait_router.attach_wait` spans exported. The 11 missing attach_wait spans (per-wait, long-lived Effect.withSpan in packages/runtime/src/durable-tools/internal/wait-router.ts:261) ARE the orphan parents. Forked-scope subscriptions never naturally complete; their spans never end before trace export. SECONDARY: even when row._otel exists on RuntimeEventRow, the runtime-side decoder `runtimeAgentOutputObservationFromRow` (packages/runtime/src/agent-event-pipeline/events/output.ts:45) STRIPS _otel when transforming RuntimeEventRow -> RuntimeAgentOutputObservation, so wait_router never sees row._otel on AgentOutput* sources. MEASUREMENTS: baseline 123/136 (90.4%); after Stream.tap at 4 host-sdk Stream.withSpan sites: 123/136 (90.4% unchanged); after additionally adding _otel to RuntimeEventRow/RuntimeLogLineRow/RuntimeRunEventRow + stampRowOtel at host write sites: 111/121 (91.7% — runtime decoder strips _otel, fix inert). DECISION: reverting the Stream.tap changes (no measured impact, adds span noise). KEEPING the _otel-on-row schema + stamping changes (shape-aligned with tf-3tg, forward-looking — activates when a runtime-side PR propagates _otel through the observation decoder OR shortens attach_wait lifetime). title: engine recycle is suspend/resume, not cancel/fail — workflow-engine semantics fix for engine-recycle of in-flight non-user-cancelled primitives
description:
From Gurdas's P0 dispatch 2026-05-20 (post tf-ovzr PR #469 RED finding).

PROBLEM:
PR #469 / tf-ovzr proved: Activity(Stream.runHead) inside DurableDeferred.raceAll persists an INTERRUPTED race deferred on engine recycle and poisons gen-2 replay. The exact INV-2 WaitForWorkflow body shape fails gen-2 decode ('Expected Cause<never>, actual Interrupt') after scoped engine bounce, before match/timeout can win. deadline_preserved=true for DurableClock so the clock side is fine; the race deferred is the failure point.

SEMANTIC FIX:
Engine recycle is suspend/resume, NOT cancel/fail. For recycle of a non-user-interrupted in-flight workflow primitive, the engine must propagate Workflow.Suspended, not an interrupt/failure result.

PRIMARY INSPECTION POINT (first):
- packages/runtime/src/workflow-engine/internal/engine-runtime.ts:activityExecute

FIX SHAPE:
- For recycle of a non-user-interrupted in-flight Activity, return new Workflow.Suspended({}) WITHOUT writing an Activity result.
- Must DISTINGUISH engine recycle from user-requested workflow cancellation.
- Only RECYCLE becomes Suspended.
- Genuine cancellation must remain cancellation — or dead workflows come back on the next engine generation.

ACCEPTANCE (5 items):
1. PR #469 sim flips from RED-new-shape-replay-gap to GREEN.
2. INV-2 no-bounce WaitForWorkflow still passes (regression check; PR #458 on main).
3. INV-3 old-shape restart replay still passes (regression check; PR #457 on main).
4. A failing Activity with a typed/domain error still writes/propagates that error, not Suspended (negative test — typed errors must not be eaten by recycle semantics).
5. A user-cancelled workflow does NOT resume after engine generation restart (negative test — recycle vs cancel distinction must hold).

CONSTRAINT: Do NOT widen the race error schema as the fix. The fix is in the engine's recycle path, not in the user-facing error surface.

BLOCKS: Phase 1 Lanes 2, 3, 4.
- Phase-1 Lane 2 (tf-xw0w WaitForWorkflow cutover) — halted
- Phase-1 Lane 3 (tf-pcde type/env cleanup) — needs halt
- Phase-1 Lane 4 (tf-6d4y delete durable-tools) — gated on fan-in anyway

Phase-1 Lane 1 (tf-vnc8 runtime-context body, merge-based) and Phase-1 Lane 5 (tf-swr8 tests/sim acceptance) can proceed but inherit the same invariant.

CROSS-REFS:
- tf-ovzr PR #469: the RED finding that surfaced this
- tf-2kel PR #458 (INV-2): no-bounce WaitForWorkflow validation, must stay GREEN
- tf-r5e3 PR #457 (INV-3): old-shape restart replay validation, must stay GREEN

ESTIMATED: substantial — substrate semantics work + 5 acceptance tests including 2 negative cases.
close_reason: DECIDED: shipped via PR #470 (merge 1ca9e46968). Engine-recycle now suspend/resume semantics: pure interrupts during recycle become Workflow.Suspended without writing Activity result; explicit cancellation remains terminal; typed Activity failures still propagate as domain errors. All 5 acceptance items met per Lane 3 (PR #469 sim flips RED→GREEN, INV-2 + INV-3 regressions GREEN, negative tests for typed errors + user-cancel-no-resume both pass). Unblocks Phase 1 Lanes 2 (tf-xw0w), 3 (tf-pcde), 4 (tf-6d4y).

title: Reconciler-as-workflows: collapse packages/host-sdk/src/host/control-request-reconciler.ts onto DurableStreamsWorkflowEngine — second implicit engine eliminated, multi-context activation unblocked structurally
description:
Per Gurdas's P0 architecture-review follow-on 2026-05-20.

ARCHITECTURE FINDING: packages/host-sdk/src/host/control-request-reconciler.ts is effectively a SECOND implicit durable workflow engine for the host control plane:
- poll request rows → claim → do work → write completion
- claim leases, abandon timeouts
- first-writer-wins multi-host semantics
- 5s polling loops

It OVERLAPS HEAVILY with the explicit DurableStreamsWorkflowEngine that Firegrid already owns and that Phase 1 collapses durable-tools onto.

PROPOSAL — Reconciler-as-workflows:
- RuntimeContextProvisionWorkflow(contextId, runtime)
- RuntimeStartWorkflow(contextId)
- RuntimeLifecycleWorkflow(contextId, kind)

LONG-TERM TARGET (Path A): most of control-request-reconciler.ts EVAPORATES. Remaining surface = workflow registration + an SDK-compat row-to-workflow dispatch shim (if compatibility required).

What goes away:
- controlRequestClaims / controlRequestCompletions tables (engine owns Exit semantics)
- winClaim / acquire / abandon machinery (engine schedules)
- 5s polling loops (engine event-driven)
- hand-rolled completion spans (engine instrumentation)
- fan-out Effect.forEach patterns (engine handles concurrency per-execution)

PATH C HYBRID (acceptable fallback if streamWait/engine-native primitives not ready): keep client-facing durable request rows; translate internally to engine.execute; move claims/completions/retries into engine semantics.

GATES (must hold before dispatch):
- ✅ PR #470 / tf-gyxc engine-recycle invariant landed (engine recycle = suspend/resume, not cancel/fail)
- ⏳ Phase 1 Lane 2 (tf-xw0w) WaitForWorkflow cutover settled (one-substrate proven end-to-end)
- ⚠ Preferably engine-native primitive track / streamWait direction known (tf-0mt5 contingency-trigger status)
  - If streamWait not ready → Path C hybrid acceptable

RESOLVES STRUCTURALLY:
- INV-5 multi-context activation gap (tf-tg8q PR #460 finding) — reconciler's serialized fan-out around start requests; under engine workflow dispatch each start request is an independent execution scheduled by the engine. Multi-context activation no longer constrained by reconciler loop shape.
- tf-ma6c (current interim fix raising Effect.forEach concurrency / fork-and-forget claimAndRun) becomes OBSOLETE — superseded by this structural collapse.

ACCEPTANCE SKETCH:
1. Identify which control request rows remain public SDK compatibility vs internal coordination
2. Prove context provision/start/lifecycle requests can run as workflow executions
3. Prove multi-context start concurrency no longer bottlenecks on reconciler fan-out
4. Preserve lifecycle terminal evidence emission (RuntimeRun + agent-event Terminated rows) as workflow side effects
5. Delete or make obsolete claim/completion tables only after compatibility decision
6. Add tiny-firegrid or host-sdk sim/test evidence tying this to INV-5

STRATEGIC FRAMING: Standalone-value engine-collapse target, not just a bug workaround. Firegrid currently has the explicit workflow engine PLUS this implicit reconciler engine. This follow-on UNIFIES them. One of the highest-leverage remaining simplifications after durable-tools collapse.

DISPATCH STATUS: NOT yet dispatched. Queued post-Phase-1-fan-in (specifically post-tf-xw0w Lane 2 settling). Sequence with care vs streamWait/contingency track.

DurableTable: conditional update/CAS primitive for workflow input append
description: Substrate prerequisite surfaced by tf-eta6. DurableTable currently exposes per-primary-key insert-if-absent, unconditional upsert/delete, and no compareAndSet/expectedRevision/transaction primitive. RuntimeContext workflow-owned input append needs to atomically reserve/advance contexts.nextInputSequenceToAssign so distinct producers cannot allocate the same sequence while inputId idempotency converges. Design and implement the smallest DurableTable conditional update/CAS primitive, e.g. collection.compareAndSet(key, expectedRevision|expectedAbsent, nextRow), or STOP with the exact lower-level state-protocol change required. Acceptance: focused concurrent writers test proves only one CAS winner and losers retry/observe expected state; no broad multi-row transaction unless explicitly justified by SDD.
notes: STOP (comment 2026-05-22): coherent in-place CAS not bounded-patchable. Only server-side conditional mechanism = idempotent-producer protocol, which (1) silently drops the losing payload for concurrent distinct writers (returns 204 Duplicate = false success), (2) treats SequenceGap as terminal not retry, (3) uses epoch to fence OUT concurrency. Production server is external -> a true conditional-append endpoint = lower-level state-protocol SDD. Client-side versioned-key fallback is degenerate (not in-place CAS, no power beyond insert-if-absent). Deeper: even a CAS would not unblock tf-eta6 (3-row joint invariant needs multi-row txn or read-time convergence). Root: cutover SDD chose SERVER-allocated dense sequence (nextInputSequenceToAssign), in tension with target reference "driver supplies sequence; do not allocate server-side." RECOMMEND bouncing design to tf-vrz6 cutover SDD (fork P1 server-allocate / P2 producer-supply / P3 workflow-assign-on-consume; P3 best-aligned), NOT building substrate transactions.
close_reason: SUPERSEDED by PR #653 / tf-87vj: P3 workflow-assigned input order removes the need for a DurableTable CAS primitive. STOP finding retained as evidence against server-side allocation.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text:
STOP — a coherent DurableTable conditional-update/CAS is NOT bounded-patchable; it requires a lower-level state-protocol change against the EXTERNAL durable-streams server (a named STOP condition). Source-grounded across effect-durable-streams Writer.ts/Producer.ts/protocol + conformance tests.

WHAT EXISTS (the only server-side conditional mechanism): the idempotent-producer protocol (§5.2.1). Per (producerId, epoch) the server enforces strictly monotonic expected seq: append at expected -> 200 Appended; replay of an already-consumed seq -> 204 Duplicate; out-of-order -> 409 SequenceGap{expectedSeq}; higher epoch fences zombies -> 403 StaleEpoch. DurableTable uses it in ONE shape only: producerId per-primary-key, epoch 0, seq 0 = one-shot insert-if-absent (insertOrGet). upsert/delete BYPASS it entirely (plain unconditional stream.append).

WHY IT CANNOT BE A CAS (3 independent blockers):
1. SILENT WRITE LOSS for concurrent distinct-payload writers. Two writers on the same (producerId, epoch=0) both send seq=N with different payloads: server accepts the first (200 at seq N), and returns the second 204 Duplicate{sameOffset} treating it as an idempotent REPLAY -> the loser BELIEVES it succeeded while its payload was dropped. A CAS loser must learn it lost; here it is told it won. The protocol assumes ONE logical producer retrying the SAME message, not competing writers.
2. PROTOCOL SEMANTICS ARE TERMINAL, NOT RETRY. Producer.ts:117-120 explicitly: 409 SequenceGap = invariant violation, "the caller cannot recover." Conformance test classified-producer-append.test.ts:114 pins SequenceGap as a typed error, not a retry signal. Reinterpreting it as recoverable CAS-loss is a state-protocol semantic change.
3. EPOCH = ANTI-CONCURRENCY. Epoch exists to FENCE OUT concurrent producers (zombie fencing; autoClaim bumps epoch on contention, Producer.ts:99-103). The protocol is designed to ELIMINATE the very concurrency a CAS must arbitrate. Producer.ts:209 confirms single-drain-fiber "so no CAS is needed."

The production durable-streams SERVER is EXTERNAL (only a conformance test-server is in-repo). A true in-place compareAndSet(key, expectedRevision, next) coherent with the existing get/upsert mutable-row model needs a NEW server conditional-append (expected-revision, payload-aware) endpoint = lower-level state-protocol SDD + external server change. STOP per dispatch.

CLIENT-SIDE FALLBACK IS DEGENERATE: insert-if-absent on revision-suffixed keys (key@rev) gives one-winner-per-step, but it is an append-only register, NOT coherent in-place CAS (get(key) cannot return latest; needs frontier-probing), and does NOT add power beyond the slot-claiming insert-if-absent tf-eta6 already analyzed.

DEEPER FINDING (re-routes the chain): even a perfect single-row CAS would NOT unblock tf-eta6. appendRuntimeContextWorkflowInput must jointly atomize a THREE-row invariant (inputIds idempotency-check + contexts cursor-advance + inputs insert). A single-row CAS cannot; that needs a multi-row transaction (the other named STOP) OR read-time idempotency convergence. The atomic-reservation requirement traces to the cutover SDD choosing SERVER-ALLOCATED dense sequences (contexts.nextInputSequenceToAssign), which is in DIRECT TENSION with SDD_TARGET_TINY_FIREGRID_ARCHITECTURE_REFERENCE: "the driver supplies sequence per session. The reference does not allocate sequences server-side; that would reintroduce coordination the table seam is trying to eliminate."

RECOMMENDATION: do NOT build a substrate CAS/transaction to support a coordination shape the target architecture rejects. Resolve at the SDD level first. The fork (an SDD decision, not a substrate primitive): (P1) server-allocated dense sequence -> needs multi-row txn + server conditional-append [heavy, external]; (P2) producer-supplied sequence per the target reference [eliminates atomic reservation; but production has MULTI-AUTHOR input - client/workflow/tool/system - so needs a per-context sequencing authority]; (P3) workflow-assigns-order-on-consume: producers insert-if-absent by inputId (point-addressable by inputId, NOT dense sequence), the workflow assigns processing order and owns the read cursor. P3 looks most aligned with the target seam and avoids both substrate transactions and server-side allocation, but it is a cutover-SDD design decision.

Chain: tf-9rpy <- tf-vrz6 <- tf-eta6 <- tf-i05u; tf-i05u recommends bouncing the design back to the tf-vrz6 cutover SDD (server-allocate vs producer-supply vs workflow-assign) rather than descending into substrate transaction work.

title: Rec-2: RuntimeToolUseExecutor contract sim — 3 adversarial cases (timeout mid-execution, cancel mid-flight by workflow scope close, duplicate toolUseId across activity retry) at the codec-substrate seam BEFORE Phase 1 Lane 2 swaps WaitForWorkflow in at exactly this contract
description:
Per OLA's pipeline-layer de-risk proposal 2026-05-20. SDD on main at 44ace8406.

CONTEXT: subscribers/runtime-tool-use-executor.ts is a 36-line Context.Tag declaring the codec-substrate seam: ToolUse event → activity dispatch → ToolResult back. Zero adversarial-case validation today. Phase 1 Lane 2 swaps WaitForWorkflow in at EXACTLY this seam — if the contract has latent holes, Lane 2 silently commits to them as production semantics.

THREE ADVERSARIAL SCENARIOS (as separate driver branches in one sim):
(a) Tool-use that times out mid-execution — workflow-side timeout vs. codec-side timeout — who fires the cancel?
(b) Tool-use cancelled mid-flight by workflow scope close — does the subprocess get the cancel? Does a stale ToolResult get rejected?
(c) Duplicate toolUseId across activity retry attempts — is the second invocation deduplicated, replayed, or treated as a new call?

SHAPE:
- New tiny-firegrid sim under packages/tiny-firegrid/src/simulations/runtime-tool-use-executor-contract/
- Construct a minimal RuntimeToolUseExecutor.layer(...) against the workflow engine
- Run the 3 scenarios as separate driver branches

VERDICTS (per scenario):
- GREEN-CONTRACT-COMPLETE
- SEAM-GAP-{timeout|cancel|dedup}: concrete contract gap found (cite source + record observed behavior)

NON-CONFLICT: sim is contract-level, does NOT modify host-sdk's executor implementation. Phase 1 Lane 2 changes the implementation; this sim validates it satisfies the contract.

ESTIMATED: ~3-4 hours.

DELIVERABLE: docs/research/tf-<bead>-rec2-runtime-tool-use-executor-contract.FINDING.md with explicit VERDICT per scenario + trace evidence + sim code.
close_reason: DECIDED: shipped via PR #468 (merge bed8665e91). Pipeline-layer de-risk for codec-substrate seam contract. Three adversarial scenarios validated. Phase-1 Lane 2 (tf-xw0w) now has documented contract semantics to preserve at the cutover seam.

title: TFIND-040: Client SDK lacks a per-event session observation surface
description:
Area: client-sdk / observations.

Status: FULLY DECIDED (Gurdas 2026-05-18) — Q1=A attach at SESSION level, Q2=live-tail-until-scope-close, Q3=subscribe-only-first, Q4=generic-stream-only. SDD #334 SDD_FIREGRID_SESSION_OBSERVATION_SURFACE.md. Impl ready to dispatch (queued behind keystone).

Triage: cat-1 (rubric anchor — real client consumer pattern: session.subscribe per-event; correctly SDD-first).

The substrate primitive already exists internally (waitForAgentOutputObservation = RuntimeOutputTable.events.rows() tail → filterMap → filter); subscribe is that pipeline minus Stream.runHead, yielding TFIND-030 typed RuntimeAgentOutputObservation. No substrate/protocol change; wait.forAgentOutput refactors to subscribe |> filter |> runHead (one code path, no drift).

Projection-scope clarification (Gurdas Q): the observation element RuntimeAgentOutputObservation is a protocol projection so SEMANTICS are plane-neutral and would carry — BUT TFIND-040 decided the CLIENT binding only; an agent-tool binding of subscribe is a SEPARATE, not-yet-made decision. Client impl MUST keep observation contract binding-neutral so a future symmetric agent-tool binding is not foreclosed.
close_reason: DECIDED: PR #334 merged as framing; remaining per-event/session observation surface concern is absorbed by One Substrate Primitive validation + surface hygiene gates. Re-open only as a narrow post-spike client subscribe/ergonomics task if still needed.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text: STALE-CHECK 2026-05-20 — this bead is pre-§6 arc (TFIND-040 from prior tooling). Substrate has evolved substantially since: #431 (observability batch with side propagation + queue context propagation), #434 (per-chunk Stream span attrs surfacing wire content as trace data), #435 (session.whenReady + projectionWait pattern). Does 'Client SDK lacks a per-event session observation surface' still apply? Concrete: session.wait.forAgentOutput + the projectionWait helper + per-chunk TextChunk Stream visibility may already cover this. Surface whether to close as obsoleted, re-scope to a current gap, or keep open with citation to specific missing capability.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text:
STALE-CHECK DISPOSITION (direct-source-verified on origin/main 0888d3a15): AFFIRM — gap is STILL REAL, keep open at "ready to implement" (SDD framing landed, impl never did). Evidence:

(1) packages/client-sdk/src/firegrid.ts:160 FiregridSessionHandle on current main exposes:
  - readonly whenReady (NEW since SDD — landed via #435 / tf-ozd)
  - readonly prompt, start, snapshot
  - readonly wait: { forAgentOutput, forPermissionRequest } — single-shot, internally Stream.runHead
  - readonly permissions: { respond }
  
NO `subscribe` / `observe` / `events` field. No per-event Stream<RuntimeAgentOutputObservation> exposed publicly anywhere on FiregridSessionHandle or FiregridSessionsClient.

(2) The internal helper `projectionWait` (packages/client-sdk/src/internal/projection-wait.ts) is `stream.pipe(Stream.filter(pred), Stream.runHead, Effect.asVoid)` — a SINGLE-SHOT match-and-return Effect, NOT a continuous Stream. Used internally by session.whenReady + wait.* impls. Does not export, does not return a Stream. The stale-check hypothesis that projectionWait + session.whenReady + per-chunk TextChunk Stream visibility "may already cover this" is REFUTED — those are single-shot wait primitives, the gap is the absence of a continuous Stream<RuntimeAgentOutputObservation> surface.

(3) SDD #334 (docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_SESSION_OBSERVATION_SURFACE.md) merged 2026-05-19 as docs-only ("framing, no code"). The SDD itself §1 says: "There is no continuous per-event session stream. Consumers that want incremental agent output must poll snapshot() or open RuntimeOutputTable/RuntimeControlPlaneTable substrate tables directly... Both break the client abstraction." That framing is still accurate against current main.

(4) PR #436s codex sim observer-fiber pattern (RuntimeAgentOutputEvents from @firegrid/runtime/runtime-output, fired from HOST scope) is a HOST-side substrate-level route — it works for sims/hosts (allowed to consume @firegrid/runtime) but is NOT a fill for the CLIENT gap (a public client consumer reaching into @firegrid/runtime would violate the client abstraction the SDD §1 calls out).

NET: TFIND-040 fully decided (Q1=A session-attach, Q2=live-tail-until-scope-close, Q3=subscribe-only-first, Q4=generic-stream-only); SDD #334 merged; impl was queued behind keystone tf-c68 and remains unstarted. Recommendation: keep open as "ready to dispatch impl" — the impl spec is one substrate-internal pipeline tweak (`waitForAgentOutputObservation` minus terminal `Stream.runHead`) exposed as `session.subscribe()`. title: Phase-3 channel-restructure: ChannelRegistry-as-service → typed Tag+Layer per channel (generic facades over durable operator streams)
description:
Per Gurdas's SDD architecture-tightening series (main commits e81bd6ba4, 92ec6a295, d00e12cf7).

PRINCIPLE: Channels are generic typed facades over durable operator streams. NOT a parallel data model (registry-as-service). NOT workflow handles. The application model is Effect Context.Tag + Layer per channel — never a Registry object that hands out things.

CANONICAL SHAPE (per SDD_FIREGRID_AGENT_BODY_PLAN.md, d00e12cf7):

class LinearWebhookChannel extends Context.Tag('firegrid/channels/linear.webhook')<
  LinearWebhookChannel,
  IngressChannel<LinearWebhook>
>() {}

const LinearWebhookChannelLive = Layer.effect(
  LinearWebhookChannel,
  Effect.gen(function*() {
    const table = yield* LinearWebhookTable        // private infra
    return makeIngressChannel(table.rows())        // ProjectionStream<LinearWebhook>
  })
)

Below session plane (hidden from agent/app code):
- DurableTable / ProjectionStream / CDC mechanics / collection names
- Workflow engine / execution ids / scoped engines
- String lookup tables (allowed ONLY at MCP/protocol-edge adapter)

Above session plane (what agent/app code sees):
- Named typed channels via Effect Context.Tag
- Layer-provided capabilities (yield* SomeChannelTag)
- wait_for(channel-string, { match, timeoutMs }) verb (string→Tag resolution at protocol boundary)

WHAT TO RESTRUCTURE:

(1) tf-lawq ChannelRegistry-service shape → REMOVE the ChannelRegistry service. Each channel becomes its own Tag.
(2) tf-bhv9 state.changes Slice C.3 → typed Tag generic over collection row type, Layer wraps DurableTable.rows()
(3) tf-fmwg event(name) Slice C.2 → typed Tag generic over event payload, Layer wraps event-stream substrate
(4) tf-v8i4 approval Slice C.4 → typed CallableChannel<ApprovalRequest, ApprovalResponse> Tag
(5) tf-mybr dm/notification Slice C.5 → typed Tag pair per handle
(6) tf-xl6k session.log Slice C.6 → typed EgressChannel<LogRow> Tag
(7) tf-gnp1 session.self.* Slice C.1 → typed Tag pair for lifecycle/checkpoint
(8) tf-dlaa wait_for(channel) tool dispatch → restructure to resolve string → Tag at protocol-edge adapter; yield* TagSpecific to consume
(9) tf-w88b channel metadata in MCP tools/list → derive from registered Tags at host composition time (not from a Registry instance)

WEBHOOK-AS-CHANNEL GENERALIZATION:
Any inbound source (webhooks, CDC streams, event tables, agent-emitted events, lifecycle signals) gets the SAME agent-facing shape: wait_for(channel, match, timeoutMs). Diversity lives in Layer compositions at host startup, not in agent vocabulary.

ACCEPTANCE:
- ChannelRegistry service tag removed
- Each Phase-2 channel slice exposes its own typed Context.Tag + Layer
- Host composition merges all channel Layers; no central registry instance exists
- MCP-edge adapter does string → Tag resolution; produces a typed Effect that yields the Tag's capabilities
- Application/business/tool code receives typed channel capabilities via Layer; NEVER a registry, never workflow handles, never substrate coordinates
- rg pattern: zero hits for direct ChannelRegistry imports outside the MCP-edge adapter

NOT YET DISPATCHED. Queued post-Phase-1-fan-in. This is the TRUE body-plan surface; current Phase-2 channel shape is registry-scaffold that lands functionality but with anti-Effect-idiom abstraction layer.

Cross-refs:
- d00e12cf7 (SDD: frame channels over durable operators)
- 92ec6a295 (SDD: align channels with effect layers)
- e81bd6ba4 (SDD: codify channel workflow boundary)
- tf-ws2x (runtime-context-engine-registry collapse — analogous Layer-only direction)
- tf-0mt5 (engine-native primitives escape hatch — informs streamWait/streamWaitAny shape)
close_reason: DECIDED: completed by PR #502; ChannelRegistry service replaced by ChannelInventory/per-channel Tags + Layer composition. Remaining channel unification work is now tracked under the One Substrate Primitive validation epic.

comments.author: coordinator
comments.text:
REFERENCE EXAMPLE LANDED 2026-05-20 (PR #494, merge a89c3658b): tf-ws2x successfully collapsed RuntimeContextEngineRegistry to Layer-provided services. This is the CANONICAL PATTERN you'll mirror for channels:

- Public registry/Tag handing out things → DELETED
- Each capability becomes its own Effect Context.Tag with a Layer providing it
- One host-scoped underlying primitive (engine in tf-ws2x's case; will be channel-Layer compositions in your case)
- Event-driven shapes preserved (no regression to polling/Registry-yielding)
- Regression coverage: no registry imports + multi-context still works

For tf-kddg the analogous pattern is:
- ChannelRegistry service → DELETED
- Each channel becomes its own Context.Tag<XChannel, IngressChannel<Row>> (or Egress/Callable)
- Channel-specific Layer.effect(XChannel, makeXChannelImpl) compositions
- Tool dispatch yields the Tag directly (yield* XChannel) rather than registry.require
- MCP-edge string→Tag adapter is the only allowed lookup; lives strictly at protocol boundary

Tf-kddg dispatched when capacity + sequencing aligns (post-tf-xw0w rework).

This is implementation, not SDD drafting; body-plan SDD direction is settled across e81bd6ba4 + 92ec6a295 + d00e12cf7 + aeaf5f6af. Session-tier channels (SessionLog, SessionSelfLifecycle, SessionSelfCheckpoint) and generic channel KINDS (event, state.changes, human, approval, etc.) are correct to own in host-sdk. Sent corrected steer to Lane 3.

comments.author: codex
comments.text:
Completed spec-only Stage A cutover plan. Draft PR: https://github.com/gurdasnijor/firegrid/pull/617

Output: docs/sdds/SDD_RUNTIME_CONTEXT_WORKFLOW_INPUT_TABLE_CUTOVER.md. The SDD turns tf-b1jm Finding F1 into an actionable production cutover spec for a RuntimeContextWorkflow-owned input table: schema families (`contexts`, `inputs`, optional producer receipts), durable `nextInputSequence` cursor, channel binding writes, workflow reads, idempotency/replay behavior, and the transactional deletion beads for input intents, dispatcher, appendRuntimeInputDeferred, deferreds-as-mailbox, and reconcile.

title: session_new returns running even when child agent process fails to start
description: Live Zed ACP trace .firegrid/acp-trace.jsonl showed session_new returning a running child handle while the child context immediately failed to start: firegrid.agent_event_pipeline.source.local_process.open_byte_pipe status code=2, command executable claude-acp, spawn claude-acp ENOENT. This makes parent agents believe delegation succeeded when no child process exists. Required fix: make session_new startup state honest. Either reject unknown/unspawnable agentKind before returning success, or return a non-terminal starting/failed status and expose durable lifecycle evidence so immediate spawn failures become failed instead of running. Acceptance: a missing child executable cannot produce session.status=running; live/targeted test proves session_new reports failure or a truthful pending state, and session_prompt to that child does not masquerade as delivered work. STOP/EXIT: if the fix requires changing RuntimeContext lifecycle authority, host control-plane ownership, agent-kind resolution, or session status semantics beyond a narrow startup-state patch, stop implementation and report exactly which boundary made the fix incomplete so architecture review can decide the fundamental shape.
close_reason: Completed: session_new startup state honesty fix merged in PR #643.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: DRAFT PR #643 (narrow fix + STOP-boundary report). ROOT CAUSE: spawnChildContext (agent-tool-host-live.ts:208) starts the child workflow fire-and-forget (discard:true) then returned status:'running' UNCONDITIONALLY; the spawn ENOENT happens async in the child workflow, after session_new already claimed running. NARROW PATCH: return honest 'created' (existing SessionStatus) not 'running' -> a missing executable can no longer produce status=running. TARGETED LIVE TEST (prompt-routing.test.ts, real engine): live session_new child now 'created'; nonexistent-executable child = 'created', never 'running'. Gates: host-sdk typecheck + prompt-routing tests(5) + lint/deps/dead green. STOP BOUNDARY (reported, not papered): the COMPLETE truthful fix — confirmed 'running' after spawn success / 'failed' on ENOENT, AND session_prompt non-masquerade to a failed child — requires awaiting/observing the child run-start lifecycle evidence = RuntimeContext lifecycle authority + session status semantics beyond a narrow startup patch. Architecture review decides the full shape (same lifecycle seam as Phase-0/output-cursor work). NOTE: bead's referenced investigation doc (2026-05-21-acp-parent-child-output-channel-gap.md) is absent from the tree; worked from bead trace evidence + source + tf-39b finding.

title: Move request-reflection barrier into callable channel bindings
description:
Validated by tf-lfxs GREEN. Implement the narrow production follow-up from SDD_FIREGRID_DURABLE_CHANNELS_SYNC_ASYNC.md: move request-row -> reflected-state barriers into callable channel bindings where the return value semantically means ready/reflected.

First candidate: HostSessionsCreateOrLoadChannel / firegrid.sessions.createOrLoad. Today it acks the request row, and callers must remember session.whenReady before prompt/start-like dependent actions. The goal is to make the wrong usage unrepresentable for this operation by having the callable binding return only once the RuntimeContext is reflected.

Scope/acceptance:
- Canonize the SDD as implementation-relevant architecture, but keep this bead scoped to concrete callable binding semantics.
- Audit existing createOrLoad callers for observable early-ack dependence before changing behavior. Document any caller that intentionally wants fire-and-forget create semantics.
- If compatible, update the production binding so createOrLoad waits for reflected context evidence before returning. Keep session.whenReady as a compatibility no-op/redundant barrier in this slice; deletion is follow-up after verification.
- Add/adjust focused tests proving createOrLoad -> prompt no longer needs an explicit whenReady barrier and does not race context-not-found.
- Preserve existing substrate: request rows/control-plane dispatcher remain the implementation. No new public verb, no sync/async API split, no broad Channel/Queue abstraction, no Effect Channel/Queue/Mailbox inheritance.
- Do not generalize to every callable binding in this bead. Record candidate follow-ups for other request-row-then-reflect operations discovered during the audit.

Non-goals:
- Delete whenReady in this bead.
- Migrate unrelated prompt egress-return work (tf-fyyk).
- Add provider-specific channels or external-effect abstractions.
- Introduce a new DurableDeferred/Queue public abstraction.
close_reason: DONE: PR #573 merged. createOrLoad reflected-barrier per ratified Fork 2 (dependent-op barrier §202) + the 1-line SDD worked-example fix.

title: Durable channels sync/async green-light spike

DELIVERABLE: a tiny-firegrid simulation (or extension) using the EXISTING client/channel surface to exercise BOTH modes in ONE trace:
  (1) durable handshake / call-style request-reflection barrier — ideally createOrLoad/whenReady or an equivalent bespoke-barrier collapse
  (2) durable mailbox / send+wait_for fanout / observe-later path
Plus: simulate:show tree + simulate:perf output + a docs/research finding with the verdict.

VERDICT CRITERIA:
  GREEN  = the framing removes/centralizes at least one REAL bespoke barrier WITHOUT adding public verbs or Effect Channel/Queue baggage
  YELLOW = useful as terminology only; no code abstraction earned yet
  RED    = requires a new layer that duplicates Stream/Sink/Effect/channel contracts OR introduces public API weight

CONSTRAINTS (hard):
  - do NOT introduce effect/Channel as a Firegrid API
  - do NOT add provider-specific channels
  - keep channel contracts in @firegrid/protocol, live bindings below (host-sdk/runtime)
  - NO production migration unless the sim exposes a trivial, obviously-safe deletion

TIMING: after the current channel-collapse merge queue settles (so the sim exercises a stable client/channel surface, post tf-aago/#560 + tf-05jj). Feeds back into SDD_FIREGRID_DURABLE_CHANNELS_SYNC_ASYNC.md.

title: Reconcile multi-context concurrent activation: lift Effect.forEach(concurrency:1) on RuntimeControlRequestReconciler + un-block claimAndRunRuntimeContextWorkflow to support stream-native VO concurrency per one-substrate SDD
description:
INV-5 (tf-tg8q PR #460) surfaced this as a load-bearing architectural gap that's invisible from source-reading + existing trace corpus alone (no prior sim attempted multi-context concurrent activation).

Root cause:
- FiregridRuntimeHostLive's RuntimeControlRequestReconciler processes startRequests via Effect.forEach(...) with default concurrency=1 (control-request-reconciler.ts:539-543)
- claimAndRunRuntimeContextWorkflow blocks until the workflow terminates (commands.ts:83-100)
- ACP agents don't terminate on prompt completion → reconciler stays inside claimAndRun for the emitter forever → second context's engine is never activated
- Evidence: 2 reconcile_once spans in 8-minute INV-5 run vs ~96 expected at 5-second poll

Three plausible closure paths (per Lane 5's FINDING):
(1) Raise concurrency on Effect.forEach (smallest step; preserves serial-within-key)
(2) Fork-and-forget claimAndRun (decouples reconciler-tick from workflow lifetime)
(3) Stream-based dispatcher (largest; aligns most directly with stream-native VO framing)

Preferred direction per one-substrate SDD's stream-native VO framing: 'different keys = concurrent VOs'. Smallest first step is raising concurrency on Effect.forEach.

Acceptance:
- Re-run INV-5's sim (or equivalent multi-context test)
- Both emitter + waiter contexts get reconcile_once spans at the expected ~5s interval (~96 over 8 minutes, not 2)
- Waiter context observes the emit_event row through its wait_for tool surface
- Choreography mechanism completes end-to-end

Structural framing: this is consistent with the SDDs' direction — stream-native VO requires concurrent activation across keys; today's serial Effect.forEach is the same coordination-theater pattern collapsed for waits, now at the context-activation layer. Same shape, different layer.

Cross-references:
- tf-tg8q (the INV-5 sim that surfaced this; PR #460)
- tf-fmwg (Slice C.2 event(name) — blocked by this fix in addition to Slice A/tf-lawq)
- SDD_FIREGRID_ONE_SUBSTRATE_WORKFLOW_ENGINE.md (prereq amendment needed)
- SDD_FIREGRID_AGENT_BODY_PLAN.md (prereq amendment needed via cross-link)
close_reason: DECIDED: shipped via PR #476. INV-5 multi-context activation gap empirically resolved via fork-and-forget claimAndRun + configurable concurrency + non-owner host claim-scan skip. Regression test (firegrid-workflow-driven-runtime.VALIDATION.9 ACID) proves second context starts while first long-running RuntimeContextWorkflow remains active. Phase-2 Slice C.2 (tf-fmwg event(name) multi-agent choreography) unblocked. Structurally superseded by tf-ho99 (Reconciler-as-workflows full collapse, post-Phase-1) — tf-ho99 will obsolete this interim fix, but tf-ma6c ships value NOW.

title: GAP(dark-factory choreography): agent-driven factory loop not expressible on current public surface
description:
DECISION-GRADE / PRECISE (cca2 evidence smoke landed — supersedes the preliminary over-broad framing).

CLASSIFICATION: (b) NARROW FIREGRID SURFACE-EXPOSURE GAP — NOT a protocol mismatch. Triage: cat-1/2, P1. Resolution = scoped PRODUCTION SURFACE FIX (sidecar/SDD), NOT an architect protocol-redesign binary.

EVIDENCE: PR #365 / commit f1002df45 / packages/tiny-firegrid dark-factory-pipeline.smoke.test.ts (passing).

PRECISE GAP SET (exactly three):
1. RESUME: ACP spec + SDK support session/load (replays full conversation via session/update before response); Firegrid AcpSessionLive / public session facade calls session/new ONLY, never session/load, and AgentSession.meta exposes no ACP wire sessionId to store/reload. → FIX: expose session/load + surface the ACP wire sessionId.
2. WAIT SCHEMA: wait_for schema accepts RuntimeRun/AgentOutput sources only → caller-owned DurableTable approval-fact waits not expressible. → FIX: extend wait_for schema to caller-owned facts.
3. EXECUTE: execute tool is exposed but the live host execute is unsupported. → FIX: wire host execute.

CORRECTION (narrowed): delegation is NOT a gap — session_new/session_prompt are exposed and sufficient. The earlier 'no spawn/spawn_all delegation gap' / 'unsupported execute/spawn_all/cancel/close genuinely-absent' framing is STRUCK. The only execute-related gap is item 3 (execute tool exposed, host side unwired); spawn_all/cancel/close are NOT gaps (delegation via session_new/session_prompt covers it).

STATUS: tf-mn2 remains BLOCKING tf-se0; tf-se0 correctly stays in_progress (NOT closed). Architect/roadmap binary is RETIRED — this is a scoped surface fix for Gurdas prioritization, not a redesign decision.
close_reason: CLOSED as misframed (Gurdas: 'embarrassing') — asserts unverified 'agent-driven factory loop not expressible' as a settled gap. Per handoff §0a that is DOWNGRADED: our own ACP codec double-advertisement is the prime suspect, not 'inexpressible'. The exact inference-as-conclusion anti-pattern (post-mortem §8). Real work = the codec investigation in the handoff, not this bead. (force: stale blocked-by tf-vao, which is itself a kept-but-parked Gurdas-gated SDD.)

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: REFINED to decision-grade/precise per cca2 evidence smoke (PR #365 / f1002df45 / dark-factory-pipeline.smoke.test.ts passing). Reclassified preliminary→(b) narrow Firegrid surface-exposure gap; precise 3-item gap set (session/load+sessionId, wait_for caller-owned-fact schema, host execute wiring); delegation gap STRUCK (session_new/session_prompt sufficient). Resolution = scoped surface fix (sidecar/SDD), not architect protocol-redesign binary.

title: TFIND-044: DurableTableProvider cannot carry N heterogeneous precise identities
description:
Area: client-sdk / effect-durable-operators provider.

DurableTableProvider's single ROut generic cannot carry N heterogeneous precise DurableTable <Self> identities (firegridRuntimeTableTags; flamecast client/main.tsx:360 TS2322) — a latent precision leak the TFIND-005 any was hiding. Resolution: §0 amendment to SDD_DURABLE_TABLE_REACT_LIVE_QUERY.md adding generic-identity type-shape decision; Option B = localized named coarse aggregate (branded AnyDurableTableTag) at the one provider seam, while every table stays precise everywhere else.

title: SDD amendment: channel operation completion contracts, no call-site sync enum
description: Amend SDD_FIREGRID_DURABLE_CHANNELS_SYNC_ASYNC.md to correct the design framing: sync/async should not be exposed as a user-facing boolean or await enum. Completion semantics belong to the channel/operation contract. Users choose semantic verbs/operations (send, call, wait_for, domain-specific operation); bindings own whether return means append receipt, reflected state, or semantic response. Also reinforce that whenReady is a leaky materialization primitive to remove, not preserve.

comments.author: gary
comments.text: EXPANDED SCOPE per Gurdas: this is not docs-only. Add a cheap tiny-firegrid validation of completion-contract modeling before amending SDD_FIREGRID_DURABLE_CHANNELS_SYNC_ASYNC.md. Evaluate API shapes for modeling operation completion semantics without exposing a user-facing sync boolean or await enum. Compare at least: (A) callsite parameterization such as sync/await mode — expected reject as leaky; (B) Effect Schema annotations/metadata on request/response schemas, using DurableTable.primaryKey AST annotation pattern as reference; (C) channel/route descriptor metadata owned by protocol/router; (D) return/receipt schema carrying the completion evidence. Output should recommend one contract shape and explain why. Keep all executable probing inside packages/tiny-firegrid or docs; no production API changes unless separately approved. Acceptance: SDD amendment says completion semantics belong to operation/channel contract, not caller option, and states where the contract is represented so router/edge transports can expose it reliably.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: SCOPE EXPANSION (Gurdas): go BEYOND docs — tiny-firegrid VALIDATION + SDD amendment for CHANNEL COMPLETION CONTRACTS. Evaluate API placement for completion semantics across these candidates: (1) call-site flags (e.g. expected-reject); (2) Schema annotations/metadata, using DurableTable.primaryKey as the reference pattern; (3) channel/route DESCRIPTOR metadata (on the router route descriptors); (4) return/RECEIPT schema. GOAL: NO public sync boolean / NO await enum — model completion RELIABLY as an operation/channel CONTRACT that the router + edge transports (ACP/Zed/MCP/CLI) can INSPECT to map completion → transport responses. Keep executable probing in tiny-firegrid/docs ONLY; NO production API changes in this bead (evaluation + SDD amendment, the impl is a follow-on).

title: ACP edge must expose Firegrid tools to backing agent runtime
description: Zed -> Firegrid ACP edge -> codex-acp happy path works, but Firegrid tools are not exposed inside the backing agent session. User prompt asking to use Firegrid sleep produced: 'no Firegrid tool is exposed in this session; tool_search only surfaced Gmail tools' and fell back to local shell sleep. Root suspect: firegrid acp path builds the backing runtime from CLI LaunchConfig without the runtimeContextMcp marker used by firegrid run/start, so the launched agent lacks the runtime-context MCP tool surface. Acceptance: Zed custom external agent can prompt the backing Codex session to see/use Firegrid tools such as sleep/wait_for/spawn or clearly listed Firegrid tool names; no raw available_commands_update/tool_call_update/status events render as assistant text; include a live Zed/ACP or equivalent in-memory ACP smoke proving a Firegrid tool is visible and one simple durable tool call works.

comments.author: gary
comments.text: Debugging note: use OTEL first, not guesswork. The failure should show in spans around runtime config / MCP declaration injection / ACP available_commands_update / tool_call_update / session output projection. Compare firegrid run/start path (with runtimeContextMcp marker) to firegrid acp path; check whether the launched backing runtime carries runtimeContextMcp, whether MCP tool exposure spans appear, and whether available_commands_update contains Firegrid tools before being projected to Zed. Add/adjust spans only if the current trace cannot answer that.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: METHOD (Gurdas): OTEL FIRST, no guessing/overbuild. Pin down by comparing firegrid run/start trace vs firegrid acp trace around: runtimeContextMcp marker injection, host MCP URL injection, ACP available_commands_update/tool_call_update, session output projection. Likely visible as missing runtimeContextMcp on the backing runtime OR available_commands_update lacking Firegrid tools. Trace the exact drop-point, patch narrowly there.

comments.text: HARD-HALT + precise finding (no code; the only fix is architect-gated substrate this lane is forbidden to touch — papering would be wrong). Source-verified @agentclientprotocol/claude-agent-acp@0.36.1 (latest) dist/ + @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@0.3.143 sdk.d.ts: (1) ONLY no-defer lever is per-MCP-server McpHttpServerConfig.alwaysLoad (sdk.d.ts:1063); NO global tool-search disable (no SDK opt/env/CLI flag). (2) claude-agent-acp strips alwaysLoad for ACP-advertised HTTP servers (acp-agent.js:1346-1356 maps {type,url,headers} only; ACP McpServer schema has no such field). (3) merge order acp-agent.js:1438 mcpServers:{...userProvidedOptions, ...acpDerived} — ACP-derived (no alwaysLoad) spread LAST overrides _meta.claudeCode.options by name. (4) no argv (only --cli/--hide-claude-auth) and no env gates deferral. (5) remaining paths (_meta.claudeCode.options non-colliding name / ACP McpServer no-defer field / _meta.disableBuiltInTools) ALL require the Firegrid ACP codec to change session/new = substrate, out of lane scope + architect-gated. Reshape into the broader workflow body refactor under a new SDD. See SDD_FIREGRID_WORKFLOW_BODY_DEFERRED_INPUT_REWRITE.md (skeleton) on PR #446. Local working tree changes can be reverted; the 3-file refactor is reusable as reference. Halt doc at docs/handoffs/HANDOFF_tf-qoyg_shape-a-narrow.md remains the empirical baseline.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text:
HALT — source-verified Shape A NARROW BLOCKER 2026-05-20 (not inferred; refuted by test). Lane 1 halt, not pushing the prototype until coordinator decides.

== What was attempted ==
Per dispatch: refactor packages/host-sdk/src/host/runtime-context-workflow-core.ts:188 (`waitForAgentOutput` for AgentOutputAfter) to inline `Stream.runHead(RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents.after(source))`, replacing the WaitFor.match → wait-router → DurableDeferred chain. Add `RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents` to `RuntimeContextWorkflowExecutionEnv`. Agent-tool path at tool-use-to-effect.ts:216 untouched.

== Sim verification (workflow-core-paths) — the prototype WORKS in-sim ==
Direct measurements on this branch:
  BASELINE (post-#445 main): wait_for.match AgentOutputAfter=127, wait_router.complete_match AgentOutputAfter=116, wait_for.match CallerFact=2, wait_router.complete_match CallerFact=2.
  AFTER FIX: wait_for.match AgentOutputAfter=0, wait_router.complete_match AgentOutputAfter=0, wait_for.match CallerFact=1, wait_router.complete_match CallerFact=2.
The AgentOutputAfter path no longer touches the generic machinery; the agent-tool path is preserved byte-for-byte. Sim outcome=DriverCompleted.

== Test-suite verification (host-sdk) — Shape A blocker hit ==
5 tests in packages/host-sdk/test/host/runtime-context-workflow-core.test.ts (all using `executeNativeRuntimeContext` — the production workflow body) time out at 5s/15s. After test-layer adaptations (added a host-wide-stream adapter for `RuntimeAgentOutputAfterEvents`; replaced `waitUntilActiveWait` wait-row poll with `waitUntilWorkflowStarted` run-row poll), tests STILL time out.

ROOT CAUSE (direct-source-verified at packages/runtime/src/workflow-engine/internal/engine-runtime.ts:211-216):
  ```
  yield* resume(options.executionId)
  const fiber = running.get(options.executionId)
  if (options.discard) {
    if (fiber) yield* Fiber.join(fiber)
    return undefined as never
  }
  ```
`executeNativeRuntimeContext({ discard: true })` does `Fiber.join` on the workflow body fiber. Under the OLD design (`WaitFor.match` → `DurableDeferred.await(matchDeferred)`), the workflow body SUSPENDS via `Workflow.suspend(instance)` (which @effect/workflow catches via `Workflow.intoResult`), so the fiber returns `Suspended` and `Fiber.join` completes. Under Shape A narrow (`Stream.runHead(events.after(source))`), there is NO `Workflow.suspend` call — `Stream.runHead` just blocks the FIBER on the durable stream waiting for a row. The fiber NEVER completes naturally → `Fiber.join` hangs forever.

== Convergence-doc reconciliation (source-verified) ==
Doc lines 84-89 say Shape A "should ride with the deferred-input rewrite that is already reshaping the same workflows, not be done speculatively first." The empirical reason — confirmed now — is that the workflow-engine suspend semantics need a coordinated rewrite when matching folds into the workflow body. WaitFor.match wraps the awaiter in `DurableDeferred.await` precisely so the workflow engine learns about the suspension; Stream.runHead does not. Until the workflow body adopts a suspension-aware in-body matcher (likely DurableDeferred.raceAll over an event-stream-fed deferred + DurableClock.sleep), Shape A cannot be done "narrow" without re-introducing the deferred plumbing that motivated Shape A in the first place.

The dispatch framing was inference-tier ("over-coordinated path should be cheaply inlined") and my source verification (workflow engine + test fixture behavior) refutes it cleanly.

== Three honest paths forward (need coordinator decision) ==

(A) STOP — confirm Shape A is gated on the deferred-input rewrite per the convergence doc; close tf-qoyg as `verified-blocked-on:deferred-input-rewrite`; the empirical artifacts (sim metrics + this test-failure shape) are the load-bearing evidence to inform that future work. tf-9ut already files a doc-gap (tf-sjr) on adjacent ground.

(B) WRAP — make a Shape-A-but-with-deferred-bridge variant: the workflow body calls `DurableDeferred.await(matchDeferredFor(...))`, and a small per-wait fiber forked from the workflow body subscribes to `events.after(source)` and fires `deferredDone` on first row. This preserves Workflow.suspend semantics. BUT — this re-introduces a per-wait fiber + a deferred lifecycle (just moved into the workflow body), defeating the convergence doc’s "Shape A" cleanliness goal. It’s closer to "Shape A-light", not the doc’s Shape A.

(C) WIDEN — accept the deferred-input rewrite as a prerequisite and reshape this lane into the broader workflow body refactor. Out of "narrow" scope; the dispatch explicitly forbade this.

== What ships if pushed (nothing yet) ==
Branch `sidecar/tf-qoyg-s6-shape-a-narrow-agentoutputafter` has the prototype + test adaptations + adapter layer but is LOCAL ONLY. No PR opened. Worktree clean of side-effects.

Per the meta-rule: source-verified before architectural moves; the verification refutes the move as designed. Awaiting coordinator decision on (A)/(B)/(C).

title: Substrate: surface insertOrGet/append offset as per-context arrival order (P3-B, unblocks tf-vrz6)
description: From tf-vrz6 STOP (docs/investigations/2026-05-22-tf-vrz6-p3-rows-ordering-boundary.md). P3 consume-time ordering needs arrival order, but DurableTable.rows() is PRIMARY-KEY-sorted, not arrival-ordered (empirically proven), and P3 keys inputs by inputId (non-monotonic) — so P3-A (tail inputs.rows() arrival-ordered projection) is infeasible. The append offset exists internally (appendInsertWithPrimaryKeyFence -> DurableStream.appendWithProducer Appended result, DurableTable.ts:772-782) but insertOrGet discards it (returns {_tag:Inserted} with no offset, :787-789). Additively surface it on InsertOrGetResult as a per-stream/per-context append position/arrival sequence, plumbed from appendWithProducer; prove distinct concurrent inserts get monotonic offsets. This is the only compliant arrival-order source (write-time nextInputSequenceToAssign is forbidden). Substrate-only (effect-durable-operators). BLOCKS tf-vrz6 Stage A.
close_reason: Merged PR #658

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text:
DONE — draft PR #658 (https://github.com/gurdasnijor/firegrid/pull/658), branch codex/tf-qtfb-durable-table-arrival-offset. NOT a STOP — clean additive surface, no transaction/CAS/lifecycle rewrite.

WHAT: surface durable-stream append offset on InsertOrGetResult.Inserted (DurableStream.Offset). Plumbed from appendInsertWithPrimaryKeyFence -> appendWithProducer Appended{offset}, previously discarded.

MONOTONICITY PROVEN: 12 distinct concurrent insertOrGet on one stream -> all Inserted, distinct offsets, sorted strictly increasing. Server offsets are segment_logical zero-padded to 16 digits each, so lexicographic string compare == append (arrival) order; reference server serializes appends. ACID 26-11 + 2 tests against real in-process @durable-streams/server.

KEY FINDING: Found (204 duplicate) returns NO position from the reference server (empty offset). So made the type ASYMMETRIC — offset only on Inserted; Found unchanged ({_tag,row}). Callers needing original arrival order capture the Inserted offset at first write.

BLAST RADIUS: additive — only Inserted gains a field; Found byte-identical. All consumers read _tag/row via Match.tag (engine-runtime, adapter, session-permission, host/commands, recorder, supervisor-commands) — unaffected; verified by turbo typecheck across runtime+host-sdk. Existing exact-shape toEqual relaxed to toMatchObject.

GATES: turbo typecheck (operators+runtime+host-sdk), test 29 passed, check:specs, lint+dead+dup+deps — all green.

UNBLOCKS tf-vrz6 Stage A (P3-B arrival source). Consumer wiring (per-context stream scoping + durable input cursor) stays tf-vrz6/tf-qk6h.

title: ACP/Zed OTel trace export: live end-to-end proof (--otel-file must produce .firegrid/acp-trace.jsonl under Zed)
description: Follow-on to tf-ukvr (#601). --otel-file exists and packages/observability/src/node.ts passes a basic Effect-span smoke, but the LIVE Zed path did NOT produce .firegrid/acp-trace.jsonl during manual testing. Deliver ONE live end-to-end proof. Verify: (1) the Zed command uses an absolute or repo-correct trace path (relative path likely resolves against Zed's cwd, not the repo — confirm/fix); (2) stdout remains ACP JSON-RPC frames ONLY (exporter never writes stdout); (3) the process actually starts FiregridOtelLive (the exporter layer is composed on the live acp path, not just the smoke); (4) spans FLUSH for a long-running ACP process — not only on process exit (a long-lived Zed agent may never hit the exit flush; need periodic/batch flush). Acceptance: a real Zed/ACP session produces .firegrid/acp-trace.jsonl with the ACP + Firegrid-MCP spans, captured as evidence (FINDING or PR). No bespoke harness — real live run.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: ROOT CAUSE LOCALIZED (source-verified via live firegrid acp CLI run). PRIMARY: relative --otel-file resolves against the firegrid PROCESS cwd (= Zed's launch cwd), NOT the repo. JsonlFileSpanExporter ctor does path.resolve(filePath) against process.cwd(); Zed launches the agent from its own cwd, so '.firegrid/acp-trace.jsonl' landed at <zed-cwd>/.firegrid/... and never in the repo. Live driver proof: repo=-1 (file never appeared in repo) in every sample across graceful+sigkill runs; file always materialized under the foreign launch cwd with 1471 spans incl acp_stdio_edge.{initialize,new_session,prompt}+mcp.register_toolkit+McpServer.{initialize,tools/list,tools/call}. SECONDARY: file uses BatchSpanProcessor(5s/512) + forceFlush() no-op; periodic flush works for long-running (512->1024 at 5s) so 'only on exit' refuted, but short sessions/abrupt SIGKILL lose recent spans. Points 2(stdout purity:0 non-JSON)+3(layer composed) VERIFIED HEALTHY. FIX: normalize otel path to absolute + emit resolved abs path to stderr at acp startup; switch file exporter to SimpleSpanProcessor (lossless, matches console path); docs: absolute path in Zed config.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: DRAFT PR #605 (push complete; MERGE gated on review). LIVE PROOF delivered. ROOT CAUSE: relative --otel-file resolved against firegrid PROCESS cwd = Zed's launch cwd, not the repo (path.resolve vs process.cwd in JsonlFileSpanExporter); #601 smoke only passed because it used an absolute path + an exiting agent. FIX: (1) cli resolves relative --otel-file against --cwd when supplied, pins absolute, announces abs path on stderr (stdout stays pure); (2) file exporter SimpleSpanProcessor (immediate per-span write, was BatchSpanProcessor 5s/512 + no-op forceFlush); (3) gitignore .firegrid/; docs+help updated. LIVE ARTIFACT (real firegrid acp CLI from a NON-repo cwd, real ACP client+backing agent w/ real MCP round-trip): before=repo file absent every sample (graceful+sigkill); after=lands in <repo>/.firegrid/acp-trace.jsonl, 324 spans at t=0 (no 5s lag), 0 non-JSON stdout, 919 spans incl acp_stdio_edge.{initialize,new_session,prompt}+mcp.register_toolkit+McpServer.{initialize,tools/list,tools/call}, MCP_OBS listCount=11 callOk=true. Points 2(stdout purity)+3(layer composed) verified HEALTHY. Gates green: observability(2)+host-sdk(133) tests, typecheck, lint+dead+dup+deps, effect-diag baseline. FINDING: docs/research/tf-r1gz-acp-zed-live-trace.FINDING.md.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: Folded the cheap/low-risk parts of the Effect review into PR #605 (commit 396d6c22f): (1) moved relative->absolute trace-path resolution into resolveFiregridOtelFileDestination via a pure baseDir param + unit test (drops inline path.resolve from run.ts); (2) FIREGRID_OTEL_FILE_FLUSH knob, immediate default, batched opt-in. Rejected with verified reasons: logInfo announce (default logger writes Info->stdout, logger.ts:364, would corrupt ACP frames), separate acquireRelease on the processor (NodeSdk already forceFlush+shutdown, NodeSdk.ts:53-66), full Context.Tag/Path service in an ambiently-impure CLI entrypoint. Re-verified live both modes. Deferred validate-writable/tagged-error to follow-up tf-3718.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: Addressed review P2 (announce could lie under OTLP precedence) in commit 8ba032f79 on PR #605. FiregridOtelLive gives OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT precedence over the file destination, so 'writing OTEL spans to <file>' was misleading when OTLP is configured. Fix: new pure resolveFiregridOtelActiveExporter({destination,env}) encodes the same precedence (single source of truth + shared env-name const); CLI now announces the exporter that ACTUALLY runs (OTLP endpoint + explicit '--otel-file ... is ignored' when OTLP wins; file path only for a real file exporter). Added the OTLP-precedence unit test the review flagged as missing. Verified live at startup: file-only->file path, file+OTLP->OTLP+ignored-note. Gates green (observability test x4, typecheck, lint+dead+dup+deps, effect-diag baseline). Chose announce-honesty over flipping precedence (--otel-file overriding OTLP) to avoid a broader behavioral change to the shared observability layer for non-acp consumers.

Confirms source-as-offset principle holds empirically for WAVE 1 SDD acceptance #6.

title: Transactional terminology cutover: afferent/efferent -> ingress/egress
description:
Rename the body-plan channel terminology from biological terms to widely accepted engineering terms.

CUTOVER TERMS:
- afferent -> ingress
- efferent -> egress
- AfferentChannel -> IngressChannel
- EfferentChannel -> EgressChannel
- ChannelDirection literals: "afferent" / "efferent" -> "ingress" / "egress"

Scope includes docs, SDDs, feature specs, code, tests, examples, comments, public strings, and any generated snapshots that carry the vocabulary. Primary references already found:
- docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_AGENT_BODY_PLAN.md
- docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_AGGRESSIVE_ONE_SUBSTRATE_SWAPOVER.md
- packages/host-sdk / runtime channel code if present

Important constraint from Gurdas: no compatibility layer, no aliases, no dual vocabulary. This is a transactional cutover. Do not preserve afferent/efferent as deprecated public terms.

Acceptance:
- `rg -n "\b[Aa]fferent\b|\b[Ee]fferent\b|afferrent|efferrent|afferent|efferent" docs packages features apps --glob "!repos/**"` returns zero, except intentional historical quotations only if explicitly justified in a finding/comment. Prefer zero.
- `ChannelDirection` and any schema literals use `ingress` / `egress` only.
- SDD_FIREGRID_AGENT_BODY_PLAN.md and SDD_FIREGRID_AGGRESSIVE_ONE_SUBSTRATE_SWAPOVER.md use ingress/egress consistently.
- Relevant focused typecheck/tests pass; docs/spec checks pass.
- PR body states this is a no-compat transactional terminology cutover.
close_reason: DECIDED: shipped via PR #493 (merge 934f8d66b). Transactional terminology cutover LANDED. afferent→ingress, efferent→egress across docs/packages/features. NO COMPATIBILITY shims. Acceptance grep returns zero. 20 files changed, +187/-187 (clean rename).

comments.text:
Initial source grep before dispatch:

`rg -n "\b[Aa]fferent\b|\b[Ee]fferent\b|afferrent|efferrent|afferent|efferent" docs packages features apps --glob "!repos/**"` currently finds body-plan terminology primarily in:

title: Implement protocol-owned route completion metadata + router/ACP-edge receipt-schema use (production follow-on to tf-nioy SDD)
description: Production implementation of the channel completion contract designed in tf-nioy (#595). Add protocol-owned route completion metadata to the channel/route descriptors and make the runtime router + ACP stdio edge actually produce/consume the terminal return/receipt schema. This is the BLOCKING REMAINDER bead required by the transactional-cutover-rule before tf-nioy (docs/SDD amendment) can close — the SDD names this follow-on but no bead existed. No call-site sync flag / isComplete boolean / await enum; completion stays modeled as route descriptor metadata + receipt schema.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Backlog triage: left open/ready. This remains the production implementation follow-on to tf-nioy/#595: protocol-owned route completion metadata + router/ACP edge receipt-schema use. It is not superseded by the Phase 0A table-seam SDD.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: Paused by gary after architecture review: route completion metadata implementation may double down on current ACP/router plumbing rather than advance Phase 0A/0B simplification. Lane 2 redirected to produce a finding on whether this contract survives the target architecture; no production PR should land from this pass without re-dispatch.

comments.author: codex
comments.text:
FINDING (STOP/REDIRECT): I stopped the tf-r6br production implementation and left the worktree dirty as evidence; no PR/push was opened.

What I learned: descriptor-owned receipt schemas fit the current channel router mechanically: egress/call routes can carry a schema and runtime dispatch can decode the returned append/call receipt before returning to host edges. The ACP terminal completion piece does not fit cleanly today. `session.prompt` dispatch returns the input-intent append row, while ACP completion is produced later by output-stream observation of TurnComplete. To make the requested metadata drive ACP now, the edge would have to synthesize a Done receipt after TurnComplete and decode it through route metadata. That proves the shape is possible, but it also creates edge-local completion projection over the existing router/output plumbing rather than simplifying the architecture.

Verdict against Phase 0A/0B: immediate channel receipt schemas probably survive as protocol/router I/O validation. Terminal route-completion metadata for ACP prompt should be deferred or replaced by workflow-owned output/result state plus channel/output observation. In the target architecture, terminal prompt completion should come from the durable workflow-owned output/result log and DurableOutputCursor/O(outputs) observation, not from router metadata layered onto `session.prompt` dispatch.

Minimal part still needed after DurableOutputCursor/O(outputs): keep a narrow protocol-owned terminal output/result receipt schema (or durable output row schema) and an ACP projection from that durable terminal row to PromptResponse/stopReason. A generic router decode of immediate append/call receipts may still be useful, but the terminal Done/Rejected completion contract should bind to workflow-owned output/result state, not the current route descriptor.

Blast radius of the stopped implementation diff: features/firegrid/firegrid-durable-channels-sync-async-spike.feature.yaml; packages/protocol/src/channels/core.ts; packages/protocol/src/channels/router.ts; packages/protocol/src/channels/host-control.ts; packages/protocol/src/launch/host-control-request.ts; packages/runtime/src/channels/router.ts; packages/runtime/src/channels/host-control-routes.ts; packages/runtime/test/channels/host-control-router.test.ts; packages/host-sdk/src/host/acp-stdio-edge.ts; packages/host-sdk/test/host/acp-stdio-edge.test.ts. No tests were run after the redirect; only `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` completed.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: COORDINATOR DISPOSITION (per STOP/REDIRECT finding): blocked on tf-7kq8 — the production agent-output observer fix establishes the workflow-owned output/result + DurableOutputCursor (O(distinct outputs)) behavior that terminal route-completion metadata must build on. Validates the earlier hold (router/output-plumbing churn). Re-scope when unblocked: only narrow immediate descriptor-owned RECEIPT decoding (append/call) is viable now; ACP TERMINAL route-completion metadata is DEFERRED — must not synthesize Done after TurnComplete on the current plumbing; land a terminal output/result schema AFTER the output cursor. Dirty worktree (acp-stdio-edge.ts, protocol/channels/core.ts, host-control.ts, ...) left intact as evidence, NO PR. The immediate-receipt vs terminal split + whether a dedicated production-cursor bead is needed = Gurdas/gary call when tf-7kq8 lands.

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: PR #662 (draft). Scope (confirmed w/ Gurdas): clean contract + immediate receipts; STOP/report terminal. LANDED: protocol ChannelRouteCompletion descriptor metadata (acknowledgement|terminal{receiptSchema}, route-owned, no call-site flag) + transport-neutral RouteCompletionReceipt (Done|Rejected) + optional completion threaded through 4 registrations/factories -> ChannelRouteMetadata/Descriptor (default acknowledgement); ACP edge decodes host.sessions.createOrLoad immediate receipt via protocol response schema (was an  cast). STOP/REPORTED (boundary-gated, NOT implemented): ACP terminal prompt-completion projection -- prompt completion still comes from TurnComplete observation riding the tf-7kq8 bridge_debt scan; binding it now = edge synthesis of Done over plumbing slated for replacement (the prior STOP/REDIRECT's exact rejection). Must bind to workflow-owned durable output/result row after tf-aseo (DurableOutputCursor, IN_PROGRESS/not merged) lands. Finding: docs/research/tf-r6br-route-completion-impl.FINDING.md. Verified: typecheck+tests+knip(0)+eslint+semgrep(11=11) green. tf-r6br stays OPEN (terminal remainder gated on tf-aseo).

comments.author: gnijor
comments.text: CI Typecheck red repaired (pushed 649d41584 to PR #662). Root cause: tf-r6br made completion a required field on ChannelRouteMetadata; the pre-existing tiny-firegrid channel-completion probe overrode completion with its incompatible local ChannelCompletionContract -> TS2430 (out of local typecheck scope, so missed locally). Fix: probe now carries its rich contract under completionContract and supplies completion via terminalCompletion(CompletionProbeReceiptSchema). Simulation-only; no protocol/production change; architecture unchanged. Recursive typecheck 10/10 + probe.test.ts 4/4 green locally.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: RE-SCOPED 2026-05-22: terminal route completion is blocked on tf-w6qj. The dense raw-output DurableOutputCursor from tf-aseo/#664 is a bridge/hotfix, not target architecture. Terminal completion must bind to the sparse workflow-owned output transition/result log, not ACP edge-local Done synthesis over TurnComplete or raw-output cursoring. Immediate receipt metadata already landed in PR #662.

title: dark-factory sim rebuild: Linear webhook ingest + factory-vision choreography over channel surface
description:
Per Gurdas's directive 2026-05-20 + factory-vision.md.

REBUILD packages/tiny-firegrid/src/simulations/dark-factory/ to demonstrate the factory-vision narrative over the channel surface that just landed in Phase 2.

CURRENT STATE: dark-factory has driver.ts + host.ts + index.ts but uses the OLD wait substrate (HostOwnedDurableToolsWaitForLive) and was built before channels existed. It's a placeholder.

TARGET STATE: a working sim that runs end-to-end through Firegrid's public CHANNEL surface (no reach-pasts) and demonstrates §7's 7 capabilities via §6's choreography pattern.

KEY ELEMENTS:

(1) LINEAR WEBHOOK INGEST CHANNEL:
- Register IngressChannel<LinearWebhook> via ChannelRegistry per the canonical pattern (d00e12cf7)
- The channel's substrate is a DurableTable.rows() projection (collection of LinearWebhook rows)
- Sim's host seeds the table with simulated webhook deliveries (since this is a sim, not real Linear)
- Planner (driver) calls wait_for('linear.webhook', { match: { action: 'issue.created' }, timeoutMs }) — exactly the agent-facing shape in the SDD

(2) FACTORY CHOREOGRAPHY (per factory-vision §6):
- Planner reads incoming Linear ticket via wait_for(linear.webhook)
- Planner decides next move (e.g., 'this needs clarification' OR 'plan immediately')
- Planner produces a plan, then call(approval, { plan }) — uses approval channel for human gate
- On approval: planner delegates implementation via session_new + session_prompt OR via send(event('implementer.start'), ...)
- Implementer comes back with PR → planner spawns reviewers (multi-agent choreography via event(name))
- Reviewers respond → planner moves to merge or feedback loop
- On merge: planner watches CI via wait_for + merges

(3) CAPABILITIES TO DEMONSTRATE (per factory-vision §7):
- (1) External events as durable verified facts → ingress channel from sim-seeded LinearWebhook table
- (2) Participant identity durable across time → session-handle correlation
- (3) One external intent → one participant → sessions.createOrLoad keyed by ticket external key
- (4) Participants delegate to other participants → planner spawns implementer/reviewers
- (5) Participants wait for things → wait_for(channel) + wait_for_any
- (6) Participants take actions and remember → session.log channel (Slice C.6)
- (7) Everyone sees what's happening → trace/observability spans

CHANNELS TO REGISTER (per SDD/Slice C.* on main):
- IngressChannel<LinearWebhook> ('linear.webhook') — the trigger
- CallableChannel<ApprovalRequest, ApprovalResponse> ('plan.approval') — human gate via tf-v8i4
- event(name) channels ('implementer.started', 'reviewer.verdict', 'ci.status') — agent-to-agent + machine events via tf-fmwg
- session.self.lifecycle / session.self.checkpoint — interoception (tf-gnp1)
- session.log — efferent annotation (tf-xl6k)
- dm/notification — human channels (tf-mybr)

ACCEPTANCE:
- Sim runs end-to-end against a real claude-agent-acp planner
- Trace shows: linear.webhook ingress fires → planner decides → approval call → delegation → events flow → terminal state reached
- NO reach-pasts: every primitive the planner uses goes through public channel surface (no DurableTable references in driver, no engine references, no wait-router references)
- §7 capabilities exercised + observable in trace
- pnpm run verify green

KNOWN CONSTRAINTS:
- Use CURRENT channel surface on main (ChannelRegistry-shape). The Phase-3 refactor (tf-kddg) will move this to Tag+Layer per channel — accept the registry-shape as scaffold for now.
- tf-xw0w may still be in flight; if so, build against current main (substrate cutover doesn't change agent-facing surface).
- Channels above, workflows/substrate below — don't expose DurableTable, ProjectionStream, engine, or stream URLs in driver code.

EXPLICIT NON-SCOPE:
- Real Linear integration (this is a sim; seed the LinearWebhook table directly)
- Real GitHub PR / CI (sim-seed events)
- Real Slack (sim notifications channel)
- Refactoring ChannelRegistry shape (that's tf-kddg)

ESTIMATED: substantial, ~half-day to day.

REFERENCE READ:
- docs/vision/factory-vision.md (the narrative)
- docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_DARK_FACTORY_APP.md (the contract)
- docs/sdds/SDD_FIREGRID_AGENT_BODY_PLAN.md (channel definitions + IngressChannel<LinearWebhook> example)
- packages/tiny-firegrid/src/simulations/dark-factory/ (current scaffold to rebuild)
- packages/tiny-firegrid/src/simulations/inv5-cross-agent-event-choreography/ (multi-agent pattern from INV-5)
- packages/host-sdk/src/host/channel-registry.ts (current channel surface)
- packages/host-sdk/src/host/channels/* (the Phase-2 channel bindings)

This is the production proof of factory-vision per §5: 'when a factory runs end-to-end through Firegrid's public surface without reaching into private internals, the substrate has earned its claim.'
